en: Canada's premiers (the leaders of provincial governments), if they have any breath left after complaining about Ottawa at their late July annual meeting, might spare a moment to do something, together, to reduce health-care costs. (32 words)
cn: 加拿大各省的省长们如果在7月下旬的年会上抱怨完渥太华之后还有气力的话，不妨抽出一点时间一起做点什么，来降低医疗保健费用。

en: They're all groaning about soaring health budgets, the fastest-growing component of which are pharmaceutical costs. (16 words)
cn: 他们都在为飞涨的医疗预算而叫苦不迭，其中增长最快的部分是药品费用。

en: According to the Canadian Institute for Health Information, prescription drug costs have risen since 1997 at twice the rate of overall health-care spending. (23 words)
cn: 根据加拿大健康信息研究所的数据，自1997年以来，处方药费用的增长速度是整体医疗保健支出增速的两倍。

en: Part of the increase comes from drugs being used to replace other kinds of treatments. (14 words)
cn: 增长部分源于药物被用来替代其他种类的治疗。

en: Part of it arises from new drugs costing more than older kinds. (11 words)
cn: 部分源于新药成本高于老药。

en: Part of it is higher prices. (6 words)
cn: 还有部分源于价格的上涨。

en: What to do? (3 words)
cn: 该怎么办？

en: Both the Romanow commission and the Kirby committee on health care—to say nothing of reports from other experts—recommended the creation of a national drug agency. (25 words)
cn: 罗曼诺委员会和柯比医疗保健委员会——更不用说其他专家的报告了——都建议成立一个国家药品机构。

en: Instead of each province having its own list of approved drugs, bureaucracy, procedures and limited bargaining power, all would pool resources, work with Ottawa, and create a national institution. (29 words)
cn: 与其让每个省都拥有自己的获批药品清单、官僚机构、程序和有限的议价能力，不如集中所有资源，与渥太华合作，创建一个全国性机构。

en: What does "national" mean? (4 words)
cn: “国家的”是什么意思？

en: Roy Romanow and Senator Michael Kirby recommended a federal-provincial body much like the recently created National Health Council. (18 words)
cn: 罗伊·罗曼诺和参议员迈克尔·柯比建议建立一个联邦与省级联合的机构，很像最近成立的国家健康委员会。

en: But "national" doesn't have to mean that. (6 words)
cn: 但“国家的”不一定意味着那样。

en: "National" could mean interprovincial—provinces combining efforts to create one body. (10 words)
cn: “国家的”可以意味着跨省——各省联合起来共同创建一个机构。

en: Either way, one benefit of a "national" organization would be to negotiate better prices, if possible, with drug manufacturers. (19 words)
cn: 无论哪种方式，“国家”组织的一个好处就是可以的话，能跟药品制造商谈到更低的价格。

en: Instead of having one province—or a series of hospitals within a province—negotiate a price for a given drug on the provincial list, the national agency would negotiate on behalf of all provinces. (32 words)
cn: 与其让一个省——或一个省内的一系列医院——就省清单上的某种药品去谈价格，国家机构将代表所有省份去谈判。

en: Rather than, say, Quebec, negotiating on behalf of seven million people, the national agency would negotiate on behalf of 31 million people. (22 words)
cn: 比如说，与其让魁北克代表700万人去谈，国家机构将代表3100万人去谈判。

en: Basic economics suggests the greater the potential consumers, the higher the likelihood of a better price. (16 words)
cn: 基本经济学原理表明，潜在消费者越多，拿到更优惠价格的可能性就越大。

en: Of course, the pharmaceutical companies will scream. (7 words)
cn: 当然，制药公司会大声抗议。

en: They like divided buyers; they can lobby better that way. (10 words)
cn: 他们喜欢分散的买家，那样他们可以更好地进行游说。

en: They can use the threat of removing jobs from one province to another. (14 words)
cn: 他们可以威胁将工作岗位从一个省转移到另一个省。

en: They can hope that, if one province includes a drug on its list, the pressure will cause others to include it on theirs. (22 words)
cn: 他们可以指望，如果一个省将某种药品列入其清单，压力会导致其他省也将其列入清单。

en: They wouldn't like a national agency, but self-interest would lead them to deal with it. (16 words)
cn: 他们不会喜欢一个全国性机构，但自身利益会促使他们与之打交道。

en: A small step has been taken in the direction of a national agency with the creation of the Canadian Coordinating Office for Health Technology Assessment, funded by Ottawa and the provinces. (28 words)
cn: 在渥太华和各省的资助下，加拿大卫生技术评估协调办公室的成立，标志着朝国家机构的方向迈出了一小步。

en: Under it, a Common Drug Review recommends to provincial lists which new drugs should be included. (15 words)
cn: 在其之下，一个通用药品审查机构向各省清单推荐应纳入哪些新药。

en: Predictably, and regrettably, Quebec refused to join. (7 words)
cn: 可以预见且令人遗憾的是，魁北克拒绝加入。

en: A few premiers are suspicious of any federal-provincial deal-making. (10 words)
cn: 一些省长对任何联邦与省级的交易都持怀疑态度。

en: They (particularly Quebec and Alberta) just want Ottawa to fork over additional billions with few, if any, strings attached. (20 words)
cn: 他们（尤其是魁北克和阿尔伯塔）只想要渥太华几乎不带任何附加条件地额外拨出数十亿。

en: That's one reason why the idea of a national list hasn't gone anywhere, while drug costs keep rising fast. (19 words)
cn: 这就是为什么国家清单的想法毫无进展的原因之一，而药品成本却在持续快速上涨。

en: Quebec's resistance to a national agency is provincialist ideology. (9 words)
cn: 魁北克对国家机构的抵制是地方保护主义意识形态。

en: One of the first advocates for a national list was a researcher at Laval University. (15 words)
cn: 最早倡导国家清单的人之一是拉瓦尔大学的一位研究人员。

en: Quebec's Drug Insurance Fund has seen its costs skyrocket with annual increases from 14.3 per cent to 26.8 per cent! (19 words)
cn: 魁北克药品保险基金目睹了其成本的急剧飙升，年增长率从14.3%到26.8%！

en: Premiers love to quote Mr. Romanow's report selectively, especially the parts about more federal money. (15 words)
cn: 省长们喜欢有选择地引用罗曼诺先生的报告，尤其是关于更多联邦资金的部分。

en: Perhaps they should read what he had to say about drugs: "A national drug agency would provide governments more influence on pharmaceutical companies in order to constrain the ever-increasing cost of drugs." (30 words)
cn: 或许他们应该读读他对药品的看法：“一个国家药品机构将使政府对制药公司拥有更大的影响力，以控制不断增长的药品成本。”

en: Or they could read Mr. Kirby's report: "the substantial buying power of such an agency would strengthen the public prescription-drug insurance plans to negotiate the lowest possible purchase prices from drug companies." (29 words)
cn: 或者他们可以读读柯比先生的报告：“这样一个机构的强大购买力，将加强公共处方药保险计划向制药公司争取最低采购价格的能力。”

en: So when the premiers gather in Niagara Falls to assemble their usual complaint list, they should also get cracking about something in their jurisdiction that would help their budgets and patients. (29 words)
cn: 因此，当省长们聚集在尼亚加拉大瀑布，罗列他们惯常的抱怨清单时，他们也该着手在自己管辖范围内做点有益于预算和患者的事了。

en: On the north bank of the Ohio river sits Evansville, Ind., home of David Williams, 52, and of a riverboat casino (a place where gambling games are played). (27 words)
cn: 俄亥俄河北岸坐落着印第安纳州的埃文斯维尔，这里是52岁的大卫·威廉姆斯的家，也是一家河船赌场的所在地。

en: During several years of gambling in that casino, Williams, a state auditor earning $35,000 a year, lost approximately $175,000. (20 words)
cn: 在那家赌场赌博的几年里，威廉姆斯，一位年薪35000美元的州审计员，输掉了大约175000美元。

en: He had never gambled before the casino sent him a coupon for $20 worth of gambling. (16 words)
cn: 在赌场寄给他一张价值20美元赌博券之前，他从未赌过。

en: He visited the casino, lost the $20 and left. (9 words)
cn: 他去了赌场，输掉那20美元就走了。

en: On his second visit he lost $800. (7 words)
cn: 第二次去时他输了800美元。

en: The casino issued to him, as a good customer, a "Fun Card", which when used in the casino earns points for meals and drinks, and enables the casino to track the user's gambling activities. (32 words)
cn: 赌场给他这个好顾客发了一张“娱乐卡”，在赌场使用时可以积攒餐饮积分，同时也使赌场得以跟踪用户的赌博活动。

en: For Williams, these activities become what he calls "electronic heroin." (10 words)
cn: 对威廉姆斯来说，这些活动成了他所谓的“电子海洛因”。

en: By the time he had lost $5,000 he said to himself that if he could get back to even, he would quit. (21 words)
cn: 等他输到5000美元的时候，他对自己说，如果能扳回本钱就收手。

en: One night he won $5,500, but he did not quit. (9 words)
cn: 一天晚上他赢了5500美元，但却没有收手。

en: In 1997 he lost $21,000 to one slot machine in two days. (12 words)
cn: 1997年，他在一台老虎机上两天内输了21000美元。

en: In March 1997 he lost $72,186. (6 words)
He sometimes played two slot machines at a time, all night, until the boat docked at 5 a.m., then went back aboard when the casino opened at 9 a.m. (28 words)
cn: 他有时同时玩两台老虎机，通宵达旦，直到清晨5点船靠岸，然后上午9点赌场一开门又回到船上。

en: Now he is suing the casino, charging that it should have refused his patronage because it knew he was addicted. (19 words)
cn: 现在他正在起诉赌场，指控赌场本应拒绝他的光顾，因为赌场知道他已经上瘾。

en: It did know he had a problem. (7 words)
cn: 赌场确实知道他有问题。

en: In March 1998 a friend of Williams's got him involuntarily confined to a treatment center for addictions, and wrote to inform the casino of Williams's gambling problem. (26 words)
cn: 1998年3月，威廉姆斯的一个朋友强制将他送入一家戒瘾治疗中心，并致信告知赌场威廉姆斯的赌博问题。

en: The casino included a photo of Williams among those of banned gamblers, and wrote to him a "cease admissions" letter. (18 words)
cn: 赌场将威廉姆斯的照片列入禁赌人员名单，并给他写了一封“停止入场”的信。

en: Noting the "medical/psychological" nature of problem gambling behavior, the letter said that before being readmitted to the casino he would have to present medical/psychological information demonstrating that patronizing the casino would pose no threat to his safety or well-being. (36 words)
cn: 信中指出问题赌博行为的“医学/心理”性质，并称在重新获准进入赌场之前，他必须出示医学/心理方面的证明，表明光顾赌场不会对他的安全或健康构成威胁。

en: Although no such evidence was presented, the casino's marketing department continued to pepper him with mailings. (16 words)
cn: 尽管没有出示这样的证明，赌场的营销部门仍不断给他发送大量邮件。

en: And he entered the casino and used his Fun Card without being detected. (13 words)
cn: 而他进入赌场并使用娱乐卡时，也没有被察觉。

en: The Wall Street Journal reports that the casino has 24 signs warning: "Enjoy the fun... and always bet with your head, not over it." (23 words)
cn: 据《华尔街日报》报道，该赌场有24个警示标志：“享受乐趣……始终用头脑下注，不要上头。”

en: Every entrance ticket lists a toll-free number for counseling from the Indiana Department of Mental Health. (16 words)
cn: 每张入场券上都印有印第安纳州心理健康部门的免费咨询电话。

en: Nevertheless, Williams's suit charges that the casino, knowing he was "helplessly addicted to gambling," intentionally worked to "lure" him to "engage in conduct against his will." (25 words)
cn: 尽管如此，威廉姆斯的诉讼指控赌场明知他“无可救药地沉迷赌博”，却还故意“引诱”他“做出违背自己意愿的行为”。

en: Well. (1 words)
cn: 嗯。

en: It is unclear what luring was required, given his compulsive behavior. (11 words)
cn: 鉴于他的强迫性行为，不清楚还需要什么引诱。

en: And in what sense was his will operative? (8 words)
cn: 而他的意志在什么意义上是起作用的呢？

en: The fourth edition of the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders says "pathological gambling" involves persistent, recurring and uncontrollable pursuit less of money than of the thrill of taking risks in quest of a windfall. (31 words)
cn: 第四版《精神障碍诊断与统计手册》指出，“病态赌博”涉及持续、反复且无法控制地追求，与其说是追求金钱，不如说是追求冒险以求意外之财的刺激感。

en: It is worrisome that society is medicalizing more and more behavioral problems, often defining as addictions what earlier, sterner generations explained as weakness of will. (25 words)
cn: 令人担忧的是，社会正将越来越多的行为问题医学化，常常将早先更为严格的几代人解释为意志力薄弱的问题，定义为成瘾症。

en: Pushed by science, or what claims to be science, society is reclassifying what once were considered character flaws or moral failings as personality disorders akin to physical disabilities. (28 words)
cn: 在科学——或者自称是科学的东西——的推动下，社会正将曾经被视为性格缺陷或道德缺失的问题，重新归类为类似于身体残疾的人格障碍。

en: Gambling has been a common feature of American life forever, but for a long time it was broadly considered a sin, or a social disease. (24 words)
cn: 赌博一直是美国人生活中一个常见的特征，但在很长一段时间里，它被普遍认为是一种罪恶，或一种社会疾病。

en: Now it is a social policy: the most important and aggressive promoter of gambling in America is the government. (18 words)
cn: 如今，它成了一项社会政策：在美国，最重要、最积极的赌博推动者是政府。

en: Forty-four states have lotteries, 29 have casinos, and most of these states are to varying degrees dependent on—you might say addicted to—revenues from wagering. (26 words)
cn: 44个州有彩票，29个州有赌场，其中大多数州在不同程度上依赖——你或许可以说是沉溺于——来自博彩的收入。

en: And since the first Internet gambling site was created in 1995, competition for gamblers' dollars has become intense. (18 words)
cn: 而且自从1995年首家互联网赌博网站创建以来，对赌徒资金的争夺已变得激烈。

en: The Oct. 28 issue of Newsweek reported that 2 million gamblers patronize 1,800 virtual casinos every week. (16 words)
cn: 《新闻周刊》10月28日一期报道称，每周有200万赌徒光顾1800家虚拟赌场。

en: With $3.5 billion being lost on Internet wagers this year, gambling has passed pornography as the Web's most profitable business. (20 words)
cn: 今年有35亿美元输在互联网博彩上，赌博已超过色情成为网络上最赚钱的生意。

en: How Can a Parent Help? (5 words)
cn: 父母能帮上什么忙？

en: Mothers and fathers can do a lot to ensure a safe landing in early adulthood for their kids. (18 words)
cn: 父母可以做很多事情，来确保他们的孩子平稳步入成年初期。

en: Even if a job's starting salary seems too small to satisfy an emerging adult's need for rapid content, the transition from school to work can be less of a setback if the start-up adult is ready for the move. (36 words)
cn: 即使一份工作的起薪似乎太低，无法满足一个刚成年的年轻人对快速获得感的需求，但如果这个起步阶段的年轻人做好了准备，从学校到工作的过渡就不至于那么受挫。

en: Here are a few measures, drawn from my book Ready or Not, Here Life Comes, that parents can take to prevent what I call "work-life unreadiness". (25 words)
cn: 以下是出自我《无论你准备好了没有，生活已经来了》一书中的几条措施，父母可以采取来防止我所说的“工作生活未就绪”。

en: Help Your Kids Figure Out Who They Are. (8 words)
cn: 帮助孩子认清自己。

en: You can start this process when they are 11 or 12. (11 words)
cn: 你可以在他们11或12岁时就开始这个过程。

en: Periodically review their emerging strengths and weaknesses with them and work together on any shortcomings, like difficulty in communicating well or collaborating. (22 words)
cn: 定期和他们一起审视他们正在显现的优点和缺点，并共同克服任何不足，比如沟通不畅或合作困难。

en: Also, identify the kinds of interests they keep coming back to, as these offer clues to the careers that will fit them best. (23 words)
cn: 同时，找出他们反复回归的兴趣类型，因为这些为他们最适合从事的职业提供了线索。

en: Talk about the Future on a Regular Basis. (8 words)
cn: 定期谈论未来。

en: Kids need a range of authentic role models—as opposed to members of their clique, pop stars and vaunted athletes. (19 words)
cn: 孩子们需要一系列真实的榜样——而非他们小圈子里的成员、流行歌手和被吹捧的运动员。

en: Have regular dinner-table discussions about people the family knows and how they got where they are. (16 words)
cn: 在餐桌上定期讨论家庭认识的人以及他们是如何取得今天的成就的。

en: Discuss the joys and downsides of your own career and encourage your kids to form some ideas about their own future. (21 words)
cn: 讨论你自己职业生涯中的快乐和烦恼，鼓励孩子对自身未来形成一些想法。

en: When asked what they want to do, they should be discouraged from saying "I have no idea." (16 words)
cn: 当被问及想做什么时，应打消他们说“我不知道”的念头。

en: They can change their minds 200 times, but having only a foggy view of the future is of little good. (20 words)
cn: 他们可以改变主意200次，但对未来只有一个模糊的看法几乎没有好处。

en: Build Your Kids' Work Skills. (5 words)
cn: 培养孩子的劳动技能。

en: Teachers are responsible for teaching kids how to learn; parents should be responsible for teaching them how to work. (19 words)
cn: 老师负责教孩子如何学习，父母则应该负责教他们如何工作。

en: Assign responsibilities around the house and make sure homework deadlines are met. (13 words)
cn: 在家里分配职责，并确保作业的截止期限得到遵守。

en: Encourage teenagers to take a part-time job. (7 words)
cn: 鼓励青少年去从事一份兼职工作。

en: Kids need plenty of practice delaying gratification and deploying effective organizational skills, such as managing time and setting priorities. (20 words)
cn: 孩子们需要大量练习延迟满足感和运用有效的组织技能，比如管理时间和设定优先级。

en: Place Time Limits on Leisure Activities. (7 words)
cn: 限制休闲活动的时间。

en: Playing video games encourages immediate content. (7 words)
cn: 玩电子游戏会助长即时满足。

en: And hours of watching TV shows with canned laughter only teaches kids to process information in a passive way. (19 words)
cn: 而长时间观看带有罐头笑声的电视节目，只会教会孩子以被动的方式处理信息。

en: At the same time, listening through earphones to the same monotonous beats for long stretches encourages kids to stay inside themselves, prevent the growth of important communication and thinking skills and make it difficult for kids to develop the kind of sustained concentration they will need for most jobs. (46 words)
cn: 与此同时，长时间戴着耳机听同样单调的节拍，会促使孩子沉浸在自己的世界里，阻碍重要的沟通和思维技能的发展，并使孩子难以培养出大多数工作所需的那种持续专注。

en: Help Kids Develop Coping Strategies. (5 words)
cn: 帮助孩子培养应对策略。

en: They should know how to deal with setbacks, stresses and feelings of inadequacy. (13 words)
cn: 他们应该知道如何应对挫折、压力和不胜任感。

en: They should also learn how to solve problems and resolve conflicts, ways to brainstorm and think critically. (17 words)
cn: 他们也应该学会如何解决问题和化解冲突，以及如何进行头脑风暴和批判性思考。

en: Discussions at home can help kids practice doing these things and help them apply these skills to everyday life situations. (20 words)
cn: 家里的讨论可以帮助孩子们练习做这些事情，并帮助他们将这些技能运用到日常生活情境中。

en: What about the son or daughter who is grown but seems to be struggling and wandering aimlessly through early adulthood? (19 words)
cn: 对于那些已经长大，却似乎在成年初期挣扎徘徊、漫无目标的子女，又该怎么办？

en: Parents still have a major role to play, but now it is more delicate. (14 words)
cn: 父母仍然要发挥重要作用，但现在这个角色更加微妙了。

en: They have to be careful not to come across as disappointed in their child. (14 words)
cn: 他们必须小心，不要流露出对孩子感到失望的样子。

en: They should exhibit strong interest and respect for whatever currently interests their fledging adult (as naive or ill conceived as it may seem) while becoming a partner in exploring options for the future. (31 words)
cn: 他们应该对羽翼未丰的年轻人当前感兴趣的任何事物表现出浓厚的兴趣和尊重（无论看起来多么天真或不成熟），同时成为一起探索未来选择的伙伴。

en: Most of all, these new adults must feel that they are respected and supported by a family that appreciates them. (18 words)
cn: 最重要的是，这些刚步入成年的年轻人必须感受到，自己被一个欣赏他们的家庭所尊重和支持。

en: The time for sharpening pencils, arranging your desk, and doing almost anything else instead of writing has ended. (17 words)
cn: 削铅笔、整理书桌、做几乎所有其他事情而不动笔写的时候已经过去了。

en: The first draft will appear on the page only if you stop avoiding the inevitable and sit, stand up, or lie down to write. (23 words)
cn: 只有当你不再回避这不可避免的事，坐下来、站起身，或者躺下来开始写，初稿才会跃然纸上。

en: It makes no difference how you write, just so you do. (11 words)
cn: 你怎么写并不重要，关键是你要写。

en: Now that you have developed a topic into a tentative thesis, you can assemble your notes and begin to flesh out whatever outline you have made. (25 words)
cn: 既然你已经将一个话题发展成一个初步的论点，你就可以整理笔记，开始充实你已拟定的任何提纲。

en: Be flexible. (2 words)
cn: 要灵活。

en: Your outline should smoothly conduct you from one point to the next, but do not permit it to railroad you. (19 words)
cn: 你的提纲应该顺畅地引导你从一个要点过渡到下一个，但不要让它把你框死。

en: If a relevant and important idea occurs to you now, work it into the draft. (15 words)
cn: 如果你此刻想到了一个相关且重要的想法，就把它写进初稿。

en: By using the first draft as a means of thinking about what you want to say, you will very likely discover more than your notes originally suggested. (27 words)
cn: 通过把初稿当作思考你想说什么的一种手段，你很可能会发现比笔记最初所提示的更多内容。

en: Plenty of good writers don't use outlines at all but discover ordering principles as they write. (16 words)
cn: 许多优秀作家根本不用提纲，而是在写作过程中发现组织原则。

en: Do not attempt to compose a perfectly correct draft the first time around. (13 words)
cn: 不要试图在第一次就写出一份完美无误的草稿。

en: Grammar, punctuation, and spelling can wait until you revise. (10 words)
cn: 语法、标点和拼写可以等到你修改的时候再说。

en: Concentrate on what you are saying. (6 words)
cn: 专注于你想要表达的内容。

en: Good writing most often occurs when you are in hot pursuit of an idea rather than in a nervous search for errors. (22 words)
cn: 好的写作往往发生在你热烈追逐一个想法的时候，而不是紧张地搜寻错误的时候。

en: To make revising easier, leave wide margins and extra space between lines so that you can easily add words, sentences, and corrections. (22 words)
cn: 为了让修改更容易，留出宽页边距和行间额外的空间，这样你就可以轻松地添加词句和修正。

en: Write on only one side of the paper. (8 words)
cn: 只在纸的一面书写。

en: Your pages will be easier to keep track of that way, and, if you have to clip a paragraph to place it elsewhere, you will not lose any writing on the other side. (31 words)
cn: 那样的话你的页面会更容易整理，而且，如果你不得不剪下一个段落放到别处，也不会丢掉另一面上的任何文字。

en: If you are working on a word processor, you can take advantage of its capacity to make additions and deletions as well as move entire paragraphs by making just a few simple keyboard commands. (32 words)
cn: 如果你在文字处理软件上写作，你可以利用它的功能，仅通过几个简单的键盘命令，就能进行增删以及移动整个段落。

en: Some software programs can also check spelling and certain grammatical elements in your writing. (12 words)
cn: 一些软件程序还可以检查你写作中的拼写和某些语法要素。

en: It's worth remembering, however, that though a clean copy fresh off a printer may look terrific, it will read only as well as the thinking and writing that have gone into it. (30 words)
cn: 然而，值得记住的是，虽然刚从打印机出来的干净稿子看起来可能很棒，但它读起来的好坏，只会跟倾注其中的思考和写作一样好。

en: Many writers prudently store their data on disks and print their pages each time they finish a draft to avoid losing any material because of power failures or other problems. (28 words)
cn: 许多作家谨慎地将数据存储在磁盘上，并在每次完成一稿时打印出来，以避免因停电或其他问题丢失任何材料。

en: These printouts are also easier to read than the screen when you work on revisions. (15 words)
cn: 当你进行修改时，这些打印稿也比屏幕更容易阅读。

en: Once you have a first draft on paper, you can delete material that is unrelated to or not convincing. (18 words)
cn: 一旦纸上有了初稿，你就可以删掉与主题无关或没有说服力的材料。

en: The student who wrote "The A&P as a State of Mind" wisely dropped a paragraph that questioned whether Sammy displays chauvinistic attitudes toward women. (22 words)
cn: 那位写了《作为一种心态的A&P》的学生，明智地删掉了一段质疑萨米是否对女性表现出沙文主义态度的段落。

en: Although this is an interesting issue, it has nothing to do with the thesis, which explains how the setting influences Sammy's decision to quit his job. (25 words)
cn: 虽然这是一个有趣的问题，但它与论文无关，论文阐释的是环境如何影响了萨米辞职的决定。

en: Instead of including that paragraph, she added one that described Lengel's crabbed response to the girls so that she could lead up to the A&P "policy" he enforces. (26 words)
cn: 她没有保留那段，而是加了一段，描述伦格尔对女孩们的乖戾反应，以便引出他所执行的A&P“政策”。

en: Remember that your initial draft is only that. (8 words)
cn: 记住，你的初稿仅仅是初稿而已。

en: You should go through the paper many times—and then again—working to substantiate and clarify your ideas. (17 words)
cn: 你应该通读文稿很多遍——然后再读一遍——努力充实和阐明你的想法。

en: You may even end up with several entire versions of the paper. (12 words)
cn: 你甚至可能最终写出好几个完整的版本。

en: Rewrite. (1 words)
cn: 重写。

en: The sentences within each paragraph should be related to a single topic. (12 words)
cn: 每个段落里的句子应该围绕一个单一主题。

en: Transitions should connect one paragraph to the next so that there are no abrupt or confusing shifts. (17 words)
cn: 过渡应该承上启下，这样才不会有突兀或令人困惑的跳跃。

en: Awkward or wordy phrasing or unclear sentences and paragraphs should be mercilessly poked and prodded into shape. (17 words)
cn: 别扭或冗长的措辞，或者含糊不清的句子和段落，都应该毫不留情地反复推敲打磨成型。

en: Coinciding with the groundbreaking theory of biological evolution proposed by Charles Darwin in the 1860s, British social philosopher Herbert Spencer put forward his own theory of biological and cultural evolution. (28 words)
cn: 与查尔斯·达尔文在19世纪60年代提出的开创性生物进化论相呼应，英国社会哲学家赫伯特·斯宾塞提出了自己的生物与文化进化论。

en: Spencer argued that all worldly phenomena, including human societies, changed over time, advancing toward perfection. (15 words)
cn: 斯宾塞认为，一切世间现象，包括人类社会，都随时间而变化，朝着至善的方向发展。

en: He argued that human evolution was characterized by a struggle he called the "survival of the fittest", in which weaker races and societies must eventually be replaced by stronger, more advanced races and societies. (32 words)
cn: 他主张，人类进化的特征在于一种他称之为“适者生存”的斗争，其中较弱的种族和社会最终必然被更强、更先进的种族和社会所取代。

en: American social scientist Lewis Henry Morgan introduced another theory of cultural evolution in the late 1800s. (16 words)
cn: 美国社会科学家路易斯·亨利·摩根在19世纪晚期提出了另一种文化进化论。

en: Morgan helped found modern anthropology—the scientific study of human societies, customs and beliefs—thus becoming one of the earliest anthropologists. (19 words)
cn: 摩根帮助创立了现代人类学——对人类社会、习俗和信仰的科学研究——从而成为最早的人类学家之一。

en: In his work, he attempted to show how all aspects of culture changed together in the evolution of societies. (19 words)
cn: 在他的著作中，他试图展示文化的各个方面是如何在社会进化中一起变化的。

en: Thus, in his view, diverse aspects of culture, such as the structure of families, forms of marriage, categories of kinship, ownership of property, forms of government, technology, and systems of food production, all changed as societies evolved. (35 words)
cn: 因此，在他看来，文化的各种不同方面，如家庭结构、婚姻形式、亲属类别、财产所有权、政府形式、技术以及粮食生产体系，都随着社会的进化而改变。

en: In the early 1900s in North America, German-born American anthropologist Franz Boas developed a new theory of culture known as historical particularism. (22 words)
cn: 20世纪初在北美，德裔美国人类学家弗朗茨·博厄斯发展出一种名为历史特殊论的新文化理论。

en: Historical particularism, which emphasized the uniqueness of all cultures, gave new direction to anthropology. (13 words)
cn: 历史特殊论强调所有文化的独特性，为人类学指明了新的方向。

en: In order to study particular cultures as completely as possible, he became skilled in linguistics, the study of languages, and in physical anthropology, the study of human biology and anatomy. (28 words)
cn: 为了尽可能完整地研究特定文化，他精通了语言学，即研究语言的学问，以及体质人类学，即研究人类生物学与解剖结构的学问。

en: Boas felt that the culture of any society must be understood as the result of a unique history and not as one of many cultures belonging to a broader evolutionary stage or type of culture. (33 words)
cn: 博厄斯认为，任何社会的文化都必须被理解为一个独特历史的结果，而不能被视为属于某个更宏阔的进化阶段或文化类型的众多文化之一。

en: Other anthropologists believed that cultural innovations, such as inventions, had a single origin and passed from society to society. (18 words)
cn: 其他人类学家认为，文化创新，比如发明，具有单一的起源，然后从一个社会传播到另一个社会。

en: This theory was known as diffusionism. (6 words)
cn: 这种理论被称为传播论。

en: Historical particularism became a dominant approach to the study of culture in American anthropology, largely through the influence of many students of Boas. (23 words)
cn: 历史特殊论成为美国人类学文化研究的主导方法，这在很大程度上是受博厄斯众多学生的影响。

en: But a number of anthropologists in the early 1900s also rejected the particularist theory of culture in favor of diffusionism. (18 words)
cn: 但20世纪初也有许多人类学家拒绝了文化特殊论，转而支持传播论。

en: Some attributed virtually every important cultural achievement to the inventions of a few, especially gifted peoples that, according to diffusionists, then spread to other cultures. (25 words)
cn: 有些人几乎将每一项重要的文化成就都归功于为数不多的、格外有天赋的民族的发明，而按照传播论者的观点，这些发明随后传播到了其他文化。

en: For example, British anthropologists Grafton Elliot Smith and W. J. Perry incorrectly suggested, on the basis of inadequate information, that farming, pottery making, and metallurgy all originated in ancient Egypt and diffused throughout the world. (31 words)
cn: 例如，英国人类学家格拉夫顿·埃利奥特·史密斯和W. J. 佩里依据不充分的信息，错误地提出农业、制陶和冶金术都起源于古埃及，然后传播到了世界各地。

en: In fact, all of these cultural developments occurred separately at different times in many parts of the world. (18 words)
cn: 事实上，所有这些文化发展都是分别在世界上许多地方的不同时期独立发生的。

en: Also in the early 1900s, French sociologist Émile Durkheim developed a theory of culture that would greatly influence anthropology. (19 words)
cn: 同样在20世纪初，法国社会学家埃米尔·涂尔干发展了一种将对人类学产生重大影响的文化理论。

en: Durkheim proposed that religious beliefs functioned to reinforce social solidarity. (10 words)
cn: 涂尔干提出，宗教信仰的功能在于巩固社会团结。

en: An interest in the relationship between the function of society and culture became a major theme in European, and especially British, anthropology. (23 words)
cn: 对社会与文化的功能之间关系的兴趣，成为欧洲，尤其是英国人类学的一个重要主题。

en: Retail sales of food and drink in Europe's largest markets are at a standstill, leaving European grocery retailers hungry for opportunities to grow. (23 words)
cn: 欧洲最大市场的食品和饮料零售额陷入了停滞，这让欧洲杂货零售商们渴望找到增长的机会。

en: Most leading retailers have already tried e-commerce, with limited success, and expansion abroad. (14 words)
cn: 大多数领先零售商已经尝试过电子商务，但收效甚微，也尝试过海外扩张。

en: But almost all have ignored the big, profitable opportunity in their own backyard: the wholesale food and drink trade, which appears to be just the kind of market retailers need. (29 words)
cn: 但几乎所有人都忽略了近在眼前的巨大盈利机会：批发食品和饮料贸易，而这似乎正是零售商所需要的那种市场。

en: For example, wholesale food and drink sales came to $268 billion in France, Germany, Italy, Spain, and the United Kingdom in 2000—more than 40 percent of retail sales. (28 words)
cn: 例如，2000年法国、德国、意大利、西班牙和英国的食品饮料批发销售额达到2680亿美元，超过了零售额的40%。

en: Moreover, average overall margins are higher in wholesale than in retail; wholesale demand from the food service sector is growing quickly as more Europeans eat out more often; and changes in the competitive dynamics of this fragmented industry are at last making it feasible for wholesalers to consolidate. (42 words)
cn: 此外，批发的平均总体利润率高于零售；随着越来越多的欧洲人更频繁地外出就餐，来自餐饮服务行业的批发需求正快速增长；而这个零散行业的竞争态势的变化，最终使得批发商的整合成为可能。

en: Despite variations in detail, wholesale markets in the countries that have been closely examined—France, Germany, Italy, and Spain—are made out of the same building blocks. (25 words)
cn: 尽管细节上存在差异，在那些已被仔细研究过的国家——法国、德国、意大利和西班牙——批发市场都是由相同的基本构件组成的。

en: Demand comes mainly from two sources: independent mom-and-pop grocery stores which, unlike large retail chains, are too small to buy straight from producers, and food service operators that cater to consumers when they don't eat at home. (34 words)
cn: 需求主要来自两个来源：一是夫妻食杂店，它们与大型零售连锁店不同，规模太小，无法直接从生产商那里采购；二是为不在家吃饭的消费者提供服务的餐饮服务经营者。

en: Such food service operators range from snack machines to large institutional catering ventures, but most of these businesses are known in the trade as "horeca": hotels, restaurants and cafés. (26 words)
cn: 这类餐饮服务经营者范围从自动零食机到大型机构餐饮企业，但这些生意中的大多数在业内被称为“horeca”：酒店、餐厅和咖啡馆。

en: Overall, Europe's wholesale market for food and drink is growing at the same sluggish pace as the retail market, but the figures, when added together, mask two opposing trends. (28 words)
cn: 总体上，欧洲食品和饮料的批发市场以与零售市场同样缓慢的速度增长，但这些数字加在一起，掩盖了两个相反的趋势。

en: The first and more important is the consumer's growing preference for eating out: the consumption of food and drink in places other than homes has risen from about 32 percent of total consumption in 1995 to 35 percent in 2000 and is expected to approach 38 percent by 2005. (45 words)
cn: 第一个且更重要的趋势是，消费者越来越偏爱外出就餐：家庭以外场所的餐饮消费占总消费的比例，从1995年的约32%上升到2000年的35%，并有望在2005年接近38%。

en: This development is boosting wholesale demand from the food service segment by 4 to 5 percent a year across Europe, compared with growth in retail demand of 1 to 2 percent. (30 words)
cn: 这一发展正推动着整个欧洲来自餐饮服务板块的批发需求，以每年4%到5%的速度增长，而零售需求的增速仅为1%到2%。

en: Meanwhile, as the recession is looming large, people are getting anxious. (12 words)
cn: 同时，随着经济衰退的阴影日益逼近，人们变得焦虑起来。

en: They tend to keep a tighter hold on their purse and consider eating at home a realistic alternative. (18 words)
cn: 他们往往会把钱包捂得更紧，并把在家就餐视为一个现实的选择。

en: All in all, this clearly seems to be a market in which big retailers could profitably apply their gigantic scale, existing infrastructure, and proven skills in the management of product ranges, logistics, and marketing intelligence. (31 words)
cn: 总而言之，这显然是一个大型零售商可以将其巨大规模、现有基础设施以及在产品种类管理、物流和营销情报方面成熟技能，有利可图地加以运用的市场。

en: Retailers that master the intricacies of wholesaling in Europe may well expect to rake in substantial profits thereby. (18 words)
cn: 掌握了欧洲批发业复杂门道的零售商，很可能期望借此赚得可观的利润。

en: At least, that is how it looks as a whole. (10 words)
cn: 至少，从整体上看是这样。

en: Closer inspection reveals important differences among the biggest national markets, especially in their customer segments and wholesale structures, as well as the competitive dynamics of individual food and drink categories. (28 words)
cn: 更仔细的审视显示，最大的国家市场之间存在重要差异，尤其体现在客户细分和批发结构上，也体现在个别食品饮料品类的竞争态势上。

en: Big retailers must understand these differences before they can identify the segments of European wholesaling in which their particular abilities might unseat smaller but entrenched competitors. (25 words)
cn: 大型零售商必须先理解这些差异，然后才能识别出欧洲批发业的哪些板块，其特定能力有可能将那些规模较小但根基稳固的竞争对手淘汰出局。

en: New skills and unfamiliar business models are needed, too. (9 words)
cn: 新的技能和不熟悉的商业模式也是必需的。

en: However, none of these requirements should deter large retailers (and even some large food producers and existing wholesalers) from trying their hand, for those that master the intricacies of wholesaling in Europe stand to reap considerable gains. (33 words)
cn: 然而，这些要求都不应阻止大型零售商（甚至一些大型食品生产商和现有批发商）一试身手，因为那些掌握了欧洲批发业复杂门道的人，有望收获巨大收益。

en: The subtle and intelligent little book The Marketplace of Ideas: Reform and Resistance in the American University should be read by every student thinking of applying to take a degree in America. (29 words)
cn: 但凡考虑申请美国学位的学生，都应该读一读《思想的市场：美国大学的改革与阻力》这本精妙睿智的小书。

en: It explains clearly the problems of the modern American university and how they arose. (13 words)
cn: 它清晰地阐释了现代美国大学的问题及其是如何产生的。

en: It also explains why reforms are so difficult. (7 words)
cn: 它也解释了为什么改革如此困难。

en: Something curious has been happening in American universities, and Louis Menand, a professor of English at Harvard University, captured it skillfully. (21 words)
cn: 美国大学里一直发生着某种奇怪的事情，而哈佛大学英语教授路易斯·梅南德巧妙地捕捉到了这一点。

en: His concern is mainly with the humanities: literature, languages, philosophy and so on. (13 words)
cn: 他主要关注的是人文学科：文学、语言、哲学等等。

en: These are disciplines that are going out of style: 22% of American college graduates now major in business compared with only 2% in history and 4% in English. (27 words)
cn: 这些都是正在变得过时的学科：如今22%的美国大学毕业生主修商科，而主修历史的仅占2%，主修英语的仅占4%。

en: However, many leading American universities want their undergraduates to have a grounding in the basic canon of ideas that every educated person should possess. (24 words)
cn: 然而，许多顶尖的美国大学希望其本科生在每一个受过教育的人都应具备的基本经典思想方面打下基础。

en: But most find it difficult to agree on what a "general education" should look like. (15 words)
cn: 但大多数人发现，很难就“通识教育”应该是什么样达成一致。

en: At Harvard, Mr. Menand notes, "the great books are read because they have been read"—they form a sort of social glue. (20 words)
cn: 在哈佛，梅南德先生指出，“那些伟大著作之所以被阅读，是因为它们一直被人们阅读”——它们形成了一种社会黏合剂。

en: One reason why it is hard to design and teach such courses is that they cut across the insistence by top American universities that liberal-arts education and professional education should be kept separate, taught in different schools. (34 words)
cn: 设计和教授这类课程之所以困难，一个原因是它们与美国顶尖大学所坚持的理念相抵触，即人文教育和专业教育应当分开，在不同的学院里教授。

en: Many students experience both varieties. (5 words)
cn: 许多学生两种类型都体验过。

en: Although more than half of Harvard undergraduates end up in law, medicine or business, future doctors and lawyers must study a non-specialist liberal-arts degree before embarking on a professional qualification. (29 words)
cn: 尽管超过半数的哈佛本科生最终进入法律、医学或商界，但未来的医生和律师在着手获取专业资格之前，必须先攻读一个非专业的人文学位。

en: Besides professionalising the professions by this separation, top American universities have professionalised the professor. (14 words)
cn: 除了通过这种分离使专业变得职业化，美国顶尖大学还把教授也职业化了。

en: The growth in public money for academic research has speeded the process: federal research grants rose fourfold between 1960 and 1990, but faculty teaching hours fell by half as research took its toll. (31 words)
cn: 用于学术研究的公共资金的增长加速了这一进程：联邦研究拨款在1960年至1990年间增长了四倍，但由于研究造成了损失，教师的教学时间减少了一半。

en: Professionalism has turned the acquisition of a doctoral degree into a prerequisite for a successful academic career: as late as 1969 a third of American professors did not possess one. (28 words)
cn: 职业化已经把获得博士学位变成成功学术生涯的先决条件：直到1969年，三分之一的美国教授都还没有博士学位。

en: But the key idea behind professionalisation, argues Mr. Menand, is that "the knowledge and skills needed for a particular specialisation are transmissible but not transferable." (22 words)
cn: 但梅南德先生认为，职业化背后的核心观念是，“特定专业所需的知识和技能是可以传授的，却是不可转移的。”

en: So disciplines acquire a monopoly not just over the production of knowledge, but also over the production of the producers of knowledge. (22 words)
cn: 因此，学科不仅垄断了知识的生产，也垄断了知识生产者的培养。

en: No disciplines have seized on professionalism with as much enthusiasm as the humanities. (12 words)
cn: 没有任何学科像人文学科那样，以如此大的热情投身于职业化。

en: You can, Mr. Menand points out, become a lawyer in three years and a medical doctor in four. (17 words)
cn: 梅南德先生指出，你可以用三年成为律师，四年成为医生。

en: But the regular time it takes to get a doctoral degree in the humanities is nine years. (16 words)
cn: 但拿到人文学科博士学位的通常时间是九年。

en: Not surprisingly, up to half of all doctoral students in English drop out before getting their degrees. (17 words)
cn: 毫不奇怪，多达一半的英语博士生在拿到学位前就辍学了。

en: Equally unsurprisingly, only about half end up with professorships for which they entered graduate school. (15 words)
cn: 同样不令人惊讶的是，只有大约一半的人最终获得了他们当初进入研究生院时所追求的教授职位。

en: There are simply too few posts. (5 words)
cn: 职位实在太少了。

en: This is partly because universities continue to produce ever more PhDs. (12 words)
cn: 这部分是因为大学在持续培养出越来越多的博士。

en: But fewer students want to study humanities subjects: English departments awarded more bachelor's degrees in 1970-71 than they did 20 years later. (22 words)
cn: 但更少的学生想学习人文学科：英语系在1970-71年授予的学士学位比20年后还要多。

en: Fewer students require fewer teachers. (5 words)
cn: 学生少了，需要的老师也少了。

en: So, at the end of a decade of thesis-writing, many humanities students leave the profession altogether. (16 words)
cn: 所以，在长达十年的论文写作之后，许多人文学科的学生干脆离开了这个行业。

en: The key to reforming higher education, concludes Mr. Menand, is to alter the way in which "the producers of knowledge are produced." (20 words)
cn: 梅南德先生总结道，改革高等教育的关键在于改变“知识生产者的培养”方式。

en: Otherwise, academics will continue to think dangerously alike, increasingly detached from the societies which they study, investigate and criticise. (19 words)
cn: 否则，学者们将继续危险地趋同思考，越来越脱离他们所研究、调查和批评的社会。

en: "Academic inquiry, at least in some fields, may need to become less exclusionary and more holistic." (16 words)
cn: “学术研究，至少在某些领域，可能需要变得不那么排外，而更具整体性。”

en: Yet quite how that happens, Mr. Menand does not say. (11 words)
cn: 然而，这究竟如何实现，梅南德先生并未说明。

en: Think of those fleeting moments when you look out of an aeroplane window and realise that you are flying, higher than a bird. (22 words)
cn: 想想那些转瞬即逝的瞬间：你望向飞机舷窗之外，意识到自己正在飞行，比鸟儿飞得还高。

en: Now think of your laptop, thinner than a brown-paper envelope, or your cellphone in the palm of your hand. (18 words)
cn: 现在再想想你的笔记本电脑，比牛皮纸信封还薄，或者你掌中的手机。

en: Take a moment or two to wonder at those marvels. (10 words)
cn: 花片刻时间来惊叹一下这些奇迹吧。

en: You are the lucky inheritor of a dream come true. (10 words)
cn: 你是美梦成真的幸运继承者。

en: The second half of the 20th century saw a collection of geniuses, warriors, entrepreneurs and visionaries labour to create a fabulous machine that could function as a typewriter and printing press, studio and theatre, paintbrush and gallery, piano and radio, the mail as well as the mail carrier. (45 words)
cn: 20世纪下半叶，一群天才、勇士、企业家和远见卓识者辛勤努力，创造出了一台神奇的机器，它能充当打字机和印刷机、工作室和剧院、画笔和画廊、钢琴和收音机，以及邮件和邮递员。

en: Not only did they develop such a device but by the turn of the millennium they had also managed to embed it in a worldwide system accessed by billions of people every day. (30 words)
cn: 他们不仅研制出了这样一种设备，而且还在世纪之交成功地将其嵌入了一个每天有数十亿人访问的全球系统中。

en: The networked computer is an amazing device, the first media machine that serves as the mode of production, means of distribution, site of reception, and place of praise and critique. (29 words)
cn: 联网计算机是一种令人惊叹的设备，是第一台集生产方式、分发手段、接收站点以及赞美与批评场所于一体的媒体机器。

en: The computer is the 21st century's culture machine. (8 words)
cn: 计算机是21世纪的文化机器。

en: But for all the reasons there are to celebrate the computer, we must also act with caution. (16 words)
cn: 但是，尽管有种种理由去赞美计算机，我们也必须谨慎行事。

en: This is because the networked computer has sparked a secret war between downloading and uploading—between passive consumption and active creation—whose outcome will shape our collective future in ways we can only begin to imagine. (34 words)
cn: 这是因为联网计算机已引发了一场下载与上传之间——被动消费与主动创造之间——的秘密战争，其结果将以我们只能初步想象的方式，塑造我们共同的未来。

en: I call it a secret war for two reasons. (9 words)
cn: 我称之为一场秘密战争，原因有两点。

en: First, most people do not realise that there are strong commercial agendas at work to keep them in passive consumption mode. (20 words)
cn: 首先，大多数人没有意识到，有强大的商业力量在运作，意图让他们停留在被动消费模式中。

en: Second, the majority of people who use networked computers to upload are not even aware of the significance of what they are doing. (24 words)
cn: 其次，大多数使用联网计算机上传的人，甚至没有意识到自己所做之事的意义。

en: All animals download, but only a few upload. (8 words)
cn: 所有动物都在下载，但只有少数在上传。

en: Beavers build dams and birds make nests. (7 words)
cn: 河狸筑坝，鸟儿筑巢。

en: Yet for the most part, the animal kingdom moves through the world downloading. (13 words)
cn: 然而在很大程度上，动物王国是以下载的方式穿行于世间的。

en: Humans are unique in their capacity to not only make tools but then turn around and use them to create superfluous material goods—paintings, sculpture and architecture—and superfluous experiences—music, literature, religion and philosophy. (32 words)
cn: 人类的独特之处在于，他们不仅能够制造工具，而且还能转而运用工具去创造富余的物质产品——绘画、雕塑和建筑——以及富余的精神体验——音乐、文学、宗教和哲学。

en: Of course, it is precisely these superfluous things that define human culture and ultimately what it is to be human. (19 words)
cn: 当然，恰恰是这些富余之物定义了人类文化，并最终定义了何以为人。

en: Downloading and consuming culture requires great skills, but failing to move beyond downloading is to strip oneself of a defining constituent of humanity. (22 words)
cn: 下载和消费文化需要高超的技能，但如果不能超越下载，就是剥夺了自己作为人的一个决定性要素。

en: For all the possibilities of our new culture machines, most people are still stuck in download mode. (17 words)
cn: 尽管我们的新文化机器有种种可能性，大多数人仍然困在下载模式里。

en: Even after the advent of widespread social media, a pyramid of production remains, with a small number of people uploading material, a slightly larger group commenting on or modifying that content, and a huge percentage remaining content to just consume. (38 words)
cn: 即使在广泛的社交媒体出现之后，一个生产的金字塔依然存在：少数人上传内容，稍多一点的人评论或修改那些内容，而绝大多数人仍然满足于纯粹的消费。

en: One reason for the persistence of this pyramid of production is that for the past half-century, much of the world's media culture has been defined by a single medium—television—and television is defined by downloading. (33 words)
cn: 这种生产金字塔持续存在的一个原因是，在过去半个世纪里，世界上大部分媒体文化是由单一媒介——电视——所定义的，而电视的定义就是下载。

en: Television is a one-way tap flowing into our homes. (9 words)
cn: 电视是一个单向流入我们家庭的水龙头。

en: The hardest task that television asks of anyone is to turn the power off after he has turned it on. (19 words)
cn: 电视要求任何人完成的最艰巨的任务，就是在打开它之后再把它关掉。

en: The networked computer offers the first chance in 50 years to reverse the flow, to encourage thoughtful downloading and, even more importantly, meaningful uploading. (24 words)
cn: 联网计算机提供了50年来第一次扭转流向的机会，去鼓励深思熟虑的下载，以及更重要的，有意义的上传。

en: What counts as meaningful uploading? (5 words)
cn: 什么才算有意义的上传？

en: My definition revolves around the concept of "stickiness"—creations and experiences to which others adhere. (14 words)
cn: 我的定义围绕着“粘性”这个概念——即他人愿意追随的创造和体验。

en: The social sciences are flourishing. (5 words)
cn: 社会科学正在蓬勃发展。

en: As of 2005, there were almost half a million professional social scientists from all fields in the world, working both inside and outside academia. (23 words)
cn: 截至2005年，全球共有近50万名来自各领域的专业社会科学家，工作在学术界内外。

en: According to the World Social Science Report 2010, the number of social-science students worldwide has swollen by about 11% every year since 2000. (22 words)
cn: 根据《2010年世界社会科学报告》，自2000年以来，全球社会科学专业学生数量每年增长约11%。

en: Yet this enormous resource is not contributing enough to today's global challenges, including climate change, security, sustainable development and health. (21 words)
cn: 然而，这一庞大的资源对当今全球挑战——包括气候变化、安全、可持续发展和健康——的贡献还远远不够。

en: These issues all have root causes in human behavior: all require behavioral change and social innovations, as well as technological development. (19 words)
cn: 这些问题的根源都在于人类行为：所有这些都需要行为改变和社会创新，以及技术发展。

en: Stemming climate change, for example, is as much about changing consumption patterns and promoting tax acceptance as it is about developing clean energy. (22 words)
cn: 例如，遏制气候变化，不仅关乎开发清洁能源，也同样关乎改变消费模式和提高税收接受度。

en: Humanity has the necessary agro-technological tools to eradicate hunger, from genetically engineered crops to artificial fertilizers. (15 words)
cn: 人类拥有从转基因作物到人工化肥等必需的农业技术工具来消除饥饿。

en: Here, too, the problems are social: the organization of food distribution and economic inequality. (14 words)
cn: 在这里，问题同样是社会性的：粮食分配的组织和经济不平等。

en: Despite these factors, many social scientists seem reluctant to tackle such problems. (12 words)
cn: 尽管有这些因素，许多社会科学家似乎不愿意去解决这类问题。

en: And in Europe, some are up in arms over a proposal to drop a specific funding category for social-science research and to integrate it within cross-cutting topics of sustainable development. (29 words)
cn: 而在欧洲，一些人正强烈抗议一项提议，该提议要取消一个专门的社会科学研究资助类别，并将其整合到可持续发展的跨领域主题中。

en: This is a shame—the community should be grasping the opportunity to raise its influence in the real world. (18 words)
cn: 这是一种遗憾——社会科学界本应抓住这个机会，提升其在现实世界中的影响力。

en: To paraphrase the great social scientist Joseph Schumpeter: there is no radical innovation without creative destruction. (15 words)
cn: 借用伟大的社会科学家约瑟夫·熊彼特的话来说：没有创造性的破坏，就没有彻底的创新。

en: Today, the social sciences are largely focused on disciplinary problems and internal scholarly debates, rather than on topics with external impact. (20 words)
cn: 今天，社会科学主要聚焦于学科问题和内部学术辩论，而非具有外部影响的主题。

en: Analyses reveal that the number of papers including the keywords "environmental change" or "climate change" have increased rapidly since 2004. (19 words)
cn: 分析显示，自2004年以来，包含“环境变化”或“气候变化”关键词的论文数量迅速增加。

en: However, the numbers are still small: in 2010, about 1,600 of the 100,000 social-sciences papers published globally included one of these keywords. (22 words)
cn: 然而，数量仍然很少：2010年，全球发表的10万篇社会科学论文中，约有1600篇包含了这些关键词中的一个。

en: When social scientists do tackle practical issues, their scope is often local: Belgium is interested mainly in the effects of poverty on Belgium, for example. (24 words)
cn: 当社会科学家确实处理实际问题时，他们的范围往往是地方性的：例如，比利时主要对贫困对比利时的影响感兴趣。

en: And whether the community's work contributes much to an overall accumulation of knowledge is doubtful. (15 words)
cn: 而且，学术界的工作是否对整体知识的积累有显著贡献，是值得怀疑的。

en: The problem is not necessarily the amount of available funding. (10 words)
cn: 问题并不一定是可用资金的数额。

en: During the late 1990s, national spending on social sciences and the humanities as a percentage of all research and development funds—including government, higher education, non-profit and corporate—varied from around 4% to 25%; in most European nations, it is about 15%. (39 words)
cn: 在20世纪90年代后期，各国花在社会科学和人文科学上的支出，占全部研究与开发资金——包括政府、高等教育、非营利机构和企业的资金——的比例从约4%到25%不等；在大多数欧洲国家，这个比例大约是15%。

en: This is an adequate amount so long as it is aimed in the right direction. (14 words)
cn: 只要瞄准了正确的方向，这是一个足够的数额。

en: Social scientists who complain about a lack of funding should not expect more in today's economic climate. (17 words)
cn: 那些抱怨缺乏资金的社会科学家，在当今的经济环境下不应期望得到更多。

en: The trick is to direct these funds better. (8 words)
cn: 诀窍在于更好地引导这些资金。

en: The European Union Framework funding programs have long had a category specifically targeted at social scientists. (15 words)
cn: 欧盟框架资助计划长期以来一直有一个专门针对社会科学家的类别。

en: This year, it was proposed that the system be changed: Horizon 2020, a new program to be enacted in 2014, would not have such a category. (24 words)
cn: 今年，有人提议改变这一体系：将于2014年颁布的新计划“地平线2020”将不再设有这样一个类别。

en: This has resulted in protests from social scientists. (8 words)
cn: 这导致了社会科学家的抗议。

en: But the intention is not to neglect social science; rather, the complete opposite. (13 words)
cn: 但其意图并不是忽视社会科学；恰恰相反。

en: The idea is to force social scientists to integrate their work with other categories, including health and demographic change; food security; marine research and the bio-economy; clean, efficient energy; and inclusive, innovative and secure societies. (32 words)
cn: 这个想法是要迫使社会科学家将他们的工作与其他领域相整合，包括健康与人口变化、粮食安全、海洋研究和生物经济、清洁高效能源，以及包容、创新和安全的社会。

en: That should create more collaborative endeavors and help to develop projects aimed directly at solving global problems. (17 words)
cn: 那应该会创造出更多的合作努力，并有助于发展旨在直接解决全球问题的项目。

en: How do archaeologists know where to find what they are looking for when there is nothing visible on the surface of the ground? (22 words)
cn: 当地表上什么都看不见时，考古学家怎么知道该去哪里找他们要找的东西呢？

en: Typically, they survey and sample (make test excavations on) large areas of terrain to determine where excavation will yield useful information. (21 words)
cn: 通常，他们对大片区域进行勘测和取样（做试探性挖掘），以确定在哪里发掘能獲得有用的信息。

en: Surveys and test samples have also become important for understanding the larger landscapes that contain archaeological sites. (16 words)
cn: 勘测和测试样本对于理解那些包含考古遗址的更大地貌也变得十分重要。

en: Some archaeological sites have always been easily observable—for example, the Parthenon in Athens; the pyramids of Giza in Egypt; and the megaliths of Stonehenge in southern England. (26 words)
cn: 有些考古遗址一直都很容易被观察到——例如，雅典的帕特农神庙、埃及的吉萨金字塔，以及英格兰南部巨石阵的巨石。

en: But these sites are exceptions to the norm. (8 words)
cn: 但这些遗址是常规之外的例外。

en: Most archaeological sites have been located by means of careful searching, while many others have been discovered by accident. (19 words)
cn: 大多数考古遗址都是通过仔细搜寻才得以定位的，而其他许多则是偶然发现的。

en: Olduvai Gorge, an early hominid site in Tanzania, was found by a butterfly hunter who literally fell into its deep valley in 1911. (22 words)
cn: 坦桑尼亚的早期人类遗址奥杜威峡谷，就是由一个蝴蝶猎人在1911年实打实地掉进那深谷时才被发现的。

en: Thousands of Aztec artifacts came to light during the digging of the Mexico City Metro in the 1970s. (17 words)
cn: 成千上万的阿兹特克文物是20世纪70年代在挖掘墨西哥城地铁时重见天日的。

en: Most archaeological sites, however, are discovered by archaeologists who have set out to look for them. (15 words)
cn: 然而，大多数考古遗址是被专门出发去寻找它们的考古学家发现的。

en: Such searches can take years. (5 words)
cn: 这类搜寻可能耗时数年。

en: British archaeologist Howard Carter knew that the tomb of the Egyptian pharaoh Tutankhamun existed from information found in other sites. (19 words)
cn: 英国考古学家霍华德·卡特从其他遗址发现的信息得知，埃及法老图坦卡蒙的陵墓是存在的。

en: Carter sifted through rubble in the Valley of the Kings for seven years before he located the tomb in 1922. (20 words)
cn: 卡特在帝王谷的碎石中筛寻了七年，才于1922年找到了那座陵墓。

en: In the late 1800s British archaeologist Sir Arthur Evans combed antique dealers' stores in Athens, Greece. (16 words)
cn: 19世纪末，英国考古学家阿瑟·埃文斯爵士仔细搜寻了希腊雅典的古董商店铺。

en: He was searching for tiny engraved seals attributed to the ancient Mycenaean civilization that dominated Greece from the 1400s to 1200s BC. (20 words)
cn: 他在寻找属于古迈锡尼文明的微小雕刻印章，该文明于公元前15世纪到公元前13世纪统治着希腊。

en: Evans's interpretations of these engravings eventually led him to find the Minoan palace at Knossos (Knosós), on the island of Crete, in 1900. (22 words)
cn: 埃文斯对这些雕刻图案的解读，最终于1900年引导他在克里特岛上发现了克诺索斯的米诺斯王宫。

en: To find their sites, archaeologists today rely heavily on systematic survey methods and a variety of high-technology tools and techniques. (19 words)
cn: 为了找到遗址，如今的考古学家主要依赖系统的勘测方法以及各种高科技工具和技术。

en: Airborne technologies, such as different types of radar and photographic equipment carried by airplanes or spacecraft, allow archaeologists to learn about what lies beneath the ground without digging. (27 words)
cn: 机载技术，例如由飞机或航天器携带的各种雷达和摄影设备，使考古学家无需挖掘就能了解地下埋着什么。

en: Aerial surveys locate general areas of interest or larger buried features, such as ancient buildings or fields. (16 words)
cn: 航空勘测可定位大致值得关注的区域，或者较大的地下特征，比如古建筑或古田野。

en: Ground surveys allow archaeologists to pinpoint the places where digs will be successful. (13 words)
cn: 地面勘测使考古学家能够精确找到挖掘会成功的地点。

en: Most ground surveys involve a lot of walking, looking for surface clues such as small fragments of pottery. (17 words)
cn: 大多数地面勘测需要大量步行，寻找地表线索，如细小的陶器碎片。

en: They often include a certain amount of digging to test for buried materials at selected points across a landscape. (19 words)
cn: 它们常常包括一定量的挖掘工作，以便在一片地域内的选定地点测试是否存在被掩埋的材料。

en: Archaeologists also may locate buried remains by using such technologies as ground radar, magnetic-field recording, and metal detectors. (18 words)
cn: 考古学家还可以借助诸如探地雷达、磁场记录和金属探测器等技术来定位被掩埋的遗迹。

en: Archaeologists commonly use computers to map sites and the landscapes around sites. (12 words)
cn: 考古学家通常使用计算机来绘制遗址及其周围景观的地图。

en: Two- and three-dimensional maps are helpful tools in planning excavations, illustrating how sites look, and presenting the results of archaeological research. (21 words)
cn: 二维和三维地图在规划发掘、展示遗址外观以及呈现考古研究成果方面都是有用的工具。

en: Surveys can cover a single large settlement or entire landscapes. (10 words)
cn: 勘测可以覆盖一个大型定居点，也可以覆盖整个地貌。

en: In one case, many researchers working around the ancient Maya city of Copán, Honduras, have located hundreds of archaeological sites by means of ground surveys conducted on foot. (28 words)
cn: 在一个案例中，许多在洪都拉斯科潘古玛雅城周边工作的研究人员，通过徒步进行地面勘测，定位了数百个考古遗址。

en: The resulting settlement maps show how the distribution and density of the rural population around the city changed dramatically between AD 500 and 850, when Copán collapsed. (26 words)
cn: 由此产生的定居点地图表明，在公元500至850年间，当科潘衰落时，城市周围农村人口的分布和密度发生了剧烈变化。

en: In another case, American archaeologists René Millon and George Cowgill spent years systematically mapping the entire city of Teotihuacán in the Valley of Mexico near what is now Mexico City. (29 words)
cn: 在另一个案例中，美国考古学家勒内·米隆和乔治·考吉尔花费数年时间，系统地绘制了位于墨西哥山谷、如今墨西哥城附近的特奥蒂瓦坎整个城市的地图。

en: At its peak around AD 600, this city was one of the largest human settlements in the world. (18 words)
cn: 在大约公元600年的鼎盛时期，这座城市是世界上最大的人类定居点之一。

en: The researchers mapped not only the city's vast and ornate ceremonial areas, but also hundreds of simpler apartment complexes where common people lived. (22 words)
cn: 研究人员不仅绘制了该城庞大而华丽的仪式区域，还绘制了数百个平民居住的较简朴的公寓建筑群。

en: How does your reading proceed? (5 words)
cn: 你的阅读是如何进行的？

en: Clearly you try to comprehend, in the sense of identifying meanings for individual words and working out relationships between them, drawing on your implicit knowledge of English grammar. (28 words)
cn: 显然你会试图去理解，从这个意义上说：借助你內隐的英语语法知识，识别每个单词的含义，并理清它们之间的关系。

en: If you are unfamiliar with words or idioms, you guess at their meaning, using clues presented in the context. (18 words)
cn: 如果你碰到不认识的单词或习语，你会利用上下文提供的线索去猜它们的意思。

en: On the assumption that they will become relevant later, you make a mental note of discourse entities as well as possible links between them. (23 words)
cn: 基于它们之后会变得相关的假设，你会在心里记下话语实体以及它们之间可能存在的联系。

en: You begin to infer a context for the text, for instance, by making decisions about what kind of speech event is involved: Who is making the utterance, to whom, when and where. (31 words)
cn: 你开始推断文本的语境，例如，通过判断所涉及的是哪种言语事件：谁在说话，对谁说话，什么时候，在哪里。

en: The ways of reading indicated here are without doubt kinds of comprehension. (13 words)
cn: 这里所指的阅读方式无疑是各种理解方式。

en: But they show comprehension to consist not just of passive assimilation but of active engagement in inference and problem-solving. (18 words)
cn: 但它们表明，理解不仅由被动的吸收组成，也由主动的推断和解决问题组成。

en: You infer information you feel the writer has invited you to grasp by presenting you with specific evidence and clues. (19 words)
cn: 你推断的信息，是你觉得作者通过向你呈现具体证据和线索而邀请你去把握的信息。

en: You make further inferences, for instance, about how the text may be significant to you, or about its validity—inferences that form the basis of a personal response for which the author will inevitably be far less responsible. (36 words)
cn: 你会做出进一步的推断，例如，关于这段文本对你而言可能有何意义，或者关于它的有效性——这些推断构成了个人反应的基础，而作者对此不可避免地要少负很多责任。

en: Conceived in this way, comprehension will not follow exactly the same track for each reader. (15 words)
cn: 按这种方式来设想，理解不会对每个读者都遵循完全一样的路径。

en: What is in question is not the retrieval of an absolute, fixed or "true" meaning that can be read off and checked for accuracy, or some timeless relation of the text to the world. (33 words)
cn: 这里讨论的问题，不是要提取一个可以被直接读出并核对准确性的绝对、固定或“真实”的含义，也不是要寻找文本与世界之间的某种永恒关系。

en: Rather, we ascribe meanings to texts on the basis of interaction between what we might call textual and contextual material: between kinds of organization or patterning we perceive in a text's formal structures (so especially its language structures) and various kinds of background, social knowledge, belief and attitude that we bring to the text. (51 words)
cn: 相反，我们赋予文本的含义，建立在可以称之为文本材料与语境材料之间的互动上：即我们在文本的形式结构（尤其是其语言结构）中感知到的各种组织或模式，与我们所带入文本的各种背景、社会知识、信念和态度之间的互动。

en: Such background material inevitably reflects who we are. (8 words)
cn: 这些背景材料不可避免地反映了我们是谁。

en: Factors such as the place and period in which we are reading, our gender, ethnicity, age and social class will encourage us towards certain interpretations but at the same time obscure or even close off others. (33 words)
cn: 诸如我们阅读的地点与时代、我们的性别、种族、年龄和社会阶层等因素，会促使我们走向某些特定的解释，但同时也遮蔽甚至阻塞了其他的解释。

en: This doesn't, however, make interpretation merely relative or even pointless. (10 words)
cn: 然而，这并不意味着解释就仅仅是相对的，甚至毫无意义。

en: Precisely because readers from different historical periods, places and social experiences produce different but overlapping readings of the same words on the page—including for texts that engage with fundamental human concerns—debates about texts can play an important role in social discussion and social change. (40 words)
cn: 正是因为来自不同历史时期、地点和社会经验的读者，对书页上相同的文字——包括那些涉及人类根本关切的文本——产生不同但有交叠的解读，关于文本的辩论才能在社会讨论和社会变革中发挥重要作用。

en: How we read a given text also depends to some extent on our particular interest in reading it. (18 words)
cn: 我们如何阅读一个特定文本，在某种程度上也取决于我们阅读它的特定兴趣点。

en: Are we studying that text and trying to respond in a way that fulfils the requirement of a given course? (20 words)
cn: 我们是在研读那个文本，并试图以一种满足某门课程要求的方式做出回应吗？

en: Reading it simply for pleasure? (5 words)
cn: 仅仅为了乐趣而阅读？

en: Skimming it for information? (5 words)
cn: 为获取信息而浏览？

en: Ways of reading on a train or in bed are likely to differ considerably from reading in a seminar room. (20 words)
cn: 在火车上或床上的阅读方式，很可能完全不同于在研讨室里的阅读方式。

en: Such dimensions of reading suggest—as others introduced later in the book will also do—that we bring an implicit (often unacknowledged) agenda to any act of reading. (26 words)
cn: 阅读的这些维度——正如本书后面介绍的其他维度也将表明的那样——表明我们对任何阅读行为都带有一个隐含的（常常未被承认的）议程。

en: It doesn't then necessarily follow that one kind of reading is fuller, more advanced or more worthwhile than another. (19 words)
cn: 那么，并不必然意味着一种阅读方式就比另一种更充实、更高级或更有价值。

en: Ideally, different kinds of reading inform each other, and act as useful reference points for and counterbalances to one another. (19 words)
cn: 理想情况下，不同类型的阅读相互充实，并互为有益的参照点，也互相制衡。

en: Together, they make up the reading component of your overall literacy, or relationship to your surrounding textual environment. (18 words)
cn: 它们共同构成了你整体读写能力中的阅读成分，或是你与周围文本环境的关系。

en: Decide if the time is right. (6 words)
cn: 判断时机是否合适。

en: As an executive coach, I've seen image upgrades be particularly helpful during transitions—when looking for a new job, stepping into a new or more public role, or changing work environments. (28 words)
cn: 作为一名高管教练，我见证过形象提升在过渡期尤其有帮助——在寻找新工作、步入新的或更公开的角色，或者变换工作环境之时。

en: If you're in a period of change or just feeling stuck and in a rut, now may be a good time. (20 words)
cn: 如果你正处在一个变化期，或者只是感觉停滞不前、墨守成规，现在或许正是个好时机。

en: If you're not sure, ask for honest feedback from trusted friends, colleagues and professionals. (13 words)
cn: 如果你不确定，向你信任的朋友、同事和专业人士寻求坦誠的反馈。

en: Look for cues about how others perceive you. (8 words)
cn: 留意别人如何看待你的线索。

en: Maybe there's no need for an upgrade and that's OK. (11 words)
cn: 也许没必要提升，那也无妨。

en: Know your goals. (3 words)
cn: 明确你的目标。

en: Get clear on what impact you're hoping to have. (10 words)
cn: 想清楚你希望产生什么样的影响。

en: Are you looking to refresh your image or pivot it? (10 words)
cn: 你是想刷新自己的形象，还是想彻底改变它？

en: For one person, the goal may be to be taken more seriously and enhance their professional image. (17 words)
cn: 对某个人来说，目标可能是要被更认真地对待，提升自己的专业形象。

en: For another, it may be to be perceived as more approachable, or more modern and stylish. (16 words)
cn: 对另一人来说，目标可能是要被认为更平易近人，或者更时尚、更有风格。

en: For someone moving from finance to advertising, maybe they want to look more "SoHo." (14 words)
cn: 对一个从金融业转到广告业的人来说，也许他们想让自己看起来更“苏荷区”范儿。

en: (It's OK to use characterizations like that.) (8 words)
cn: （用那样的描述是没问题的。）

en: Understand the context. (3 words)
cn: 了解环境背景。

en: Look at your work environment like an anthropologist. (8 words)
cn: 像人类学家一样审视你的工作环境。

en: What are the norms of your environment? (7 words)
cn: 你所在环境的规范是什么？

en: What conveys status? (4 words)
cn: 什么能体现地位？

en: Who are your most important audiences? (7 words)
cn: 谁是你最重要的观众？

en: How do the people you respect and look up to present themselves? (13 words)
cn: 你所尊重和敬仰的人是如何展示自己的？

en: The better you understand the cultural context, the more control you can have over your impact. (17 words)
cn: 你越是了解文化背景，就越能掌控你所产生的影响。

en: Work with professionals. (3 words)
cn: 与专业人士合作。

en: Enlist the support of professionals and share with them your goals and context. (13 words)
cn: 争取专业人士的支持，并与他们分享你的目标和所处环境。

en: Hire a stylist instead of trying to shop on your own, and try a tailor rather than a bargain store. (19 words)
cn: 雇一位造型师，而不是自己试着去购物；找一位裁缝，而不是去折扣店。

en: Consult a dermatologist instead of just trying drugstore creams, or hire a personal trainer instead of following random online workouts. (20 words)
cn: 咨询皮肤科医生，而不只是试用药店的护肤霜；或者聘请一位私人教练，而不是跟着网上隨意的训练视频练。

en: Work with a professional photographer instead of your spouse or friend. (11 words)
cn: 和专业摄影师合作，而不是让你的配偶或朋友来拍。

en: It's not as expensive as you might think. (8 words)
cn: 这并不像你想象的那么贵。

en: Make it efficient. (3 words)
cn: 让它变得高效。

en: The point of a style upgrade isn't to become more vain or to spend more time fussing over what to wear. (20 words)
cn: 形象提升的意义不在于变得更虚荣，或者花更多时间在穿什么上纠结。

en: Instead, use it as an opportunity to reduce decision fatigue. (10 words)
cn: 相反，把它當作一个减少決策疲劳的机会。

en: Pick a standard work uniform or a few go-to options. (10 words)
cn: 选定一套标准的工作行头，或者几个首选方案。

en: Buy all your clothes at once with a stylist instead of shopping alone, one article of clothing at a time. (21 words)
cn: 和造型师一起一次性买完所有衣服，而不是自己一个人去逛街，一次买一件。

en: Charles Dickens is probably the best-known and, to many people, the greatest English novelist of the 19th century. (18 words)
cn: 查尔斯·狄更斯可能是19世纪最著名，而且对许多人来说，最伟大的英国小说家。

en: A moralist, satirist, and social reformer, Dickens crafted complex characters and vivid plots that exposed social injustices while entertaining readers. (19 words)
cn: 作为一位道德家、讽刺作家和社会改革家，狄更斯塑造了复杂的人物和生动的故事情节，在娱乐读者的同时又揭露了社会不公。

en: Dickens was born in Portsmouth, on England's southern coast. (9 words)
cn: 狄更斯出生在英格兰南部海岸的朴茨茅斯。

en: His father was a clerk in the British Navy pay office—a respectable position, but with little social status. (19 words)
cn: 他的父亲是英国海军军需处的一名职员——一个体面的职位，但没什么社会地位。

en: His paternal grandparents, a steward and a housekeeper, possessed even less status, having been servants, and Dickens later concealed their background. (21 words)
cn: 他的祖父母是一名管家和一名女佣，地位更低，曾是仆人，狄更斯后来隐瞒了他们的背景。

en: Dickens's mother supposedly came from a more respectable family. (9 words)
cn: 狄更斯的母亲据说来自一个更体面的家庭。

en: Yet two years before Dickens's birth, his mother's father was caught stealing and fled to Europe, never to return. (19 words)
cn: 但在狄更斯出生前两年，他的外祖父因偷窃被抓，逃往欧洲，再也没有回来。

en: The family's increasing poverty forced Dickens out of school at age 12 to work in Warren's Blacking Warehouse, a shoe-polish factory, where the other working boys mocked him as "the young gentleman." (31 words)
cn: 家境的日益贫困迫使狄更斯在12岁时辍学，到沃伦鞋油厂工作，那是一家鞋油厂，那里的其他童工嘲笑他是“小绅士”。

en: His father was then imprisoned for debt. (7 words)
cn: 他的父亲随后因欠债而被关进监狱。

en: The humiliations of his father's imprisonment and his labor in the blacking factory formed Dickens's greatest wound and became his deepest secret. (21 words)
cn: 父亲入狱的屈辱以及在鞋油厂的劳动，构成了狄更斯最大的伤痛，也成为他最深的秘密。

en: He could not confide them even to his wife, although they provide the unacknowledged foundation of his fiction. (17 words)
cn: 他甚至不能向妻子吐露这些，尽管它们為他的小说提供了未被承认的基石。

en: Soon after his father's release from prison, Dickens got a better job as errand boy in law offices. (18 words)
cn: 父亲从监狱获释后不久，狄更斯找到了一份更好的工作，在律师事务所当跑腿。

en: He taught himself shorthand to get an even better job later as a court stenographer and as a reporter in Parliament. (21 words)
cn: 他自学了速记，后来求得了一份更好的工作，先是当法庭速记员，后是当议会记者。

en: At the same time, Dickens, who had a reporter's eye for transcribing the life around him, especially anything comic or odd, submitted short sketches to obscure magazines. (26 words)
cn: 与此同时，狄更斯用记者的眼光记录周围的生活，尤其是任何滑稽或古怪的事情，并向不知名的杂志投递短篇速写。

en: The first published sketch, "A Dinner at Poplar Walk" brought tears to Dickens's eyes when he discovered it in the pages of The Monthly Magazine. (23 words)
cn: 当狄更斯在《每月杂志》的页面上发现第一篇被刊印的速写《白杨路的一次晚餐》时，他热泪盈眶。

en: From then on his sketches, which appeared under the pen name "Boz" in The Evening Chronicle, earned him a modest reputation. (19 words)
cn: 从那以后，他以笔名“博兹”在《晚间纪事报》上发表的速写，为他赢得了不小的名气。

en: Soon after Sketches by Boz appeared, a publishing firm approached Dickens to write a story in monthly installments, as a backdrop for a series of woodcuts by the then-famous artist Robert Seymour, who had originated the idea for the story. (36 words)
cn: 《博兹速写集》问世后不久，一家出版公司找到狄更斯，请他按月分期写一个故事，作为当时著名画家罗伯特·西摩一系列木刻画的配文，西摩是这个故事创意的始创者。

en: With characteristic confidence, Dickens successfully insisted that Seymour's pictures illustrate his own story instead. (13 words)
cn: 狄更斯带着他一贯的自信，成功坚持要西摩的画反过来为自己的故事配图。

en: After the first installment, Dickens wrote to the artist and asked him to correct a drawing Dickens felt was not faithful enough to his prose. (24 words)
cn: 在第一期连载之后，狄更斯写信给这位画家，要求他修改一幅狄更斯认为不够忠实于他散文的画。

en: Seymour made the change, went into his backyard, and expressed his displeasure by committing suicide. (14 words)
cn: 西摩做了修改，然后走到后院，以自杀表达了他的不满。

en: Dickens and his publishers simply pressed on with a new artist. (10 words)
cn: 狄更斯和他的出版商干脆换了一位新画家继续。

en: The comic novel, The Posthumous Papers of the Pickwick Club, appeared serially in 1836 and 1837 and was first published in book form in 1837. (25 words)
cn: 这部喜剧小说《匹克威克俱乐部外传》于1836年和1837年分期连载，并于1837年首次以图书形式出版。

en: The runaway success of The Pickwick Papers, as it is generally known today, secured Dickens's fame. (19 words)
cn: 《匹克威克外传》——如今人们普遍这样称呼它——的巨大成功，奠定了狄更斯的名声。

en: There were Pickwick coats and Pickwick cigars, and the plump, spectacled hero, Samuel Pickwick, became a national figure. (17 words)
cn: 市面上有了匹克威克外套和匹克威克雪茄，而那位丰满的、戴着眼镜的主人公塞缪尔·匹克威克，成了全国性的人物。

en: After Pickwick, Dickens plunged into a bleaker world. (8 words)
cn: 《匹克威克外传》之后，狄更斯投入了一个更为灰暗的世界。

en: In Oliver Twist, he traces an orphan's progress from the workhouse to the criminal slums of London. (17 words)
cn: 在《雾都孤儿》中，他追溯了一个孤儿从济贫院到伦敦犯罪贫民窟的人生历程。

en: Nicholas Nickleby, his next novel, combines the darkness of Oliver Twist with the sunlight of Pickwick. (16 words)
cn: 他的下一部小说《尼古拉斯·尼克尔贝》将《雾都孤儿》的黑暗与《匹克威克外传》的阳光结合在了一起。

en: The popularity of these novels consolidated Dickens as a nationally and internationally celebrated man of letters. (16 words)
cn: 这些小说的普及，巩固了狄更斯作为一位享誉国内外的文豪地位。

en: The history of the Eisenhower Executive Office Building (EEOB) began long before its foundations were laid. (16 words)
cn: 艾森豪威尔行政办公大楼的历史，远在其地基奠定之前就开始了。

en: The first executive offices were constructed between 1799 and 1820. (10 words)
cn: 第一批行政办公室建于1799年至1820年间。

en: A series of fires (including those set by the British in 1814) and overcrowded conditions led to the construction of the existing Treasury Building. (23 words)
cn: 一系列火灾（包括1814年英国军队纵火事件）以及过度拥挤的状况，导致了现存的财政部大楼的建造。

en: In 1866, the construction of the North Wing of the Treasury Building necessitated the demolition of the State Department building. (19 words)
cn: 1866年，财政部大楼北翼的建造使得国务院大楼的拆除成为必要。

en: The State, War, and Navy Building, as it was originally known, housed the three Executive Branch Departments most intimately associated with formulating and conducting the nation's foreign policy in the last quarter of the nineteenth century and the first quarter of the twentieth century—the period when the United States emerged as an international power. (48 words)
cn: 这座大楼最初被称为国务院、陆军部和海军部大楼，容纳了在19世纪最后25年和20世纪前25年——美国崛起为世界强国的时期——与制定和执行国家外交政策最密切相关的三个行政部门。

en: The building has housed some of the nation's most significant diplomats and politicians and has been the scene of many historic events. (20 words)
cn: 这座大楼曾容纳过美国历史上一些最重要的外交官和政治家，也是许多历史性事件的现场。

en: In December of 1869, Congress appointed a commission to select a site and prepare plans and cost estimates for a new State Department Building. (23 words)
cn: 1869年12月，国会任命了一个委员会，为新国务院大楼选址并制定方案和成本估算。

en: The commission was also to consider possible arrangements for the War and Navy Departments. (14 words)
cn: 该委员会还要考虑陆军部和海军部的可能安排。

en: To the horror of some who expected a Greek Revival twin of the Treasury Building to be erected on the other side of the White House, the elaborate French Second Empire style design by Alfred B. Mullett was selected, and construction of a building to house all three departments began in June of 1871. (48 words)
cn: 一些人原指望在白宫另一侧建造一座希腊复兴风格、与财政部大楼孪生的建筑，令他们惊愕的是，被选中的却是阿尔弗雷德·B·马利特的精美的法兰西第二帝国风格设计，一座容纳全部三个部门的大楼于1871年6月动工。

en: The Eisenhower Executive Office Building commands a unique position in both the national history and the architectural heritage of the United States. (21 words)
cn: 艾森豪威尔行政办公大楼在美国国家历史和建筑遗产中都占有独特的地位。

en: Designed by Supervising Architect of the Treasury, Alfred B. Mullett, it was built from 1871 to 1888 to house the growing staffs of the State, War, and Navy Departments, and is considered one of the best examples of French Second Empire architecture in the country. (42 words)
cn: 它由财政部监管建筑师阿尔弗雷德·B·马利特设计，于1871年至1888年间建造，用于容纳国务院、陆军部和海军部不断增长的职员，被认为是该国法兰西第二帝国建筑的最佳典范之一。

en: Construction took 17 years as the building slowly rose wing by wing. (12 words)
cn: 建造耗时17年，大楼一翼一翼地慢慢拔地而起。

en: When the EEOB was finished, it was the largest office building in Washington, with nearly 2 miles of black and white tiled corridors. (25 words)
cn: EEOB竣工时，它是华盛顿最大的办公楼，拥有近2英里的黑白瓷砖走廊。

en: Almost all of the interior detail is of cast iron or plaster; the use of wood was minimized to insure fire safety. (21 words)
cn: 几乎所有的内部装饰细节都是铸铁或石膏；尽量减少木材的使用，以确保防火安全。

en: Eight monumental curving staircases of granite with over 4,000 individually cast bronze balusters are capped by four skylight domes and two stained glass rotundas. (23 words)
cn: 八座宏伟的花岗岩弧形楼梯，配有4000多个独立铸造的青铜栏杆，其顶部是四个天窗穹顶和两个彩色玻璃圆形大厅。

en: Completed in 1875, the State Department's south wing was the first to be occupied, with its elegant four-story library (completed in 1876), Diplomatic Reception Room, and Secretary's office decorated with carved wood, Oriental rugs, and stenciled wall patterns. (33 words)
cn: 1875年完工的国务院南翼是最先入驻的，那里有典雅的四层图书馆（1876年完工）、外交接待室和用雕刻木头、东方地毯及模板印刷墙面图案装饰的国务卿办公室。

en: The Navy Department moved into the east wing in 1879, where elaborate wall and ceiling stenciling and marquetry floors decorated the office of the Secretary. (24 words)
cn: 海军部于1879年搬入东翼，那里精致的墙壁和天花板模板印刷以及镶嵌地板装饰着部长办公室。

en: Many of the most celebrated national figures have participated in historical events that have taken place within the EEOB's granite walls. (20 words)
cn: 许多最著名的全国性人物都曾在EEOB花岗岩墙壁内参与过历史事件。

en: Presidents Theodore Roosevelt, William Howard Taft, Dwight D. Eisenhower, Lyndon B. Johnson, Gerald Ford, and George H. W. Bush all had offices in this building before becoming President. (26 words)
cn: 西奥多·罗斯福、威廉·霍华德·塔夫脱、德怀特·D·艾森豪威尔、林登·B·约翰逊、杰拉尔德·福特和乔治·H·W·布什，这些总统在成为总统之前，都曾在这座大楼里拥有办公室。

en: It has housed 16 Secretaries of the Navy, 21 Secretaries of War, and 24 Secretaries of State. (16 words)
cn: 这里曾容纳过16位海军部长、21位陆军部长和24位国务卿。

en: Winston Churchill once walked its corridors and Japanese emissaries met here with Secretary of State Cordell Hull after the bombing of Attack on Pearl Harbor. (24 words)
cn: 温斯顿·丘吉尔曾走过这里的走廊，日本特使在珍珠港遭袭之后，曾在这里与国务卿科德尔·赫尔会面。

en: In his 1936 work How to Win Friends and Influence People, Dale Carnegie wrote: "There is only one way … to get the best of an argument—and that is to avoid it." (27 words)
cn: 戴尔·卡耐基在他1936年的著作《如何赢得朋友和影响他人》中写道：“只有一种方法……能在争论中取得最佳结果——那就是避开它。”

en: This aversion to arguments is common, but it depends on a mistaken view of arguments that causes profound problems for our personal and social lives—and in many ways misses the point of arguing in the first place. (36 words)
cn: 这种对争论的厌恶很常见，但它源于一种对争论的错误看法，这种看法给我们的个人和社会生活都带来了深刻的问题——并且在很多方面，从根本上就误解了争论的意义。

en: Carnegie would be right if arguments were fights, which is how we often think of them. (16 words)
cn: 如果争论就是打架，那么我们通常对争论的看法倒是对的，卡耐基也就是对的。

en: If you win a fight, your opponent loses. (9 words)
cn: 如果你打赢了一架，你的对手就输了。

en: In the end, one side may walk away feeling victorious while the other feels defeated, and both may end up no better off. (22 words)
cn: 到头来，一方可能带着胜利感离开，而另一方则感到挫败，并且双方最终可能都毫无收益。

en: Your prospects would be almost as dismal if arguments were even just competitions—like, say, tennis games. (16 words)
cn: 如果争论哪怕只是比赛——比如说，像网球赛——你的前景也同样几乎黯淡无光。

en: Pairs of opponents hit the ball back and forth until one winner emerges from all who entered. (17 words)
cn: 成对的对手来回击球，直到从所有参赛者中产生出一个胜者。

en: Everybody else loses. (3 words)
cn: 其他所有人都输了。

en: This kind of thinking is why so many people try to avoid arguments, especially about politics and religion. (18 words)
cn: 正是这种思维，导致那么多人试图回避争论，尤其是关于政治和宗教的争论。

en: These views of arguments also undermine reason. (7 words)
cn: 这些关于争论的看法也会破坏理性。

en: If you see a conversation as a fight or competition, you can win by cheating as long as you don't get caught. (21 words)
cn: 如果你把对话看成是打架或比赛，你就可以通过作弊来取胜，只要你别被抓住。

en: You will be happy to convince people with bad arguments. (10 words)
cn: 你会乐于用糟糕的论证去说服别人。

en: You can call their views stupid, or joke about how ignorant they are. (14 words)
cn: 你可以称他们的观点愚蠢，或者拿他们多么无知来开玩笑。

en: None of these tricks will help you understand them, their positions or the issues that divide you, but they can help you win—in one way. (26 words)
cn: 这些伎俩没有一个能帮助你去理解他们、他们的立场或者那些使你们产生分歧的问题，但它们能以某种方式帮你赢。

en: There is a better way to win arguments. (8 words)
cn: 有一种更好的方式来赢得争论。

en: Imagine that you favor increasing the minimum wage in our state, and I do not. (15 words)
cn: 想象一下，你赞成提高我们州的最低工资，而我不赞成。

en: If you yell, "Yes," and I yell, "No," neither of us learns anything. (13 words)
cn: 如果你高喊“同意”，而我高喊“反对”，我们俩都学不到任何东西。

en: We neither understand nor respect each other, and we have no basis for compromise or cooperation. (16 words)
cn: 我们既不了解也不尊重彼此，而且我们没有妥协或合作的基础。

en: In contrast, suppose you give a reasonable argument: that full-time workers should not have to live in poverty. (18 words)
cn: 相比之下，假设你给出一个合理的论证：全职工人不应该被迫生活在贫困中。

en: Then I counter with another reasonable argument: that a higher minimum wage will force businesses to employ fewer people for less time. (21 words)
cn: 然后我用另一个合理的论证来反驳：提高最低工资会迫使企业减少雇佣人数、缩短工时。

en: Now we can understand each other's positions and recognize our shared values, since we both care about needy workers. (18 words)
cn: 现在我们能理解彼此的立场，并且认识到我们共同的价值观，因为我们都在乎那些贫困的工人。

en: Of course, many discussions are not so successful. (8 words)
cn: 当然，许多讨论并非如此成功。

en: Still, we need to be careful not to accuse opponents of bad arguments too quickly. (15 words)
cn: 尽管如此，我们仍需当心，不要过快地指责对手论证糟糕。

en: We need to learn how to evaluate them properly. (9 words)
cn: 我们需要学会如何恰当地评价它们。

en: A large part of evaluation is calling out bad arguments, but we also need to admit good arguments by opponents and to apply the same critical standards to ourselves. (27 words)
cn: 评价的很大一部分是要指出糟糕的论证，但我们也需要承认对手给出的好论证，并把同样的评判标准用在自己身上。

en: Humility requires you to recognize weakness in your own arguments and sometimes also to accept reasons on the opposite side. (19 words)
cn: 谦逊要求你认识到自己论证中的弱点，有时也要接受对方的理由。

en: None of this will be easy, but you can start even if others refuse to. (15 words)
cn: 这一切都不容易，但即使别人拒绝，你也可以开始。

en: Next time you state your position, formulate an argument for what you claim and honestly ask yourself whether your argument is any good. (23 words)
cn: 下次你陈述立场时，为你的主张構建一个论证，并诚实地问问自己，你的论证是否有可取之处。

en: Next time you talk with someone who takes a stand, ask them to give you a reason for their view. (20 words)
cn: 下次你与持某个立场的人交谈时，请他们为其观点给出一个理由。

en: Spell out their argument fully and charitably. (7 words)
cn: 完整而宽厚地阐发他们的论证。

en: Assess its strength impartially. (4 words)
cn: 不偏不倚地评估其力度。

en: Raise objections and listen carefully to their replies. (9 words)
cn: 提出异议，并仔细倾听他们的回答。

en: These tools can help you win every argument—not in the unhelpful sense of beating your opponents but in the better sense of learning about the issues that divide people, learning why they disagree with us and learning to talk and work together with them. (42 words)
cn: 这些工具能帮助你赢得每一场争论——不是贏在那种无益的击败对手的意义上，而是赢在更好的意义上：了解那些使人们产生分歧的问题，了解他们为什么不同意我们，并学会与他们交谈和共事。

en: If we readjust our view of arguments—from a verbal fight or tennis game to a reasoned exchange through which we all gain mutual respect and understanding—then we change the very nature of what it means to win an argument. (39 words)
cn: 如果我们調整对争论的看法——从一场口舌之争或网球赛，转变为一种我们都能借此获得相互尊重和理解的理性交流——那么我们就能改变赢得一场争论的本质意义。

en: Eye contact can be a friendly social signal. (8 words)
cn: 眼神交流可以是一种友好的社交信号。

en: We know that a typical infant will instinctively gaze into its mother's eyes, and she will look back. (18 words)
cn: 我们知道，一个典型的婴儿会本能地凝视母亲的眼睛，而母亲也会回望。

en: This mutual gaze is a major part of the attachment between mother and child. (15 words)
cn: 这种相互凝视是母子之间情感依恋的主要部分。

en: In adulthood, looking at someone else in a pleasant way can be a complimentary sign of paying attention. (18 words)
cn: 成年后，以愉快的方式看着别人，可以是一种表示关注的赞赏性信号。

en: It can catch someone's attention in a crowded room. (9 words)
cn: 在拥挤的房间里，它能吸引别人的注意。

en: "Eye contact and smiles" can signal availability and confidence, a common-sense notion supported in studies by psychologist Monica Moore. (18 words)
cn: “眼神交流和微笑”能示意出友善和自信，这是一个常识性的观念，已得到心理学家莫妮卡·穆尔的研究支持。

en: Biological factors behind eye contact are being investigated. (8 words)
cn: 眼神交流背后的生物学因素正在被研究。

en: Neuroscientist Bonnie Auyeung found that the hormone oxytocin increased the amount of eye contact from men toward the interviewer during a brief interview when the direction of their gaze was recorded. (29 words)
cn: 神经科学家邦妮·欧阳发现，在一次简短的、视线方向被记录下来的访谈中，催产素这种荷尔蒙增加了男性对访谈者的目光接触量。

en: This was also found in high-functioning men with some autistic spectrum symptoms, who may tend to avoid eye contact. (18 words)
cn: 这在一些可能倾向于回避目光接觸、有某些自闭症谱系症狀的高功能男性身上也得到了证实。

en: Specific brain regions that respond during direct gaze are being explored by other researchers, using advanced methods of brain scanning. (18 words)
cn: 其他研究人员正在使用先进的脑部扫描方法，探索在直接凝视时作出反应的具体大脑区域。

en: Eye contact can be aggressive. (5 words)
cn: 眼神交流可以是挑衅性的。

en: With the use of eye-tracking technology, Julia Minson of the Harvard Kennedy School of Government concluded that eye contact can signal very different kinds of messages, depending on the situation. (29 words)
cn: 哈佛大学肯尼迪政府学院的朱莉娅·明森利用眼动追踪技术得出结论，目光接触可以根据情境传递出非常不同的信息。

en: While eye contact may be a sign of connection or trust in friendly situations, it's more likely to be associated with dominance or intimidation in adversarial situations. (26 words)
cn: 在友好的情境中，目光接触可能是联系或信任的标志，而在敌对情境中，它更可能与支配或威吓相关联。

en: "Whether you're a politician or a parent, it might be helpful to keep in mind that trying to maintain eye contact may backfire if you're trying to convince someone who has a different set of beliefs than you," said Minson. (38 words)
cn: 明森说：“无论你是政治家还是家长，都不妨记住：如果你试图说服一个与你持不同信念的人，保持目光接触可能会适得其反。”

en: Eye fixations are brief. (4 words)
cn: 目光注视是短暂的。

en: When we look at a face or a picture, our eyes pause on one spot at a time, often on the eyes or mouth. (22 words)
cn: 当我们看一张脸或一幅画时，我们的眼睛一次只停留在一点上，常常是在眼睛或嘴巴上。

en: These pauses typically occur at about three per second, and the eyes then jump to another spot, until several important points in the image are registered like a series of snapshots. (32 words)
cn: 这些停顿通常大约每秒发生三次，然后眼睛会跳到另一个点，直到图像中的几个重要点像一系列快照一样被记录下来。

en: How the whole image is then assembled and perceived is still a mystery although it is the subject of current research. (21 words)
cn: 整个图像随后是如何被组装和感知的，尽管是当前研究的课题，但依然是个谜。

en: Personality can affect how a person reacts to eye contact. (10 words)
cn: 性格会影响一个人对目光接触的反应方式。

en: In people who score high in a test of neuroticism, a personality dimension associated with negative emotions and instability, direct eye contact triggered more activity in brain regions linked with self-consciousness and social avoidance, according to the Finnish researcher Jari Hietanen and colleagues. (39 words)
cn: 根据芬兰研究员雅里·希耶塔宁及其同事的研究，在神经质测试中得分高的人——神经质是一种与负面情绪和不稳定性相关的人格维度——其直接目光接触会触发与自我意识和社会回避相关的大脑区域更活跃。

en: "Our findings indicate that people do not only feel different when they are the centre of attention but that their brain reactions also differ." (24 words)
cn: “我们的研究结果表明，人们不仅在被关注时感受不同，而且他们的大脑反应也不同。”

en: A more direct finding is that people who scored high for negative emotions like anxiety looked at others for shorter periods of time and reported more comfortable feelings when others did not look directly at them. (33 words)
cn: 一个更直接的发现是，在焦虑等负面情绪上得分高的人，看别人的时间更短，并且报告说，当别人不直视他们时，他们感觉更自在。

en: Companies are using artificial intelligence to remove some of the unconscious bias from hiring decisions. (14 words)
cn: 企业正在利用人工智能来消除招聘决策中的一些无意识偏见。
