en: "There are experiments that show that, naturally, the results of interviews are much more biased than what AI does," says Pedro Domingos, author of The Master Algorithm and a computer science professor at the University of Washington. (34 words)
cn: 《终极算法》作者、华盛顿大学计算机科学教授佩德罗·多明戈斯说：“有实验表明，自然地，面试的结果比人工智能所做的结果更具偏见性。”

en: "AI looks at résumés in greater numbers than humans would be able to, and selects the more promising candidates." (19 words)
cn: “人工智能能比人类看得多得多的简历，并筛选出更有前途的候选人。”

en: One company that's doing this is called Blendoor. (8 words)
cn: 一家正在这样做的公司叫 Blendoor。

en: It uses analytics to help identify where there may be bias in the hiring process. (15 words)
cn: 它运用分析技术来帮助识别招聘过程中可能存在偏见的地方。

en: More effective marketing. (3 words)
cn: 更有效的市场营销。

en: Some AI software can analyze and optimize marketing email subject lines to increase open rates. (14 words)
cn: 一些人工智能软件可以分析和优化营销邮件标题，以提高打开率。

en: One company in the UK, Phrasee, claims their software can outperform humans by up to 10 percent when it comes to email open rates. (24 words)
cn: 英国一家名为 Phrasee 的公司声称，在邮件打开率方面，其软件的表现最多能比人类好10%。

en: This can mean millions more in revenue. (8 words)
cn: 这可能意味着成百上千万的额外收入。

en: There are also companies like Acquisio, which analyzes advertising performance across multiple channels like Adwords, Bing and social media and makes adjustments or suggestions about where advertising funds will yield best results. (32 words)
cn: 还有像 Acquisio 这样的公司，它分析广告在 Adwords、必应和社交媒体等多个渠道上的表现，并就广告资金投向何处會產生最佳效果做出调整或建议。

en: These are "tools that help people use data, not a replacement for people," says Patrick H. Winston. (15 words)
cn: 帕特里克·H·温斯顿说，这些是“帮助人们使用数据的工具，而不是人的替代品”。

en: Saving customers money. (3 words)
cn: 为客户省钱。

en: Energy companies can use AI to help customers reduce their electricity bills, saving them money while helping the environment. (19 words)
cn: 能源公司可以利用人工智能帮助客户减少电费账单，为他们省钱，同时有益于环境。

en: Companies can also optimize their own energy use and cut down on the cost of electricity. (16 words)
cn: 企业也可以优化自身的能源使用，降低电力成本。

en: Insurance companies, meanwhile, can base their premiums on AI models that more accurately assess risk. (15 words)
cn: 与此同时，保险公司可以将保费建立在更精确评估风险的人工智能模型之上。

en: Domingos says, "Before, they might not insure the ones who felt like a high risk or charge them too much, or they would charge them too little and then it would cost the company money." (32 words)
cn: 多明戈斯说：“以前，他们可能不给那些被认为风险高的人投保，或者向他们收取太高的费用，要么就收取太低的费用，这反倒会让公司赔钱。”

en: Improved accuracy. (2 words)
cn: 提高准确性。

en: "Machine learning often provides a more reliable form of statistics which makes data more valuable," says Winston. (17 words)
cn: 温斯顿说：“机器学习常常提供一种更可靠的统计形式，这使得数据更有价值。”

en: It "helps people make smarter decisions." (6 words)
cn: 它“帮助人们做出更明智的决策”。

en: Some AI systems can review legal contracts and point out missing clauses or mistakes. (14 words)
cn: 一些人工智能系统可以审查法律合同，并指出缺失的条款或错误。

en: This process, along with employees reviewing the contracts, is faster and more accurate. (13 words)
cn: 这一过程，连同员工复查合同，会更快、更准确。

en: Protecting and maintaining infrastructure. (4 words)
cn: 保护与维护基础设施。

en: A number of companies, particularly in energy and transportation, use AI image processing technology to inspect infrastructure and prevent equipment failure or leaks before they happen. (27 words)
cn: 许多公司，尤其是能源和交通领域的公司，使用人工智能图像处理技术来检查基础设施，并在设备故障或泄漏发生之前加以预防。

en: "If they fail first and then you fix them, it's very expensive," says Domingos. (15 words)
cn: 多明戈斯说：“如果它们先壞了，然后你再来修，那代价太高了。”

en: "You want to predict if something needs attention now and point to where it's useful for employees to go to." (20 words)
cn: “你要预测某些东西现在是否需要关注，并指出員工去哪些地方是有用的。”

en: I was a zoo and wildlife park employee for years. (11 words)
cn: 我曾多年是动物园和野生动物园的员工。

en: Both the wildlife park and zoo claimed to be operating for the benefit of the animals and for conservation purposes. (20 words)
cn: 野生动物园和动物园都声称是为了动物福利和动物保护的目的而运营。

en: This claim was false. (4 words)
cn: 这种说辭是假的。

en: Neither one of them actually participated in any contributions to animal research or conservation. (15 words)
cn: 它们没有一个真正參與过对动物研究或动物保护的任何贡献。

en: They are profitable institutions whose bottom line is much more important than the condition of the animals. (18 words)
cn: 它们是盈利机构，其盈亏底线远比动物的状况更重要。

en: Animals despise being captives in zoos. (7 words)
cn: 动物厌恶被关在动物园里。

en: No matter how you "enhance" enclosures, they do not allow for freedom, a natural diet or adequate exercise. (17 words)
cn: 不管你如何“改善”围舍，它们都不会提供自由、天然饮食或足够的运动。

en: Animals end up stressed and unhealthy or dead. (8 words)
cn: 动物最终会应激、不健康或死亡。

en: It's past time for transparency with these institutions, and it's past time to eliminate zoos from our culture. (18 words)
cn: 是时候让这些机构保持透明了，也是时候从我们的文化中消除动物园了。

en: Zoos should have been closed down as they prioritize moneymaking over animals' well-being. (13 words)
cn: 动物园本应被关闭，因为它们把赚钱置于动物的安康之上。

en: As a zoology professor, I agree with Emma Marris that zoo displays can be sad and cruel. (17 words)
cn: 作为一名动物学教授，我同意艾玛·马里斯关于动物园展览可能悲伤而残忍的看法。

en: But she underestimates the educational value of zoos. (8 words)
cn: 但她低估了动物园的教育价值。

en: The zoology program at my university attracts students for whom zoo visits were the crucial formative experience that led them to major in biological sciences. (23 words)
cn: 我所在大学的动物学专业吸引的学生，正是那些把参观动物园作为关键启蒙经历，从而主修生物科学的人。

en: These are mostly students who had no opportunity as children to travel to wilderness areas, wildlife refuges or national parks. (19 words)
cn: 这些学生大多在孩童时没有机会去荒野地区、野生动物保护区或国家公园旅行。

en: Although good TV shows can help stir children's interest in conservation, they are no substitute for a firsthand experience. (19 words)
cn: 尽管好的电视节目有助于激起孩子们对自然保護的兴趣，但它们不能替代第一手体验。

en: Surely there must be some middle ground that balances zoos' treatment of animals with their educational potential. (17 words)
cn: 一定存在某种中间地带，能在动物园对待动物的方式与其教育潜力之间取得平衡。

en: While animals in captivity deserve sympathy, zoos play a significant role in starting young people down the path of related sciences. (21 words)
cn: 虽然圈养动物值得同情，但动物园在引导年輕人走上相关科学道路上，扮演着重要角色。

en: Emma Marris's article is an insult and a disservice to the thousands of passionate, dedicated people who work tirelessly to improve the lives of animals and protect our planet. (28 words)
cn: 艾玛·马里斯的那篇文章，是对成千上万热情、敬业、不懈努力改善动物生活和保护我们地球的人们的侮辱和伤害。

en: She uses outdated research and decades-old examples to undermine the noble mission of organizations committed to connecting children to a world beyond their own. (22 words)
cn: 她用过时的研究和几十年前的例子来破坏那些致力于将儿童与超越自身世界联结起来的组织的崇高使命。

en: Zoos are at the forefront of conservation and constantly evolving to improve how they care for animals and protect each species in its natural habitat. (24 words)
cn: 动物园处在动物保護的前沿，并不断進化，以改善他们照顾动物的方式，并在其自然栖息地保護每一个物种。

en: Are there tragedies? (3 words)
cn: 存在悲剧吗？

en: Of course. (2 words)
cn: 当然存在。

en: But they are the exception, not the norm that Ms. Marris implies. (12 words)
cn: 但它们是例外，而非马里斯女士所暗示的常态。

en: A distressed animal in a zoo will get as good or better treatment than most of us at our local hospital. (20 words)
cn: 动物园里一只悲伤痛苦的动物，会得到跟我们大多数人在当地医院一样好，甚至更好的治疗。

en: Zoos, which spare no effort to take care of animals, should not be subjected to unfair criticism. (17 words)
cn: 动物园不遗余力地照顾动物，不应受到不公正的批评。

en: As a fellow environmentalist, animal-protection advocate and longtime vegetarian, I could properly be in the same camp as Emma Marris on the issue of zoos. (24 words)
cn: 作为一位同为环境主义者、动物保护倡导者和长期素食者，在动物园问题上，我本可以恰当地和艾玛·马里斯站在同一阵线。

en: But I believe that well-run zoos, and the heroic animals that suffer their captivity, do serve a higher purpose. (18 words)
cn: 但我相信，运营良好的动物园，以及那些承受着圈养之苦的英勇动物们，确实服务于一个更高的目标。

en: Were it not for opportunities to observe these beautiful, wild creatures close to home, many more people would be driven by their fascination to travel to wild areas to seek out, disturb and even hunt them down. (35 words)
cn: 如果没有在家门口观赏这些美丽野兽的机会，会有更多的人受着迷之心的驱使，前往野外地区去寻找、打扰，甚至猎杀它们。

en: Zoos are, in that sense, similar to natural history and archaeology museums, serving to satisfy our need for contact with these living creatures while leaving the vast majority undisturbed in their natural environments. (31 words)
cn: 从这个意义上说，动物园类似于自然历史博物馆和考古博物馆，在满足我们接触这些生灵的需要的同时，让绝大多数动植物在其自然环境中不受打扰。

en: Zoos save people trips to wilderness areas and thus contribute to wildlife conservation. (13 words)
cn: 动物园省去了人们前往荒野地区的旅行，从而有助于野生动物保護。

en: Emma Marris selectively describes and misrepresents the findings of our research. (10 words)
cn: 艾玛·马里斯有选择地描述并歪曲了我们研究的发现。

en: Our studies focused on the impact of zoo experiences on how people think about themselves and nature, and the data points extracted from our studies do not, in any way, discount what is learned in a zoo visit. (36 words)
cn: 我们的研究聚焦于动物园体验对人们如何看待自身和自然的影响，而从我们研究中提取的数据点，丝毫不贬低在动物园参观中所学到的东西。

en: Zoos are tools for thinking. (5 words)
cn: 动物园是思考的工具。

en: Our research provides strong support for the value of zoos in connecting people with animals and with nature. (19 words)
cn: 我们的研究为动物园在联结人与动物、人与自然方面的价值提供了强有力的支持。

en: Zoos provide a critical voice for conservation and environmental protection. (10 words)
cn: 动物园为自然保护与环境保护发出关键的声音。

en: They afford an opportunity for people from all backgrounds to encounter a range of animals, from drone bees to springbok or salmon, to better understand the natural world we live in. (31 words)
cn: 它们为来自各种背景的人们提供了一个机会，去接触从雄蜂到跳羚或鲑鱼等一系列动物，以更好地理解我们所生活的自然世界。

en: Marris distorts our findings, which actually prove that zoos serve as an indispensable link between humans and nature. (17 words)
cn: 马里斯曲解了我们的发现，其實这些发现恰恰证明，动物园是人与自然之间不可或缺的纽带。

en: Two centuries ago, the idea of preserving nature, rather than exploiting it, was a novel one to many US settlers. (20 words)
cn: 两个世纪前，保护自然而非开发自然的想法，对许多美国定居者来说还是一个新颖的观念。

en: One of the turning points in public support for land conservation efforts—and recognizing the magnificence of the Yellowstone National Park region in particular—came in the form of vivid photographs. (28 words)
cn: 公众对土地保护努力支持的一个转折点——特别是对黄石国家公园地区壮丽景色的认可——是以生动照片的形式出现的。

en: Though Native Americans (and later miners and fur trappers) had long recognized the area's riches, most Americans did not. (18 words)
cn: 尽管美洲原住民（以及后来的矿工和毛皮猎人）早已认识到该地区的丰富资源，但大多数美国人并未认识到。

en: That's why Ferdinand V. Hayden's expedition aimed to produce a fuller understanding of the Yellowstone River region, from its hot springs and waterfalls to its variety of flora and fauna. (30 words)
cn: 这就是为什么费迪南德·V·海登的探险队旨在更全面地了解黄石河地区，从其温泉和瀑布到其丰富多样的动植物群。

en: In addition to the entourage of scientists, the team also included artists: painter Thomas Moran and photographer William Henry Jackson were charged with capturing this astounding natural beauty and sharing it with the world. (32 words)
cn: 除了随行的科学家之外，该团队还包括艺术家：画家托马斯·莫兰和摄影师威廉·亨利·杰克逊负责捕捉这令人惊叹的自然美景，并将其分享给全世界。

en: Last year marked the 150th anniversary of a series of Yellowstone photographs by the renowned landscape photographer William Henry Jackson. (18 words)
cn: 去年是著名风景摄影师威廉·亨利·杰克逊一系列黄石照片问世的150周年。

en: Jackson snapped the first-ever shots of iconic landmarks such as the Tetons, Old Faithful and the Colorado Rockies on a late 19th-century expedition through the Yellowstone Basin, conducted by Hayden. (29 words)
cn: 杰克逊在19世纪末由海登领导的穿越黄石盆地的探险中，首次拍摄到了诸如蒂顿山脉、老忠实泉和科罗拉多落基山脉等标志性地标的照片。

en: The team included a meteorologist, a zoologist, a mineralogist, and an agricultural statistician. (11 words)
cn: 该团队包括一名气象学家、一名动物学家、一名矿物学家和一名农业统计员。

en: The journey officially began in Ogden on June 8, 1871. (11 words)
cn: 这次旅程于1871年6月8日在奥格登正式开启。

en: Over nearly four months, dozens of men made their way on horseback into Montana and traversed along the Yellowstone River and around Yellowstone Lake. (25 words)
cn: 在近四个月的时间里，几十个人骑着马进入蒙大拿州，沿黄石河并绕黄石湖跋涉前行。

en: That fall, they concluded the survey in Fort Bridger. (9 words)
cn: 那年秋天，他们在布里杰堡结束了这次勘测。

en: Throughout the trip, Jackson juggled multiple cameras and plate sizes using the collodion process that involved coating the plates with a chemical mixture, exposing them and developing the resulting images with a portable darkroom. (32 words)
cn: 在整个旅程中，杰克逊使用火棉胶湿版工艺操作多台相机和不同尺寸的底片，该工艺涉及用药剂涂布底片、曝光以及在便携式暗房中冲洗生成的图像。

en: The crude technique required heavy equipment, and several men had to assist in its transportation. (15 words)
cn: 这种粗糙的技术需要笨重的设备，且需要好几个人协助运输。

en: Despite these challenges, Jackson captured dozens of striking photos, ranging from majestic images like his now-famous snapshot of Old Faithful, to casual portraits of expedition members at the camp. (27 words)
cn: 尽管面临这些挑战，杰克逊还是拍下了几十张引人注目的照片，从如今闻名遐迩的老忠实泉壮观图像，到探险队成员在营地轻松随意的肖像照。

en: While veterans of previous expeditions had written at length about stunning sights, these vivid photographs were another thing entirely. (18 words)
cn: 虽然以前探险队的老队员们曾详尽地描写过那些令人惊叹的景观，但这些生动的照片则完全是另一回事。

en: Perhaps most importantly, these images provided documentary evidence that later made a powerful case for preserving Yellowstone. (17 words)
cn: 也许最重要的是，这些影像提供了记录性证据，后来为保护黄石提供了有力的理由。

en: Hayden incorporated the team's observations into an extensive report aimed at convincing senators and representatives, along with colleagues at government agencies like the Department of the Interior, that Yellowstone ought to be preserved. (31 words)
cn: 海登将团队的观察结果融入了一份详尽的报告中，旨在说服参议员和众议员，以及内政部等政府机构的同僚，黄石应当受到保护。

en: An effective Washington operator, Hayden sensed that he could capitalize on the expedition's stunning visuals. (15 words)
cn: 作为一个有效的华盛顿运作者，海登意识到他可以利用这次探险令人惊叹的视觉资料。

en: He asked Jackson to print out large copies and distributed them, along with reproductions of Moran's paintings, to each member of Congress. (20 words)
cn: 他让杰克逊打印出大尺寸的副本，并将它们与莫兰画作的复制品一起，分发给国会的每位议员。

en: "The visualization, particularly those photographs, really hit home that this is something that has to be protected," says Alicia Murphy, Yellowstone's park historian. (22 words)
cn: 黄石公园历史学家艾丽西亚·墨菲说：“视觉化，尤其是那些照片，真正使人深切地认识到，这是必须保护的东西。”

en: The bill proved largely popular and sailed through Congress with large majorities in favor. (15 words)
cn: 该法案被证明广受欢迎，并以巨大优势在国会顺利通过。

en: In quick succession, the Senate and House passed legislation protecting Yellowstone in early 1872. (14 words)
cn: 参众两院紧跟着在1872年初通过了保护黄石的立法。

en: That March, President Ulysses S. Grant signed an act into law that established Yellowstone as the world's first national park. (19 words)
cn: 那年三月，尤利西斯·S·格兰特总统签署了一项法案，将黄石建立为世界上第一个国家公园。

en: While some locals opposed the designation, the decision was largely accepted—and Jackson's photos played a key role in the fight to protect the area. (25 words)
cn: 虽然一些当地人反对这一认定，但这一决定在很大程度上被接受了——而杰克逊的照片在保护该地区的斗争中发挥了关键作用。

en: Simply, there are people in Nigeria who cannot travel to the Smithsonian Institution to see that part of their history and culture represented by the Benin Bronzes. (26 words)
cn: 很简单，尼日利亚有人无法前往史密森尼学会，去看由贝宁青铜器所代表的他们历史与文化的那一部分。

en: These should be available to them as part of their cultural heritage and history and as a source of national pride. (22 words)
cn: 这些应当作为他们文化遗产与历史的一部分，以及民族自豪感的源泉，让他们触手可及。

en: There is no good reason that these artifacts should be beyond the ordinary reach of the educational objectives or inspiration of the generations to which they were left. (27 words)
cn: 没有充分理由让这些工艺品置于它们所留传世代的教育目标或启发作用所能触及的寻常范围之外。

en: They serve no purpose in a museum in the United States or elsewhere except as curious objects. (17 words)
cn: 它们在美国或别处的博物馆里，除了作为猎奇物件外，毫无用处。

en: They cannot be compared to works of art produced for sale which can be passed from hand to hand and place to place by purchase. (25 words)
cn: 它们不能与为出售而創作的、可通过购买而转手易地的艺术品相提并论。

en: The real value of artifacts can only be recognized in their countries of origin rather than anywhere else. (18 words)
cn: 工艺品的真正价值只能在其原产国得到承认，而非其他任何地方。

en: We know very exact reproductions of artwork can be and are regularly produced. (14 words)
cn: 我们知道，非常精确的艺术复制品是能够、并且也常被定期制作出来的。

en: Perhaps museums and governments might explore some role for the use of nearly exact reproductions as a means of resolving issues relating to returning works of art and antiquities. (29 words)
cn: 或许博物馆和政府可以探索一下用几乎一模一样的复制品，作为解决与返还艺术品和文物相关的问题的一种方式。

en: The context of any exhibit is more important to me than whether the object being displayed is 2,000 years old or 2 months old. (23 words)
cn: 对我而言，任何展览的背景都比展出的物件是2000年之久还是2个月之久更重要。

en: In many cases the experts have a hard time agreeing on what is the real object and what is a forgery. (20 words)
cn: 在许多情况下，专家们很难就哪个是真品、哪个是赝品达成一致。

en: Again, the story an exhibit is trying to tell is what matters. (13 words)
cn: 再说一次，要紧的是展览想要讲述的故事。

en: The monetary value of the objects on display is a distant second place in importance. (16 words)
cn: 展品的货币价值在重要性上远居次位。

en: Museum visitors can still learn as much from artifacts copies after the originals are returned. (15 words)
cn: 原件归还后，博物馆参观者仍能从工艺品的复制品中学到同样多的东西。

en: When visiting the Baltimore Museum of Art, I came across a magnificent 15th-century Chinese sculpture. (15 words)
cn: 参观巴尔的摩艺术博物馆时，我碰到了一件精美的15世纪中国雕塑。

en: It inspired me to learn more about the culture that it represented. (13 words)
cn: 它激励我去更多地了解它所代表的文化。

en: Artifacts in museums have the power to inspire, and perhaps spark that need to learn and understand the nature of their creators. (21 words)
cn: 博物馆里的工艺品有能力启发灵感，或许还能激发那种去学习和理解其创造者本质的需要。

en: Having said that, I do feel that whatever artifacts find their way to public museums should, in fact, be sanctioned as having been obtained on loan, legally purchased, or obtained by treaty. (30 words)
cn: 话虽如此，我确实觉得，任何进入公共博物馆的工艺品，实际上都应被认可为是通过借用、合法购买或根据条约获得的。

en: Stealing artifacts from other peoples' cultures is obscene; it robs not only the physical objects, but the dignity and spirit of their creators. (22 words)
cn: 从其他民族的文化中窃取文物是可耻的；它掠夺的不仅是实物，还有其创造者的尊严和精神。

en: Ways to get artifacts from other countries must be decent and lawful. (12 words)
cn: 从其他国家获取工艺品的途径必须是体面且合法的。

en: Ancient art that is displayed in foreign countries by all means should be returned to the original country. (19 words)
cn: 在外国展出的古代艺术品，无论如何都应归还给原属国。

en: The foreign countries have no right to hold back returning the items. (13 words)
cn: 外国无权扣留不归还这些物品。

en: I would ask that the foreign nations and the original country discuss the terms of transfer. (16 words)
cn: 我会要求外国与原属国讨论轉讓的条件。

en: Yes, there is the risk that the original will not have as good security as do the foreign-countries. (18 words)
cn: 是的，存在原属国没有外国那样好的安保条件的风险。

en: But look at what happened to Boston's Gardner Museum theft in 1990, including the loss of Rembrandt Vermeer, Manet and other masterpieces. (21 words)
cn: 但看看1990年波士顿加德纳博物馆的盗窃案吧，包括伦勃朗、维米尔、马奈及其他杰作的遗失。

en: Nothing is absolutely safe, nowhere, and now Climate Change agitators are attacking publicly displayed work in European museum. (18 words)
cn: 没有什么、也没有哪里是绝对安全的，如今气候变迁煽动者还在攻击欧洲博物馆公开展出的作品。

en: Concern over security is no excuse for refusing to return the artifacts to their countries of origin. (18 words)
cn: 对安保的担忧，不能成为拒绝将工艺品归还原属国的借口。

en: To those of you in the comments section who are having strong feelings about artifacts being removed from cities in the US and Britain and returned to their countries of origin. (31 words)
cn: 致评论区那些对工艺品正从美英城市被移走并归还原属国反应强烈的你们。

en: I would ask you to consider: why do you think Americans have more of a right to easily access the Benin Bronzes than people of Nigeria? (25 words)
cn: 我想请你们考虑一下：为什么你们认为美国人比尼日利亚人，更有权轻松接触到贝宁青铜器？

en: Why are people who live within a day's drive of London entitled to go and see the Elgin Marbles wherever they want, but the people of Athens aren't? (28 words)
cn: 为什么住在距伦敦一天车程范围内的人，就有权随时随地去看埃尔金大理石雕塑，而雅典人却不行？

en: What intrinsic factors make the West a suitable home for these artifacts but preclude them from being preserved and displayed by their countries of origin? (24 words)
cn: 是什么内在因素，使西方成为这些工艺品合适的家园，却阻碍了它们由其原属国保存和展示？

en: If your conclusion is based on the idea that Western institutions are better equipped to preserve these artifacts, then perhaps you should reconsider whether that assumption is actually fair or even true. (30 words)
cn: 如果你的结论基于西方机构更有能力保存这些工艺品这一想法，那么也许你应当重新考虑，这一假定是否真公允，甚至是否属实。

en: It's a flawed line of reasoning to argue against returning artifacts to their countries of origin. (17 words)
cn: 反对将工艺品归还原属国，是一个有缺陷的推理逻辑。

en: Five years ago, at summer's end, Andrew Fusek Peters was diagnosed with bowel cancer. (13 words)
cn: 五年前，夏末时分，安德鲁·富塞克·彼得斯被诊断出患有肠癌。

en: "I was waiting for surgery, feeling really ill, sitting in my garden. It was amazing weather and there were painted lady butterflies everywhere," he says. (24 words)
cn: 他说：“我在等待手术，感觉病得很重，坐在自家花园里。天气好极了，到处都有小红蛱蝶。”

en: "They were a symbol of fragile life, of hope and defiance, and something appealed to my soul." (19 words)
cn: “它们是脆弱生命的象征，是希望与反抗的象征，有某种东西触动了我心深处。”

en: A children's author and poet who had become a keen amateur photographer, Peters watched the butterflies and idly wondered if he could capture them in flight. (26 words)
cn: 作为一名已成为热衷于业余摄影师的儿童作家兼诗人，彼得斯看着这些蝴蝶，心中泛起遐想，不知能否拍到它们飞行时的样子。

en: It swiftly became an obsession as he recovered from a successful operation to remove the cancer. (17 words)
cn: 当他从一次成功的癌症切除手术中康复时，这很快变成了一种痴迷。

en: In recent summers, he has travelled the length and breadth of United Kingdom to photograph all 58 native species of butterfly. (21 words)
cn: 在近年的夏天，他跑遍了全英国，去拍摄所有58种原生蝴蝶。

en: Now the fruits of those summers have been published in a beautiful new book. (14 words)
cn: 如今，那些夏天的成果已在一本精美的新书中出版。

en: Britain has relatively few butterfly species compared with mainland Europe and 80% are in decline, mostly because intensive chemical farming has reduced many species to tiny fragments of habitat and small nature reserves. (32 words)
cn: 与欧洲大陆相比，英国的蝴蝶物种相对较少，其中80%数量正在下降，主要是因为集约化化学农业已将许多物种逼至栖息地碎片化和小型自然保护区的地步。

en: Global heating is benefiting some species but others are too isolated to find suitable new habitat, and gardening habits—paving over gardens and using pesticides—aren't helping either. (26 words)
cn: 全球变暖正使一些物种受益，但其他物种因太过孤立而无法找到适宜的新栖息地，而园艺习惯——花园铺装地面和使用杀虫剂——也无济于事。

en: Butterflies may not pollinate as many plants as wild bees and hoverflies, but because British butterflies are the best-studied insects in the world, they are a key indicator species for the overall health of the environment and wider declines in flying insects. (39 words)
cn: 蝴蝶可能不如野生蜜蜂和食蚜蝇那样为许多植物授粉，但由于英国蝴蝶是世界上研究得最透彻的昆虫，它们是衡量环境整体健康和飛行昆虫更广泛衰退的一项关键指标物种。

en: Peters' signature shot is a butterfly "take-off", showing the multiple wing-beats of one butterfly in one frame as it lifts off from a flower. (23 words)
cn: 彼得斯的招牌镜头是蝴蝶“起飞”，在同一个画面中展示一只蝴蝶从花上腾空而起时多次扑动翅膀的样态。

en: How does he capture it? (5 words)
cn: 他是如何抓拍到它的呢？

en: Technology helps. (2 words)
cn: 技术帮了忙。

en: A typical digital SLR camera shoots 20 frames a second. (10 words)
cn: 一台典型的数码单反相机每秒可拍20帧。

en: He uses a high-speed OM System which shoots 120 frames a second. (13 words)
cn: 他使用的是高速OM系统，每秒可拍摄120帧。

en: A butterfly takes off so quickly it is still impossible to react quickly enough to capture that take-off but if he half-presses the shutter, the camera saves the 70 previous frames before the shutter is fully pressed. (37 words)
cn: 蝴蝶起飞如此之快，仍不可能足够快地反应过来捕捉到那起飞瞬间，但如果他半按快门，相机就会保存在快门完全按下之前的那70帧。

en: "It's like being able to go back in time and catch the take-off," he says. (16 words)
cn: 他说：“这就像能够回到过去，抓到起飞瞬间一样。”

en: After he's captured the butterfly taking off, he layers 10 to 15 frames together in Adobe Photoshop. (17 words)
cn: 在他捕捉到蝴蝶起飞后，他会把10到15帧在Adobe Photoshop中叠加到一起。

en: That makes it sound easy, and artificial, but Peters insists it is still a massive challenge. (17 words)
cn: 那使它听起来既简单又作假，但彼得斯坚称这仍是一个巨大的挑战。

en: He typically takes between 10,000 and 20,000 shots to get one butterfly take-off sequence in focus. (16 words)
cn: 他通常要拍10000到20000张照片，才能得到一个对焦清晰的蝴蝶起飞序列。

en: At such high shutter speeds, the depth of field is tiny, and as butterflies do not fly in a straight line they swiftly flutter out of focus. (27 words)
cn: 在如此高的快门速度下，景深极小，而且由于蝴蝶不沿直线飞行，它们会迅速飘出焦点范围。

en: As well as thousands of attempts, it takes patience and fieldcraft to anticipate a butterfly's likely flight-line—and catch it—in focus. (20 words)
cn: 除了成千上万次的尝试，还需要耐心和野外技巧来预判蝴蝶可能的飞行路线——并在焦点内抓住它。

en: Peters likes to photograph butterflies in a landscape, celebrating the beauty of their surroundings as well as the insects themselves. (20 words)
cn: 彼得斯喜欢在风景中拍摄蝴蝶，既赞美周遭环境的美，也赞美昆虫本身。

en: His pictures of a Glanville fritillary rising from the sea-pinks beside the chalk cliffs of Compton Bay on the Isle of Wight are particularly glorious. (25 words)
cn: 他拍摄的一张格蘭維爾豹纹蝶从怀特岛康普顿湾白垩悬崖边的海石竹花丛中升起的照片格外壮丽。

en: These take-off shots are even more challenging because they require a wide-angle lens, which means he must be less than 2cm from the butterfly. (25 words)
cn: 这些起飞镜头更具挑战性，因为它们需要广角镜头，这意味着他必须距离蝴蝶不到2厘米。

en: It's incredibly difficult to get that close to a skittish, sun-warmed insect. (13 words)
cn: 要如此近距离地靠近一只被太阳晒暖、容易受惊的昆虫，简直难上加难。

en: Unlike some photographers, who "cheat" by keeping insects in a fridge to slow them down, Peters refuses to tamper with wild butterflies. (22 words)
cn: 与一些通过把昆虫关在冰箱里使其行动迟缓来“作弊”的摄影师不同，彼得斯拒绝摆弄野生蝴蝶。

en: So what's the appeal of a long, sweaty day in pursuit of an elusive, fast-moving wild animal? (17 words)
cn: 那么，漫长而汗流浃背的一天，去追逐一只难以捉摸、快速移動的野生动物，其吸引力何在？

en: "It just feels bloody brilliant," says Peters. (7 words)
cn: 彼得斯说：“感覺就是棒透了。”

en: "If I've had a full day of good encounters with butterflies, met interesting butterfly people and I've got some good shots, that becomes a vault in my spiritual bank. It's a happy feeling." (33 words)
cn: “如果我度过了充实的一天，与蝴蝶美好相遇，遇到有趣的蝴蝶爱好者，再拍到一些好照片，那就会成为我精神银行里的一笔珍贵储蓄。这是一种幸福的感觉。”

en: You don't need to get out more. (7 words)
cn: 你不需要多出门。

en: If, like most business people, you spend your life dashing from office to plane, train to home, boardroom to washroom, what you really need to do is stay in more, sit down—and read a book. (33 words)
cn: 如果你像大多数商界人士一样，一生都在从办公室奔向飞机、火车再回家、从会议室奔向洗手间，那么你真正需要做的，是多多待在家里，坐下来——读一本书。

en: Reading has also been found to make us more helpful, to reduce bias, and even to increase longevity—something we will enjoy all the more if we have a good book in our hands. (32 words)
cn: 阅读还被发现能让我们更樂於助人，减少偏见，甚至延长寿命——如果手头有一本好书，我们就会更加享受这一点。

en: (And yes, all these benefits are more closely associated with physical books than digital ones.) (16 words)
cn: （没错，所有这些好处，跟纸质书的关系都比跟电子书更密切。）

en: We are living through a golden age of science writing. (10 words)
cn: 我们正生活在科学写作的黄金时代。

en: So lucid and accessible that even lay readers can relish the unpredictability of discovery. (14 words)
cn: 如此清晰易懂，连外行读者也能享受到发现的不可预测性。

en: Daunted by uncertainty? (3 words)
cn: 被不确定性吓住了吗？

en: Stand in the shoes of scientists and witness the degree to which breakthroughs emerge from accidents, conflict and sheer mental stamina. (22 words)
cn: 站在科学家的立场上，去亲眼目睹突破在多大程度上源于意外、冲突和纯粹的心智耐力。

en: "We are never sure of anything," says the physicist Carlo Rovelli. (11 words)
cn: 物理学家卡洛·罗维利说：“我们對任何事情都從不确知。”

en: Read fiction. (2 words)
cn: 读小说。

en: Any fiction. (2 words)
cn: 任何小说都行。

en: Free yourself from algorithms and choose—anything. (7 words)
cn: 从算法中解放出来，去选择——随便什么都行。

en: None of the carbon cost and all of the benefits of travel. (12 words)
cn: 没有旅行的碳成本，却尽享旅行的全部益处。

en: You don't need technology for an immersive experience—just surrender to narratives across time and place. (16 words)
cn: 你不需要技术来获得沉浸式体验——只需臣服于跨越时空的叙事。

en: Modern (Sebastian Barry or Olga Tokarczuk), classic (Virginia Woolf or James Baldwin) or genre (Stephen King, Margaret Atwood, Georges Simenon)—it doesn't matter. (22 words)
cn: 现代的（塞巴斯蒂安·巴里或奥尔加·托卡尔丘克），经典的（弗吉尼亚·伍尔夫或詹姆斯·鲍德温）或是类型小说（斯蒂芬·金、玛格丽特·阿特伍德、乔治·西默农）——都无关紧要。

en: Fiction invites you to loiter unseen in the lives of others. (12 words)
cn: 小说邀你无形地徘徊于他人的生命之中。

en: We are living through a golden age of translation too, so you can go anywhere in time or place. (19 words)
cn: 我们也正生活在一个翻译的黄金时代，所以你可以在时间或空间上去往任何地方。

en: And just read a single poem. (6 words)
cn: 再就是，只读一首诗。

en: In his Oxford lectures, Seamus Heaney argued that a poem draws a picture of reality, a "glimpsed alternative" that sets up a contradiction with your own, in ways little and large. (29 words)
cn: 谢默斯·希尼在他的牛津讲座中提出，一首诗描绘了一幅现实图景，一种“瞥见的替代选择”，它以或大或小的方式与你自己的现实形成了一种矛盾。

en: The negotiation, between you and it, is the heart of the matter. (13 words)
cn: 你与它之间的协商，才是问题的核心。

en: What does the poet see that you don't? (8 words)
cn: 诗人看到了什么你没有看到的东西？

en: What does the difference mean? (5 words)
cn: 这种差异意味着什么？

en: It could be one of the best conversations you ever have. (12 words)
cn: 它可能是你拥有过的最好的对话之一。

en: Forget self-help books; reading is self-help in action. (8 words)
cn: 忘记自助书籍吧；阅读就是行動中的自助。

en: Neuroscientists have been at pains to demonstrate that the pleasure a book provides isn't indulgence; it's good for you. (19 words)
cn: 神经科学家一直在竭力证明，一本书所提供的乐趣并非放纵；它对你有好处。

en: Reading will keep you better informed about the world but it can also improve our tech-shattered ability to concentrate. (19 words)
cn: 阅读会让你对世界更加了解，但它也能改善我们被科技击碎的专注力。

en: Standing in the shoes of others fine tunes our social understanding, useful as we struggle to understand friends, neighbours, customers and co-workers. (22 words)
cn: 站在他人的立场上，能微调我们的社会理解力，在我們努力理解朋友、邻居、客户和同事时，这会很有用。

en: Different parts of our brain engage as we simulate scenes, characters and mental states. (14 words)
cn: 当我们模拟场景、人物和心理状态时，我们大脑的不同部分会参与进来。

en: Our imagination—remember that?—is rekindled. (5 words)
cn: 我们的想象力——还记得吗？——被重新点燃了。

en: But for the most part, this isn't what the business community does. (13 words)
cn: 但在很大程度上，这并不是商界所做的事情。

en: I have yet to meet a chief executive who reads regularly. (12 words)
cn: 我至今还没遇到过一位经常阅读的首席执行官。

en: Many skip newspapers, and magazines are a stretch. (9 words)
cn: 许多人連报纸都不看，看杂志就更勉强了。

en: They don't have time, they say. (6 words)
cn: 他们说，没有时间。

en: It's inefficient; they can get the information they need from those around them. (14 words)
cn: 这效率太低了；他们可以从身边人那里获取所需的信息。

en: At a pinch, they might pick up a business book before a long flight, in the hope that, like a cookbook, it will provide a foolproof recipe. (28 words)
cn: 在紧要关头，他们可能會在长途飞行前拿起一本商业书籍，希望它能像烹饪书一样，提供一个万无一失的秘诀。

en: Some are drawn to what I think of as "business car crash" books—reading the stories of Theranos, Purdue Pharma, WeWork. (19 words)
cn: 有些人被我认为是“商业车祸”类的书所吸引——阅读Theranos、普渡制药、WeWork的故事。

en: But outside those narrow pools of interest, a vast ocean awaits, bountiful with simmering ideas, mental adventure and imaginative refreshment. (19 words)
cn: 但在那些狭隘的兴趣范围之外，一片浩瀚的海洋正等待着，充满了酝酿中的思想、心智的冒险和想象力的焕新。

en: It is undoubtedly true that all work and no play really does make Jack, or Jill, dull. (18 words)
cn: 只工作不玩耍，的确会让杰克或吉尔变得迟钝，这无疑是真的。

en: The cure is right at hand. (6 words)
cn: 解药就在手边。

en: Reading is cheap, easy and, most important, it's fun. (10 words)
cn: 阅读廉价、简单，而且最重要的是，它很有趣。

en: Liberate your imagination this year. (5 words)
cn: 今年就解放你的想象力吧。

en: Both Boeing and Airbus have trumpeted the efficiency of their newest aircraft, the 787 and A350 respectively.
cn: 波音和空客都在鼓吹各自最新机型——787和A350的性能。

en: Their clever designs and lightweight composites certainly make a difference.
cn: 巧妙的设计与轻质的复合材料确实让它们与众不同。

en: But a group of researchers at Stanford University, led by Ilan Kroo, has suggested that airlines could take a more naturalistic approach to cutting jet-fuel use and it would not require them to buy new aircraft.
cn: 但斯坦福大学以伊兰·克鲁为首的一组研究人员认为，航空公司可以采取更仿生的办法来节省燃油，而且不需要购买新飞机。

en: The answer, says Dr. Kroo, lies with birds.
cn: 克鲁博士说，答案就在鸟类身上。

en: Since 1914, scientists have known that birds flying in formation—a V-shape—expend less energy.
cn: 自1914年以来，科学家们已经知道，鸟类以编队——V字形——飞行时消耗的能量更少。

en: The air flowing over a bird's wings curls upwards behind the wingtips, a phenomenon known as upwash.
cn: 流过鸟类翅膀的气流在翼尖后方向上卷曲，这种现象被称为上洗流。

en: Other birds flying in the upwash experience reduced drag, and spend less energy propelling themselves.
cn: 在上洗流中飞行的其他鸟类受到的阻力会减小，推动自身前进所消耗的能量也更少。

en: This upwash experience may save propelling energy as well as reducing resistance.
cn: 这种上洗流体验不仅可以减少阻力，还可以节省推进能量。

en: Peter Lissaman, an aeronautics expert who was formerly at Caltech and the University of Southern California, has suggested that a formation of 25 birds might enjoy a range increase of 71%.
cn: 曾任职于加州理工学院和南加州大学的航空学专家彼得·利萨曼提出，25只鸟组成的编队可能将飞行距离提升71%。

en: When applied to aircraft, the principles are not substantially different.
cn: 将这些原理应用到飞机上，并没有本质上的不同。

en: Dr. Kroo and his team modeled what would happen if three passenger jets departing from Los Angeles, San Francisco and Las Vegas were to assemble over Utah, assume an inverted V-formation, occasionally change places so all could have a turn in the most favorable positions, and proceed to London.
cn: 克鲁博士及其团队模拟了这样一个场景：三架分别从洛杉矶、旧金山和拉斯维加斯起飞的客机在犹他州上空集合，组成倒V字形编队，并偶尔交换位置以便所有飞机都能轮流占据最有利的位置，然后继续飞往伦敦。

en: They found that the aircraft consumed as much as 15% less fuel, coupled with a reduction in carbon-dioxide output.
cn: 他们发现，飞机消耗的燃油减少了多达15%，同时二氧化碳排放量也减少了。

en: Nitrogen-oxide emissions during the cruising portions of the flight fell by around a quarter.
cn: 飞行巡航阶段的氮氧化物排放量下降了约四分之一。

en: There are, of course, knots to be worked out.
cn: 当然，还有一些难题需要解决。

en: One consideration is safety, or at least the perception of it.
cn: 一个考虑因素是安全性，或者至少是人们对安全的观感。

en: Would passengers feel comfortable travelling in companion?
cn: 乘客们结伴飞行会感到舒适吗？

en: Dr. Kroo points out that the aircraft could be separated by several nautical miles, and would not be in the intimate groupings favoured by display teams like the Red Arrows.
cn: 克鲁博士指出，飞机之间可以相隔数海里，不会像红箭飞行表演队那样采用密集编队。

en: A passenger peering out of the window might not even see the other planes.
cn: 乘客望向窗外可能根本看不到其他飞机。

en: However, this does not necessarily make formation flight more comfortable for passengers.
cn: 然而，这并不一定会让编队飞行对乘客来说更舒适。

en: Whether the separation distances involved would satisfy air-traffic-control regulations is another matter, although a working group at the International Civil Aviation Organization has included the possibility of formation flying in a blueprint for new operational guidelines.
cn: 所涉及的间隔距离是否符合空中交通管制规定则是另一个问题，不过国际民用航空组织的一个工作组已将编队飞行的可能性纳入了新运营指南的蓝图。

en: It remains to be seen how weather conditions affect the air flows that make formation flight more efficient.
cn: 天气状况如何影响使编队飞行更高效的气流，仍有待观察。

en: In zones of increased turbulence, the planes' wakes will decay more quickly and the effect will diminish.
cn: 在湍流增强的区域，飞机的尾流会消散得更快，效果也会减弱。

en: Dr. Kroo says this is one of the areas his team will investigate further, meaning the role that weather plays in formation flight has not yet been clearly defined.
cn: 克鲁博士说，这是他的团队将进一步研究的领域之一，这意味着天气在编队飞行中所起的作用尚未明确。

en: It might also be hard for airlines to co-ordinate the departure times and destinations of passenger aircraft in a way that would allow them to gain from formation flight.
cn: 航空公司可能也很难协调客机的起飞时间和目的地，以便让它们从编队飞行中获益。

en: Cargo aircraft, in contrast, might be easier to reschedule, as might routine military flight.
cn: 相比之下，货机可能更容易重新安排时间，常规军事飞行也是如此。

en: As it happens, America's armed forces are on the case already.
cn: 碰巧的是，美国军方已经在着手处理此事了。

en: Earlier this year the country's Defence Advanced Research Projects Agency announced plans to pay Boeing to investigate formation flight, though the programme has yet to begin.
cn: 今年早些时候，美国国防高级研究计划局宣布计划资助波音公司研究编队飞行，不过该项目尚未启动。

en: There are reports that some military aircraft flew in formation when they were low on fuel during the Second World War, but Dr. Lissaman says they are unsubstantiated and have not been documented.
cn: 有报道称，二战期间一些军用飞机在燃料不足时会编队飞行，但利萨曼博士说这些说法没有实据，也没有文献记载。

en: "My father was an RAF pilot and my cousin the skipper of a Lancaster lost over Berlin," he adds. So he should know.
cn: “我父亲是皇家空军飞行员，我堂兄是一架在柏林上空失踪的兰开斯特轰炸机的机长，”他补充道。所以他应该清楚。

en: Leading doctors today weigh in on the debate over the government's role in promoting public health by demanding that ministers impose "fat taxes" on unhealthy food and introduce cigarette-style warnings to children about the dangers of a poor diet.
cn: 知名医生们今天参与了关于政府在促进公共健康方面作用的辩论，要求部长们对不健康食品征收“脂肪税”，并针对儿童引入烟盒式警告，提示不良饮食的危害。

en: Andrew Lansley held that the government should strengthen the sense of responsibility among businesses, insisting that it could not force people to make healthy choices and promising to free businesses from public health regulations.
cn: 安德鲁·兰斯利认为，政府应该加强企业的责任感，坚称政府不能强迫人们做出健康的选择，并承诺让企业免受公共健康法规的约束。

en: But senior medical figures argue for stronger intervention.
cn: 但资深医学界人士主张进行更强有力的干预。

en: They want to stop fast-food outlets opening near schools, restrict advertising of products high in fat, salt or sugar, and limit sponsorship of sports events by fast-food producers such as McDonald's.
cn: 他们希望阻止快餐店在学校附近开业，限制高脂肪、高盐或高糖产品的广告，并限制麦当劳等快餐生产商对体育赛事的赞助。

en: They argue that government action is necessary to curb Britain's addiction to unhealthy food and help halt spiraling rates of obesity, diabetes and heart disease.
cn: 他们认为，政府有必要采取行动来遏制英国对不健康食品的沉迷，并帮助遏制不断攀升的肥胖、糖尿病和心脏病发病率。

en: Professor Terence Stephenson agreed that cigarette-style warnings should be introduced to children about the dangers of a poor diet, and that such measures should be considered as serious as those used against smoking or excessive drinking.
cn: 特伦斯·斯蒂芬森教授同意应针对儿童引入烟盒式警告，提示不良饮食的危害，并且这些措施应该被视为与禁烟或限制过度饮酒同样严肃。

en: "Thirty years ago, it would have been inconceivable to have imagined a ban on smoking in the workplace or in pubs, and yet that is what we have now. Are we willing to be just as courageous in respect of obesity? I would suggest that we should be," said the leader of the UK's children's doctors.
cn: “三十年前，根本无法想象会在工作场所或酒吧禁烟，然而我们现在已经做到了。我们愿意在肥胖问题上展现出同样的勇气吗？我认为我们应该如此，”这位英国儿童医生的领导者说。

en: Lansley has alarmed health campaigners by suggesting he wants industry rather than government to take the lead.
cn: 兰斯利暗示他希望由行业而非政府来牵头，这让健康运动人士感到震惊。

en: He said that manufacturers of crisps and candies could play a central role in the Change4Life campaign, and that the producers of crisps and candies could contribute significantly to it, as part of government efforts to improve public health.
cn: 他说，薯片和糖果制造商可以在Change4Life运动中发挥核心作用，并且作为政府改善公共健康努力的一部分，薯片和糖果生产商可以为此做出重大贡献。

en: Stephenson suggested potential restrictions could include banning TV advertisements for foods high in fat, salt or sugar before 9 p.m. and limiting them on billboards or in cinemas.
cn: 斯蒂芬森建议，可能的限制措施包括在晚上9点前禁止高脂肪、高盐或高糖食品的电视广告，并限制其在广告牌或电影院中的投放。

en: "If we were really bold, we might even begin to think of high-calorie fast food in the same way as cigarettes—by setting strict limits on advertising, product placement and sponsorship of sports events," he said.
cn: “如果我们真的足够大胆，甚至可能开始像对待香烟一样对待高热量快餐——通过对其广告、植入式营销和体育赛事赞助设置严格限制，”他说。

en: Such a move could affect firms such as McDonald's, which sponsors the youth coaching scheme run by the Football Association.
cn: 此举可能会影响麦当劳等公司，该公司赞助了足球协会举办的青少年教练计划。

en: Fast-food chains should also stop offering "inducements" such as toys, cute animals and mobile phone credit to lure young customers, Stephenson said.
cn: 斯蒂芬森说，快餐连锁店还应该停止提供玩具、可爱动物和手机话费等“诱惑”来吸引年轻顾客。

en: Professor Dinesh Bhugra, president of the Royal College of Psychiatrists, said that the government should ban fast-food outlets in the neighborhood of schools, and also urged councils to impose "fast-food-free zones" around schools and hospitals—areas within which takeaways cannot open.
cn: 皇家精神科医学院院长迪内什·布格拉教授说，政府应该禁止在学校附近开设快餐店，并敦促地方议会在学校和医院周围设立“无快餐区”——即外卖店不得开业的区域。

en: He also emphasized that parents should set good examples for their children by keeping a healthy diet at home.
cn: 他还强调，父母应该通过在家保持健康饮食来为孩子树立好榜样。

en: A Department of Health spokesman said: "We need to create a new vision for public health where all of society works together to get healthy and live longer. This includes creating a new 'responsibility deal' with business, built on social responsibility, not state regulation. Later this year, we will publish a white paper setting out exactly how we will achieve this."
cn: 卫生部发言人说：“我们需要为公共健康创造一个全社会共同努力、获得健康并延长寿命的新愿景。这包括与企业达成一项建立在社会责任而非国家监管基础上的新‘责任协议’。今年晚些时候，我们将发布一份白皮书，具体阐述我们将如何实现这一目标。”

en: The food industry will be alarmed that such senior doctors back such radical moves, especially the call to use some of the tough tactics that have been deployed against smoking over the last decade.
cn: 食品行业会感到震惊，因为如此资深的医生支持如此激进的举措，特别是要求使用过去十年针对吸烟所采取的一些强硬策略。

en: "Universal history, the history of what man has accomplished in this world, is at bottom the History of the Great Men who have worked here," wrote the Victorian sage Thomas Carlyle.
cn: “普世历史，即人类在这个世界上所取得成就的历史，归根结底是曾在此世间奋斗过的伟人的历史，”维多利亚时代的哲人托马斯·卡莱尔写道。

en: But not any more it is not.
cn: 但如今已不再是这样了。

en: Suddenly, Britain looks to have fallen out with its favorite historical form.
cn: 突然间，英国似乎已经摒弃了其最喜爱的历史形式。

en: This could be no more than a passing literary craze, but it also points to a broader truth about how we now approach the past: less concerned with learning from our forefathers and more interested in feeling their pain.
cn: 这可能不过是一时的文学热潮，但它也揭示了一个关于我们如今如何对待历史的更广泛的事实：我们不再那么关注向先辈学习，而是更有兴趣去感受他们的痛苦。

en: Today, we want empathy, not inspiration.
cn: 今天，我们想要的是共情，而非启迪。

en: From the earliest days of the Renaissance, the writing of history meant recounting the exemplary lives of great men.
cn: 从文艺复兴初期开始，历史的书写就意味着讲述伟人的典范生平。

en: In 1337, Petrarch began work on his rambling writing De Viris Illustribus—On Famous Men, emphasizing the virtue of classical heroes.
cn: 1337年，彼特拉克开始创作他那部洋洋洒洒的《名人传》，着重强调古典英雄的美德。

en: Petrarch emphasized the virtue of classical heroes, celebrating their greatness in conquering fortune and rising to the top.
cn: 彼特拉克强调古典英雄的美德，颂扬他们征服命运、登上顶峰的伟大。

en: This was the biographical tradition which Niccolò Machiavelli turned on its head.
cn: 这正是尼可罗·马基雅维利颠覆的传记传统。

en: In The Prince, he dismissed virtue as unnecessary for successful leaders, instead championing cunning, ruthlessness, and boldness.
cn: 在《君主论》中，他摒弃了美德，认为它对成功的领导者来说并非必需，反而推崇狡诈、冷酷和大胆。

en: Over time, the attributes of greatness shifted.
cn: 随着时间的推移，伟大的特质发生了转变。

en: The Romantics commemorated the leading painters and authors of their day, highlighting the public glory of the leading artists and stressing the uniqueness of artistic experience.
cn: 浪漫主义者纪念他们同时代的杰出画家和作家，突出一流艺术家的公众荣耀，并强调艺术体验的独特性。

en: By contrast, the Victorian author Samuel Smiles wrote Self-Help as a catalogue of the worthy lives of engineers, industrialists and explorers.
cn: 相比之下，维多利亚时代的作家塞缪尔·斯迈尔斯撰写了《自助》一书，将其作为工程师、实业家和探险家有价值人生的目录。

en: Samuel Smiles depicted the worthy lives of engineers, industrialists and explorers, emphasizing self-help, perseverance, and moral improvement.
cn: 塞缪尔·斯迈尔斯描绘了工程师、实业家和探险家有价值的人生，强调自助、毅力和道德修养。

en: This was all a bit bourgeois for Thomas Carlyle, who focused his biographies on epochal figures whose lives were hard to imitate.
cn: 这对托马斯·卡莱尔来说有点太平民化了，他将传记的焦点集中在那些人生难以模仿的划时代人物身上。

en: Thomas Carlyle focused on epochal figures such as Luther, Cromwell and Napoleon, presenting them as heroic but largely unrepeatable lives.
cn: 托马斯·卡莱尔聚焦于路德、克伦威尔和拿破仑等划时代人物，将他们的人生呈现为英雄般的，但在很大程度上是不可复制的。

en: Not everyone was convinced by such bombast.
cn: 并非所有人都信服这种浮夸之词。

en: Marx and Engels held that history should be the story of the masses and their record of struggle.
cn: 马克思和恩格斯认为，历史应该是人民群众的故事及其斗争的记录。

en: They argued that real history is made by ordinary people under social and economic conditions, and that great men alone do not make history.
cn: 他们主张，真正的历史是由普通人在社会和经济条件下创造的，伟人并不能单独创造历史。

en: This tradition revolutionized our appreciation of the past and opened up new realms of understanding history, including class, gender, race, and cultural studies.
cn: 这一传统彻底改变了我们对过去的认知，并开辟了理解历史的新领域，包括阶级、性别、种族和文化研究。

en: The hugely popular blog the Skint Foodie chronicles how Tony balances his love of good food with living on benefits.
cn: 极受欢迎的博客“穷光美食家”记录了托尼如何在靠救济金生活的同时，平衡自己对美食的热爱。

en: After bills, Tony has £60 a week to spend, £40 of which goes on food.
cn: 付完账单后，托尼每周有60英镑可供开销，其中40英镑花在食物上。

en: But 10 years ago he was earning £130,000 a year working in corporate communications and eating at London's best restaurants at least twice a week.
cn: 但10年前，他从事企业传播工作，年薪13万英镑，每周至少在伦敦最好的餐厅用餐两次。

en: Then his marriage failed, his career burned out and his drinking became serious.
cn: 后来，他的婚姻破裂了，事业走到了尽头，酗酒问题也变得严重起来。

en: "The community mental health team saved my life. And I felt like that again, to a certain degree, when people responded to the blog so well. It gave me the validation and confidence that I'd lost. But it's still a day-by-day thing."
cn: “社区心理健康小组救了我的命。当人们对博客反响如此之好时，我在某种程度上又找到了那种感觉。它给了我失去的认可和信心。但这仍然是一件日复一日的事情。”

en: Now he's living in a council flat and fielding offers from literary agents.
cn: 现在他住在政府的廉租房里，并收到了文学经纪人抛来的橄榄枝。

en: He's feeling positive, but he'll carry on blogging—not about eating as cheaply as you can—"there are so many people in a much worse state, with barely any money to spend on food"—but about eating well on a budget.
cn: 他感觉积极乐观，但他会继续写博客——不是关于如何尽可能吃得便宜——“有很多人的情况糟得多，几乎没钱买食物”——而是关于如何在预算有限的情况下吃得好。

en: Here's his advice for economical foodies.
cn: 以下是他给经济型美食家的建议。

en: Planning is everything. Impulsive spending isn't an option, so plan your week's menu in advance, making shopping lists for your ingredients in their exact quantities.
cn: 计划就是一切。冲动消费不可取，所以要提前计划好一周的菜单，列出所需食材的确切数量购物清单。

en: I have an Excel template for a week of breakfast, lunch and dinner. Stop laughing: it's not just cost effective but helps you balance your diet.
cn: 我有一个一周早餐、午餐和晚餐的Excel模板。别笑：这不仅划算，还有助于你平衡饮食。

en: It's also a good idea to shop daily instead of weekly, because, being human, you'll sometimes change your mind about what you fancy.
cn: 每天购物而不是每周购物也是个好主意，因为，作为人，你有时会改变主意，想吃点别的。

en: Stick to what you need.
cn: 只买你需要的东西。

en: This is where supermarkets and their anonymity come in handy.
cn: 这就是超市及其匿名购物的便利所在。

en: With them, there's not the same embarrassment as when buying one carrot in a little greengrocer.
cn: 在超市里，不会像在小蔬菜水果店只买一根胡萝卜那样尴尬。

en: And if you plan properly, you'll know that you only need, say, 350g of shin of beef and six rashers of bacon, not whatever weight is pre-packed in the supermarket chiller.
cn: 如果你计划得当，你就会知道，比如说，你只需要350克牛腱肉和六片培根，而不是超市冰柜里预包装好的任意重量。

en: Waste not, want not.
cn: 俭以防匮。

en: You may proudly claim to only have frozen peas in the freezer—that's not good enough.
cn: 你可能会自豪地说冰箱里只有冷冻豌豆——这还不够好。

en: Mine is filled with leftovers, bread, stock, meat and fish.
cn: 我的冰箱装满了剩菜、面包、高汤、肉和鱼。

en: Planning ahead should eliminate wastage, but if you have surplus vegetables you'll do a vegetable soup, and all fruits threatening to "go off" will be cooked or juiced.
cn: 提前计划应该能杜绝浪费，但如果你有多余的蔬菜，可以做蔬菜汤，所有快要“变质”的水果都会被煮成菜或榨成果汁。

en: Shopkeepers are your friends. Everyone says this, but it really is a top tip for frugal eaters.
cn: 店主是你的朋友。大家都这么说，但这对于节俭的食客来说确实是一个顶级建议。

en: Shop at butchers, delis and fish-sellers regularly, even for small things, and be super friendly.
cn: 定期光顾肉铺、熟食店和鱼贩，哪怕只是为了买点小东西，并且要极其友好。

en: Soon you'll feel comfortable asking if they've any knuckles of ham for soups and stews, or beef bones, free.
cn: 很快你就能自在地询问他们是否有免费的、用来做汤和炖菜的猪腿骨，或者牛骨。

en: Remember to treat yourself.
cn: 记得犒劳自己。

en: You won't be eating out a lot, but save your pennies and once every few months treat yourself to a set lunch at a good restaurant.
cn: 你不会经常外出就餐，但可以把零钱攒起来，每隔几个月去一家好餐厅犒劳自己一顿套餐午餐。

en: £1.75 a week for three months gives you £21—more than enough for a three-course lunch at Michelin-starred Arbutus. It's £16.95 there—or £12.99 for a large pizza from Domino's: I know which I'd rather eat.
cn: 每周攒1.75英镑，三个月就有21英镑——足以在米其林星级餐厅Arbutus享用一顿三道菜的午餐。那里只要16.95英镑——或者花12.99英镑在达美乐买个大批萨：我知道我宁愿吃哪个。

en: Emerging in the late Sixties and reaching a peak in the Seventies, Land Art was one of a range of new forms, including Body Art, Performance Art, Action Art and Installation Art, which pushed art beyond the traditional confines of the studio and gallery.
cn: 大地艺术兴起于六十年代末，在七十年代达到顶峰，它与身体艺术、行为艺术、行动艺术和装置艺术等一系列新形式一起，将艺术推向了工作室和画廊的传统界限之外。

en: Rather than portraying landscape, land artists used the physical substance of the land itself as their medium.
cn: 大地艺术家不是描绘风景，而是将土地本身的物质实体作为创作媒介。

en: The British Land Art, typified by Richard Long's piece, was not only more domestically scaled, but a lot quirkier than its American counterpart.
cn: 以理查德·朗的作品为代表的英国大地艺术，不仅规模更具本土化，而且比美国的大地艺术古怪得多。

en: Indeed, while you might assume that an exhibition of Land Art would consist only of records of works rather than the works themselves, Long's photograph of his work is the work.
cn: 事实上，虽然你可能认为大地艺术展只会包含作品的记录而非作品本身，但朗为其作品拍摄的照片就是作品本身。

en: Since his "action" is in the past, the photograph is its sole embodiment.
cn: 由于他的“行动”发生在过去，照片便成为其唯一的体现。

en: That might seem rather an obscure point, but it sets the tone for an exhibition that contains a lot of black-and-white photographs and relatively few natural objects.
cn: 这看起来可能有点晦涩难懂，但它为一个包含大量黑白照片而自然物体相对较少的展览定下了基调。

en: Long is Britain's best-known Land Artist and his Stone Circle, a perfect ring of purplish rocks from Portishead beach laid out on the gallery floor, represents the elegant, rarefied side of the form.
cn: 朗是英国最著名的大地艺术家，他的作品《石圈》用来自波蒂斯黑德海滩的紫色石头在画廊地板上铺成一个完美的圆环，代表了这种形式优雅、精练的一面。

en: Stone Circle—represents the elegance of the British land art.
cn: 《石圈》——代表了英国大地艺术的优雅。

en: The Boyle Family, on the other hand, stand for its dirty, urban aspect.
cn: 另一方面，博伊尔家族则代表了其脏乱、都市化的一面。

en: Comprising artists Mark Boyle and Joan Hills and their children, they recreated random sections of the British landscape on gallery walls.
cn: 由艺术家马克·博伊尔和琼·希尔斯及其子女组成的这个团体，在画廊墙上重现了英国景观的随机片段。

en: Their Olaf Street Study, a square of brick-strewn waste ground, is one of the few works here to embrace the commonplaceness that characterises most of our experience of the landscape most of the time.
cn: 他们的《奥拉夫街习作》是一块布满砖块的方形荒地，是这里为数不多的、拥抱平凡性的作品之一，这种平凡性恰恰是我们大多数时候对景观体验的特征。

en: Olaf Street Study—depicts the ordinary side of the British land art.
cn: 《奥拉夫街习作》——描绘了英国大地艺术平凡的一面。

en: Parks feature, particularly in the earlier works, such as John Hilliard's very funny Across the Park, in which a long-haired stroller is variously smiled at by a pretty girl and unwittingly assaulted in a sequence of images that turn out to be different parts of the same photograph.
cn: 公园在作品中尤为突出，特别是在早期作品中，例如约翰·希利亚德非常有趣的《穿过公园》，在这组照片中，一个长发散步者时而受到一个漂亮女孩的微笑，时而又遭到无意的袭击，而这些图像原来都是同一张照片的不同部分。

en: Across the Park—contains images from different parts of the same photograph.
cn: 《穿过公园》——包含了同一张照片不同部分的图像。

en: Some works move towards landscapes that are traditionally considered beautiful such as the Lake District or the Wiltshire Downs.
cn: 有些作品转向了传统上被认为是美丽的风景，例如湖区或威尔特郡丘陵地。

en: While it probably wasn't apparent at the time, much of this work is permeated by a spirit of romantic escapism that the likes of Wordsworth would have readily understood.
cn: 尽管在当时可能并不明显，但大部分这类作品都弥漫着一种浪漫主义的逃避精神，华兹华斯之流会很容易理解这一点。

en: Derek Jarman's yellow-tinted film Towards Avebury, a collection of long, mostly still shots of the Wiltshire landscape, evokes a tradition of English landscape painting stretching from Samuel Palmer to Paul Nash.
cn: 德里克·贾曼的黄色调电影《朝向埃夫伯里》汇集了对威尔特郡风景的长镜头、多为静态的拍摄，它唤起了从塞缪尔·帕尔默到保罗·纳什的英国风景画传统。

en: Towards Avebury—reminds people of the English landscape painting tradition.
cn: 《朝向埃夫伯里》——让人们想起英国风景画传统。

en: In the case of Hamish Fulton, you can't help feeling that the Scottish artist has simply found a way of making his love of walking pay.
cn: 至于哈米什·富尔顿，你会不禁觉得这位苏格兰艺术家只是找到了一种方式，让自己对步行的热爱变得有利可图。

en: A typical work, such as Seven Days, consists of a single beautiful black-and-white photograph taken on an epic walk, with the mileage and number of days taken listed beneath.
cn: 他的典型作品，如《七天》，包含一张在史诗般徒步中拍摄的唯美黑白照片，照片下方列出了所走的里程和天数。

en: Seven Days—A originates from a long walk that the artist took.
cn: 《七天》——源自艺术家的一次长途步行。

en: British Land Art as shown in this well selected, but relatively modestly scaled exhibition wasn't about imposing on the landscape, more a kind of landscape-orientated light conceptual art created passing through.
cn: 正如这场精心挑选但规模相对适中的展览所展示的，英国大地艺术并非要强加于景观之上，而更像是一种以景观为导向、在通行过程中创作出来的轻概念艺术。

en: It had its origins in the great outdoors, but the results were as gallery-bound as the paintings of Turner and Constable.
cn: 它起源于广袤的户外，但其成果却像透纳和康斯特布尔的画作一样，被束缚在画廊之内。

en: Unfortunately, life is not a bed of roses. We are going through life facing sad experiences.
cn: 不幸的是，生活并非总是铺满玫瑰。我们在人生的旅途中总会面对悲伤的经历。

en: Moreover, we are grieving various kinds of loss: a friendship, a romantic relationship or a house.
cn: 而且，我们会因各种各样的失去而悲伤：一段友谊、一段恋情或一栋房子。

en: Hard times may hold you down at what usually seems like the most inopportune time, but you should remember that they won't last forever.
cn: 艰难时刻似乎总在最不合时宜的时候将你击垮，但你要记住，它们不会永远持续下去。

en: When our time of mourning is over, we press forward, stronger with a greater understanding and respect for life.
cn: 当哀悼期结束，我们继续前行，变得更坚强，对生命有了更深的理解和尊重。

en: Furthermore, these losses make us mature and eventually move us toward future opportunities for growth and happiness.
cn: 此外，这些失去使我们成熟，并最终推动我们走向未来成长和幸福的机会。

en: I want to share these old truths I've learned along the way.
cn: 我想分享这些一路走来我领悟到的古老真理。

en: Fear is both useful and harmful.
cn: 恐惧既有用又有害。

en: This normal human reaction is used to protect us by signaling danger and preparing us to deal with it.
cn: 这种正常的人类反应通过发出危险信号并让我们做好应对准备来保护我们。

en: Unfortunately, people create inner barriers with the help of exaggerating fears.
cn: 不幸的是，人们借助夸张的恐惧制造了内心的障碍。

en: My favorite actor Will Smith once said, "Fear is not real. It is a choice."
cn: 我最喜欢的演员威尔·史密斯曾说过：“恐惧不是真实的，它是一种选择。”

en: I do completely agree that fears are just the product of our luxuriant imagination.
cn: 我完全同意，恐惧只不过是我们丰富想象力的产物。

en: If you are surrounded by problems and cannot stop thinking about the past, try to focus on the present moment.
cn: 如果你被问题包围，无法停止回想过去，试着把注意力集中在当下。

en: Many of us are weighed down by the past or anxious about the future.
cn: 我们很多人被过去所累，或对未来感到焦虑。

en: You may feel guilt over your past, but you are poisoning the present with the things and circumstances you cannot change.
cn: 你可能会为过去感到内疚，但你这是在用无法改变的事情和境遇毒害现在。

en: Value the present moment and remember how fortunate you are to be alive.
cn: 珍惜当下，记住你活着是多么幸运。

en: Enjoy the beauty of the world around and keep the eyes open to see the possibilities before you.
cn: 享受周围世界的美好，睁开眼睛看看你面前的可能性。

en: Happiness is not a point of future and not a moment from the past, but a mindset that can be designed into the present.
cn: 幸福既不是未来的某个节点，也不是过去的某个时刻，而是一种可以植入当下的心态。

en: Sometimes it is easy to feel bad because you are going through tough times.
cn: 有时候，因为正经历艰难时期，很容易感到难过。

en: You can be easily caught up by life problems that you forget to pause and appreciate the things you have.
cn: 你可能很容易被生活中的问题所困，以至于忘记停下来感激你所拥有的东西。

en: Only strong people prefer to smile and value their life instead of crying and complaining about something.
cn: 只有坚强的人才会选择微笑并珍惜生活，而不是哭泣和抱怨。

en: No matter how isolated you might feel and how serious the situation is, you should always remember that you are not alone.
cn: 无论你感到多么孤立无援，无论情况多么严重，你都要始终记住，你并不孤单。

en: Try to keep in mind that almost everyone respects and wants to help you if you are trying to make a good change in your life, especially your dearest and nearest people.
cn: 尽量记住，如果你正努力在生活中做出好的改变，几乎每个人都会尊重并愿意帮助你，尤其是你最亲近的人。

en: You may have a circle of friends who provide constant good humor, help and companionship.
cn: 你可能有一个朋友圈，他们不断提供好的心情、帮助和陪伴。

en: If you have no friends or relatives, try to participate in several online communities, full of people who are always willing to share advice and encouragement.
cn: 如果你没有朋友或亲戚，试着加入几个在线社区，那里有很多总是愿意分享建议和鼓励的人。

en: Today many people find it difficult to trust their own opinion and seek balance by gaining objectivity from external sources.
cn: 如今，许多人觉得很难相信自己的观点，并试图通过从外部来源获取客观性来寻求平衡。

en: This way you devalue your opinion and show that you are incapable of managing your own life.
cn: 这样做你贬低了自己的观点，并表明你没有能力管理自己的生活。

en: When you are struggling to achieve something important you should believe in yourself and be sure that your decision is the best.
cn: 当你努力去实现某件重要的事情时，你应该相信自己，并确信你的决定是最佳的。

en: You live in your skin, think your own thoughts, have your own values and make your own choices.
cn: 你活在自己的躯壳里，思考自己的想法，拥有自己的价值观，并做出自己的选择。

en: As adults, it seems that we are constantly pursuing happiness, often with mixed results.
cn: 作为成年人，我们似乎一直在追求幸福，结果往往喜忧参半。

en: Yet children appear to have it down to an art—and for the most part they don't need self-help books or therapy.
cn: 然而孩子们似乎深谙此道——而且在大多数情况下，他们不需要自助书籍或心理治疗。

en: Instead, they look after their wellbeing instinctively, and usually more effectively than we do as grownups.
cn: 相反，他们本能地维护自己的身心健康，而且通常比我们成年人做得更有效。

en: Perhaps it's time to learn a few lessons from them.
cn: 也许是时候向他们学习一些经验了。

en: What does a child do when he's sad? He cries. When he's angry? He shouts. Scared? Probably a bit of both.
cn: 孩子伤心的时候会做什么？他会哭。生气的时候呢？他会喊。害怕的时候呢？可能两者都有一点。

en: As we grow up, we learn to control our emotions so they are manageable and don't dictate our behaviours, which is in many ways a good thing.
cn: 随着我们长大，我们学会了控制情绪，以便让情绪可控，不让情绪支配我们的行为，这在很多方面是件好事。

en: But too often we take this process too far and end up suppressing emotions, especially negative ones.
cn: 但很多时候我们做得太过头了，最终压抑了情绪，尤其是负面情绪。

en: That's about as effective as brushing dirt under a carpet and can even make us ill.
cn: 这几乎和把灰尘扫到地毯下一样有效，甚至可能让我们生病。

en: What we need to do is find a way to acknowledge and express what we feel appropriately, and then—
cn: 我们需要做的是找到一种方式来承认并恰当地表达我们的感受，然后——

en: A couple of Christmases ago, my youngest stepdaughter, who was nine years old at the time, got a Superman T-shirt for Christmas.
cn: 几年前的一个圣诞节，我最小的继女，当时九岁，收到了一件超人T恤作为圣诞礼物。

en: It cost less than a fiver but she was overjoyed, and couldn't stop talking about it.
cn: 这衣服花了不到五英镑，但她欣喜若狂，不停地谈论它。

en: Too often we believe that a new job, bigger house or better car will be the magic silver bullet that will allow us to finally be content, but the reality is these things have very little lasting impact on our happiness levels.
cn: 我们常常相信，一份新工作、更大的房子或更好的汽车会是最终让我们满足的神奇妙方，但现实是，这些东西对我们幸福水平的持久影响微乎其微。

en: Instead, being grateful for small things every day is a much better way to improve wellbeing.
cn: 相反，每天对小事心怀感激是提升幸福感的更好方式。

en: Have you ever noticed how much children laugh?
cn: 你有没有注意到孩子们有多爱笑？

en: If we adults could indulge in a bit of silliness and giggling, we would reduce the stress hormones in our bodies, increase good hormones like endorphins, improve blood flow to our hearts and even have a greater chance of fighting off infection.
cn: 如果我们成年人能放纵一下，做一些傻事、咯咯笑一笑，我们就会减少体内的压力荷尔蒙，增加内啡肽等有益荷尔蒙，改善心脏的血流，甚至更有可能抵御感染。

en: All of which would, of course, have a positive effect on our happiness levels.
cn: 当然，所有这些都会对我们的幸福水平产生积极影响。

en: The problem with being a grownup is that there's an awful lot of serious stuff to deal with—work, mortgage payments, figuring out what to cook for dinner.
cn: 成年人的问题是有一大堆严肃的事情要处理——工作、房贷、琢磨晚饭做什么。

en: But as adults we also have the luxury of being able to control our own diaries and it's important that we schedule in time to enjoy the things we love.
cn: 但作为成年人，我们也拥有可以掌控自己日程的奢侈，重要的是我们要安排时间去享受我们喜爱的事物。

en: Those things might be social, sporting, creative or completely random (dancing around the living room, anyone?)—it doesn't matter, so long as they're enjoyable, and not likely to have negative side effects, such as drinking too much alcohol or going on a wild spending spree if you're on a tight budget.
cn: 这些事情可以是社交的、体育的、创造性的或完全随机的（有人想在客厅跳舞吗？）——这没关系，只要它们令人愉快，而且不太可能产生负面影响，比如喝太多酒，或者在预算紧张的情况下疯狂消费。

en: Having said all of the above, it's important to add that we shouldn't try too hard to be happy.
cn: 说了这么多，有必要补充一点，我们不应该太刻意地去追求快乐。

en: Scientists tell us this can backfire and actually have a negative impact on our wellbeing.
cn: 科学家告诉我们，这可能会适得其反，实际上对我们的身心健康产生负面影响。

en: As the Chinese philosopher Chuang Tzu is reported to have said: "Happiness is the absence of striving for happiness."
cn: 据报道，中国哲学家庄子曾说：“幸福就是不刻意追求幸福。”

en: And in that, once more, we need to look to the example of our children, to whom happiness is not a goal but a natural byproduct of the way they live.
cn: 在这一点上，我们再次需要以孩子为榜样，对他们来说，幸福不是一个目标，而是他们生活方式自然产生的副产品。

en: The decline in American manufacturing is a common refrain, particularly from Donald Trump. "We don't make anything anymore," he told Fox News, while defending his own made-in-Mexico clothing line.
cn: 美国制造业的衰退是一个老生常谈的话题，唐纳德·特朗普尤其爱提。他在为自己在墨西哥生产的服装系列辩护时对福克斯新闻说：“我们什么也不生产了。”

en: Without question, manufacturing has taken a significant hit during recent decades, and further trade deals raise questions about whether new shocks could hit manufacturing.
cn: 毫无疑问，制造业在最近几十年遭受了重创，而进一步的贸易协议又引发了新的冲击是否会影响制造业的疑问。

en: But there is also a different way to look at the data.
cn: 但也有另一种看待这些数据的方式。

en: Across the country, factory owners are now grappling with a new challenge: Instead of having too many workers, they may end up with too few.
cn: 在全国各地，工厂主们现在正努力应对一项新挑战：他们可能最终面临工人太少的情况，而不是工人太多。

en: Despite trade competition and outsourcing, American manufacturing still needs to replace tens of thousands of retiring boomers every year.
cn: 尽管存在贸易竞争和业务外包，美国制造业每年仍需替换数以万计的退休婴儿潮一代。

en: Millennials may not be that interested in taking their place. Other industries are recruiting them with similar or better pay.
cn: 千禧一代可能对顶替他们的位置不太感兴趣。其他行业正以相近或更高的薪酬招募他们。

en: For factory owners, it all adds up to stiff competition for workers—and upward pressure on wages.
cn: 对工厂主来说，这一切都意味着对工人的激烈竞争——以及工资上涨的压力。

en: "They're harder to find and they have job offers," says Jay Dunwell, president of Wolverine Coil Spring, a family-owned firm.
cn: “他们更难找到，而且他们手里有工作机会，”家族企业Wolverine Coil Spring的总裁杰伊·邓威尔说。

en: "They may be coming into the workforce, but they've been plucked by other industries that are also doing as well as manufacturing."
cn: “他们可能会进入劳动力市场，但他们已经被其他和制造业表现一样好的行业抢走了。”

en: Mr. Dunwell has begun bringing high school juniors to the factory so they can get exposed to its culture.
cn: 邓威尔先生已经开始带高中三年级学生到工厂来，让他们接触工厂文化。

en: At RoMan Manufacturing, a maker of electrical transformers and welding equipment that his father cofounded in 1980, Robert Roth keeps a close eye on the age of his nearly 200 workers. Five are retiring this year.
cn: 在他父亲于1980年共同创立的、生产变压器和焊接设备的罗曼制造公司，罗伯特·罗斯密切关注着近200名员工的年龄。今年有五名员工即将退休。

en: Mr. Roth has three community-college students enrolled in a work-placement program, with a starting wage of $13 an hour that rises to $17 after two years.
cn: 罗斯先生招了三名社区学院的学生参加一个工作实习项目，起薪为每小时13美元，两年后涨到17美元。

en: At a worktable inside the transformer plant, young Jason Stenquist looks flustered by the copper coils he's trying to assemble and the arrival of two visitors. It's his first week on the job.
cn: 在变压器厂内的工作台旁，年轻的杰森·斯滕奎斯特正试图组装铜线圈，面对两位访客的到来，他显得有些慌张。这是他上岗的第一周。

en: Later, he explains his interest in technical work, switching to electrical engineering. "I love working with tools. I love creating," he says.
cn: 后来，他解释了自己对技术工作的兴趣，并转到了电气工程领域。“我喜欢用工具干活。我喜欢创造，”他说。

en: "The gap is between the jobs that take no skills and those that require a lot of skill," says Rob Spohr, a business professor at Montcalm Community College.
cn: “差距在于那些不需要技能的工作和需要大量技能的工作之间，”蒙特卡姆社区学院的商科教授罗布·斯波尔说。

en: "There're enough people to fill the jobs at McDonald's and other places where you don't need to have much skill. It's that gap in between, and that's where the problem is."
cn: “有足够多的人去填补麦当劳和其他不需要太多技能的地方的职位。问题就在于这中间的缺口，这才是问题所在。”

en: Julie Parks of Grand Rapids Community College points to another key to luring Millennials into manufacturing: a work/life balance.
cn: 大急流城社区学院的朱莉·帕克斯指出了吸引千禧一代进入制造业的另一个关键因素：工作与生活的平衡。

en: While their parents were content to work long hours, young people value flexibility. "Overtime is not attractive to this generation. They really want to live their lives," she says.
cn: 虽然他们的父母满足于长时间工作，但年轻人看重灵活性。“加班对这一代人没有吸引力。他们真的想要过自己的生活，”她说。

en: Conversations are links, which means when you have a conversation with a new person a link gets formed and every conversation you have after that moment will strengthen the link.
cn: 对话是纽带，这意味着当你和一个新认识的人交谈时，一条纽带就形成了，而此后你们的每一次交谈都会加强这条纽带。

en: You meet new people every day: the grocery worker, the cab driver, new people at work or the security guard at the door.
cn: 你每天都会遇到新的人：杂货店员工、出租车司机、工作中的新同事或门口的保安。

en: Simply starting a conversation with them will form a link.
cn: 仅仅是与他们开始交谈就会形成一条纽带。

en: Here are five simple ways that you can make the first move and start a conversation with strangers.
cn: 这里有五种简单的方法，可以让你迈出第一步，与陌生人开始交谈。

en: Suppose you are in a room with someone you don't know and something within you says "I want to talk with this person"—this is something that mostly happens with all of us.
cn: 假设你和某个不认识的人同处一室，你内心有个声音说“我想和这个人说话”——这种情况我们大多数人都遇到过。

en: You wanted to say something—the first word—but it just won't come out. It feels like it is stuck.
cn: 你想说点什么——第一个词——但就是说不出口，感觉像是卡住了。

en: Just think: what is the worst that could happen? They won't talk with you? Well, they are not talking with you now!
cn: 只要想一想：最坏的情况会是什么？他们不和你说话？可是，他们现在也没有在和你说话！

en: I truly believe that once you get that first word out everything else will just flow.
cn: 我坚信，一旦你说出了第一个词，其他的一切就会顺理成章。

en: So keep it simple: "Hi", "Hey" or "Hello"—do the best you can to gather all of the enthusiasm and energy you can, put on a big smile and say "Hi".
cn: 所以简单点：“嗨”、“嘿”或者“你好”——尽你所能鼓足所有的热情和精力，露出一个大大的笑容，然后说“嗨”。

en: It's a problem all of us face; you have limited time with the person that you want to talk with and you want to make this talk memorable.
cn: 这是我们所有人都面临的一个问题；你和想要交谈的人时间有限，而你希望让这次交谈令人难忘。

en: Honestly, if we got stuck in the rut of "hi", "hello", "how are you?" and "what is going on?" you will fail to give the initial jolt to the conversation that can make it so memorable.
cn: 老实说，如果我们陷入“嗨”、“你好”、“你好吗？”和“最近怎么样？”的俗套，你将无法给予交谈最初的那个震撼，从而使其令人难忘。

en: So don't be afraid to ask more personal questions. Trust me, you'll be surprised to see how much people are willing to share if you just ask.
cn: 所以，不要害怕问更私人的问题。相信我，你会惊讶地发现，只要你开口问，人们是多么愿意分享。

en: When you meet a person for the first time, make an effort to find the things which you and that person have in common so that you can build the conversation from that point.
cn: 当你初次与人见面时，努力找出你和对方的共同点，这样你就可以从此处开始切入来构建对话。

en: When you start a conversation from there and then move outwards, you'll find all of a sudden that the conversation becomes a lot easier.
cn: 当你从那个点开始交谈，然后向外扩展，你会突然发现交谈变得容易多了。

en: Imagine you are pouring your heart out to someone and they are just busy on their phone, and if you ask for their attention you get the response "I can multitask".
cn: 想象一下，你正在向某人倾诉衷肠，而他们却只顾忙着看手机，如果你要求他们专心一点，得到的回应是“我能同时处理多项任务”。

en: So when someone tries to communicate with you, just be in that communication wholeheartedly.
cn: 所以，当有人试图与你交流时，就全心全意地投入到那次交流中。

en: Make eye contact, you can feel the conversation.
cn: 进行眼神交流，你能感受到对话。

en: You all came into a conversation where you first met the person, but after some time you may have met again and have forgotten their names. Isn't that awkward!
cn: 你们都有过这样的经历：初次与某人交谈时认识了对方，但过了一段时间再次相遇时却忘记了他们的名字。这多尴尬啊！

en: So remember the little details of the people you met or you talked with; perhaps the places they have been to, the place they want to go, the things they like, the things they hate—
cn: 所以，记住你见过或交谈过的人的小细节；也许是他们去过的地方、他们想去的地方、他们喜欢的东西、他们讨厌的东西——

en: When you remember such things you can automatically become investor in their wellbeing. So they feel a responsibility to you to keep that relationship going.
cn: 当你记住这些事情时，你会自然而然地成为他们福祉的投资者。因此，他们会对你有责任感，从而维持这段关系。

en: In choosing a new home, Camille McClain's kids have a single demand: a backyard.
cn: 在选择新家时，卡米尔·麦克莱恩的孩子们只有一个要求：一个后院。

en: McClain's little ones aren't the only kids who have an opinion when it comes to housing, and in many cases youngsters' views weigh heavily on parents' real estate decisions, according to a 2018 Harris Poll survey of more than 2,000 U.S. adults.
cn: 根据2018年哈里斯民意调查对2000多名美国成年人进行的调查，麦克莱恩家的小孩并非唯一在住房问题上有发言权的孩子，而且在很多情况下，年轻人的观点严重影响着父母的房产决策。

en: While more families buck an older-generation proclivity to leave kids in the dark about real estate decisions, realty agents and psychologists have mixed views about the financial, personal and long-term effects kids' opinions may have.
cn: 虽然越来越多的家庭反对老一辈那种对房产决策瞒着孩子的倾向，但房地产经纪人和心理学家对于孩子们的意见可能产生的经济、个人和长期影响持有不同看法。

en: The idea of involving children in a big decision is a great idea because it can help them feel a sense of control and ownership in what can be an overwhelming process, said Ryan Hooper, a clinical psychologist in Chicago.
cn: 芝加哥的临床心理学家莱恩·胡珀说，让孩子参与重大决策是个好主意，因为这能帮助他们在可能令人不知所措的过程中获得一种掌控感和归属感。

en: "Children may face serious difficulties in coping with significant moves, especially if it removes them from their current school or support system," he said.
cn: “孩子们在应对重大搬家时可能会面临严重困难，特别是如果搬家使他们离开了当前的学校或支持体系，”他说。

en: Greg Jaroszewski, a real estate broker with Gagliardo Realty Associates, said he's not convinced that kids should be involved in selecting a home—but their opinions should be considered in regards to proximity to friends and social activities, if possible.
cn: Gagliardo Realty Associates的房地产经纪人格雷格·雅罗斯柴夫斯基说，他不认为应该让孩子们参与选房——但如果可能的话，在靠近朋友和社交活动方面应考虑他们的意见。

en: Younger children should feel like they're choosing their home—without actually getting a choice in the matter, said Adam Bailey, a real estate attorney based in New York.
cn: 纽约的房地产律师亚当·贝利说，应该让年幼的孩子觉得是他们在选房——而实际上他们在选房这件事上并没有选择权。

en: Asking them questions about what they like about the backyard of a potential home will make them feel like they're being included in the decision-making process, Bailey said.
cn: 贝利说，问问他们喜欢意向房屋后院的哪些方面，会让他们觉得被纳入了决策过程。

en: Many of the aspects of homebuying aren't a consideration for children, said Tracey Hampson. He argues that parents should be careful not to let children's opinions dominate the decision.
cn: 特蕾西·汉普森说，购房的许多方面都不在孩子的考虑范围内。他认为，父母应该小心，不要让孩子的意见主导决策。

en: "Speaking with your children before you make a real estate decision is wise, but I wouldn't base the purchasing decision solely on their opinions," Hampson said.
cn: “在做房产决定之前和你的孩子谈谈是明智的，但我不会仅仅基于他们的意见来做购买决定，”汉普森说。

en: The other issue is that many children—especially older ones—may base their real estate knowledge on HGTV shows, said Aaron Norris of The Norris Group in Riverside, Calif.
cn: 另一个问题是，许多孩子——尤其是年龄较大的孩子——可能会把他们对房地产的认知建立在HGTV的节目上，加州里弗赛德市诺里斯集团的亚伦·诺里斯说。

en: "They love Chip and Joanna Gaines just as much as the rest of us," he said. "HGTV has seriously changed how people view real estate. It's not shelter, it's a lifestyle. With that mindset change come some serious money consequences."
cn: “他们和我们其他人一样喜欢奇普和乔安娜·盖恩斯，”他说。“HGTV已经严重改变了人们看待房地产的方式。它不仅仅是居所，更是一种生活方式。伴随这种心态转变而来的是一些严重的金钱后果。”

en: Kids tend to get stuck in the features and the immediate benefits to them personally, Norris said.
cn: 诺里斯说，孩子们往往会拘泥于房屋的特点和给他们个人带来的眼前利益。

en: Parents need to remind their children that their needs and desires may change over time, said Julie Gurner, a real estate analyst with FitSmallBusiness.com.
cn: FitSmallBusiness.com的房地产分析师朱莉·古纳说，父母需要提醒孩子，他们的需求和愿望可能会随着时间而改变。

en: "Their opinions can change tomorrow," Gurner said. "Harsh as it may be to say, that decision should likely not be made contingent on a child's opinions, but rather made for them with great consideration into what home can meet their needs best—and give them an opportunity to customize it a bit and make it their own."
cn: “他们的意见明天就可能改变，”古纳说。“虽然这么说可能很残酷，但这个决策很可能不应该取决于孩子的意见，而是应该为他们做出，要充分考虑什么样的家最能满足他们的需求——并给他们一个机会，稍加定制，把它变成他们自己的家。”

en: This advice is more relevant now than ever before, even as more parents want to embrace the ideas of their children, despite the current housing crunch.
cn: 尽管当前住房市场紧缩，尽管更多父母想要接纳孩子的想法，但这一建议现在比以往任何时候都更有现实意义。

en: Is it possible to like everyone in your office? Think about how tough it is to get together 15 people, much less 50, who all get along perfectly.
cn: 有没有可能喜欢上办公室里的每一个人？想想看，让15个完全合得来的人聚在一起有多难，更别说50个人了。

en: But unlike in friendships, you need coworkers. You work with them every day, and you depend on them just as they depend on you.
cn: 但与友谊不同，你需要同事。你每天都和他们一起工作，你依赖他们，就像他们依赖你一样。

en: Here are some ways that you can get the whole office on your side.
cn: 这里有一些方法可以让你赢得整个办公室的人心。

en: If you have a bone to pick with someone in your workplace, you may try to stay tight-lipped around them. But you won't be helping either one of you.
cn: 如果你对工作场所的某个人有意见，你可能会试图在他们面前守口如瓶。但这样做对你俩都没有帮助。

en: A Harvard Business School study found that observers consistently rated those who were frank about themselves more highly, while those who hid lost trustworthiness.
cn: 哈佛商学院的一项研究发现，观察者一贯对那些坦率谈论自己的人评价更高，而那些有所隐瞒的人则失去了可信度。

en: The lesson is not that you should make your personal life an open book, but rather, when given the option to offer up details about yourself or painstakingly conceal them, you should just be honest.
cn: 这里的教训不是要你把个人生活变成一本打开的书，而是说，当面临是提供关于自己的细节还是煞费苦心地隐藏这些细节的选择时，你应该诚实。

en: Just as important as being honest about yourself is being receptive to others.
cn: 与对自己诚实同样重要的是要善于接纳他人。

en: We often feel the need to tell others how we feel, whether it's a concern about a project, a stray thought, or a compliment.
cn: 我们常常觉得有必要告诉别人我们的感受，无论是对项目的担忧、一个随意的想法，还是一句赞美。

en: Those are all valid, but you need to take time to hear out your coworkers, too.
cn: 这些都很合理，但你也需要花时间听取同事的意见。

en: In fact, rushing to get your own ideas out there can cause colleagues to feel you don't value their opinions.
cn: 事实上，急于表达自己的想法会让同事觉得你不重视他们的意见。

en: Do your best to engage coworkers in a genuine, back-and-forth conversation, rather than prioritizing your own thoughts.
cn: 尽最大努力让同事参与到真诚、有来有往的交谈中，而不是优先考虑你自己的想法。

en: It's common to have a "cubicle mate" or special confidant in a work setting.
cn: 在工作环境中，有一个“隔间密友”或特殊的知己很常见。

en: But in addition to those trusted coworkers, you should expand your horizons and find out about all the people around you.
cn: 但除了这些值得信赖的同事之外，你还应该拓宽视野，了解你身边所有的人。

en: Use your lunch and coffee breaks to meet up with colleagues you don't always see.
cn: 利用午餐和咖啡休息时间与你不太常碰面的同事聚一聚。

en: Find out about their lives and interests beyond the job.
cn: 了解他们工作之外的生活和兴趣。

en: It requires minimal effort and goes a long way.
cn: 这几乎不费什么力气，但大有裨益。

en: This will help to grow your internal network, in addition to being a nice break in the work day.
cn: 除了能让工作日在愉快的休息中度过，这还将帮助扩展你的内部人际网络。

en: Positive feedback is important for anyone to hear. And you don't have to be someone's boss to tell them they did an exceptional job on a particular project.
cn: 正面反馈对任何人来说都很重要。而且你不必是某人的老板才能告诉他们在某个项目上做得很出色。

en: This will help engender good will in others. But don't overdo it or be fake about it.
cn: 这将有助于激发他人的善意。但不要做得过分，也不要虚情假意。

en: One study found that people responded best to comments that shifted from negative to positive, possibly because it suggested they had won somebody over.
cn: 一项研究发现，人们对从消极转向积极的评论反应最好，这可能是因为这暗示着他们赢得了某人的认可。

en: This one may be a bit more difficult to pull off, but it can go a long way to achieving results.
cn: 这一点做到可能有点困难，但它对达成结果很有帮助。

en: Remember in dealing with any coworker what they appreciate from an interaction.
cn: 记住，与任何同事打交道时，要了解他们在互动中看重什么。

en: Watch out for how they verbalize with others.
cn: 留意他们与他人沟通的言辞方式。

en: Some people like small talk in a meeting before digging into important matters, while others are more straightforward.
cn: 有些人喜欢在会议中先闲聊一会儿再切入正题，而有些人则更直截了当。

en: Jokes that work on one person won't necessarily land with another.
cn: 对一个人有效的玩笑，对另一个人未必奏效。

en: So, adapt your style accordingly to type.
cn: 所以，要根据对方类型相应调整你的风格。

en: Consider the person that you're dealing with in advance and what will get you to your desired outcome.
cn: 提前考虑你要打交道的人，以及什么方式能让你达成预期的结果。

en: Your boss proposes a new initiative you think won't work. Your senior colleague outlines a project timeline you believe is unrealistic.
cn: 你的老板提出了一项你认为行不通的新举措。你的资深同事向你概述了一个你认为不切实际的项目时间表。

en: What do you say when you disagree with someone who has more power than you do? How do you decide whether it's worth speaking up? And if you do, what exactly should you say?
cn: 当你与权力大过你的人意见相左时，你该说些什么？你如何决定是否值得发声？如果要说，具体又该怎么说？

en: Here's how to disagree with someone more powerful than you.
cn: 下面是如何与权力大过你的人意见不合的方法。

en: You may decide it's best to hold off on voicing your opinion.
cn: 你可能会认定，最好是先暂缓发表你的意见。

en: Maybe you haven't finished thinking the problem through, or you want to get a clearer sense of what the group thinks.
cn: 也许你还没有把问题完全思考清楚，或者你想更清楚地了解团队的想法。

en: If you think other people are going to disagree, too, you might want to gather your army first.
cn: 如果你认为其他人也会持反对意见，你可能想先集结你的盟友。

en: People can contribute experience or information to your thinking—all the things that would make the disagreement stronger or more valid.
cn: 人们可以为你的思考贡献经验或信息——所有这些都会使你的反对意见更具说服力或更有依据。

en: It's also a good idea to delay the conversation if you're in a meeting or other public space.
cn: 如果你正在开会或其他公共场所，推迟谈话也是个好主意。

en: Discussing the issue in private will make the powerful person feel less threatened.
cn: 私下讨论这个问题会让有权势的人感觉不那么受威胁。

en: Before you share your thoughts, think about what the powerful person cares about—it may be the credibility of their team or getting a project done on time.
cn: 在分享你的想法之前，想想那位有权势的人关心什么——可能是其团队的信誉，或者是让项目按时完成。

en: You're more likely to be heard if you can connect your disagreement to a higher purpose.
cn: 如果你能将你的异议与更高的目标联系起来，你的话就更有可能会被听进去。

en: State it overtly, contextualizing your statements so that you're seen not as a disagreeable subordinate but as a colleague who's trying to advance a common objective.
cn: 要公开陈述，将你的陈述置于具体情境中，这样你就不会被看做一个不好相处的下属，而是一位试图推进共同目标的同事。

en: The discussion will then become more like a chess game than a boxing match.
cn: 这样，接下来的讨论会更像一盘棋局，而不是一场拳击赛。

en: This step may sound overly deferential, but it's a smart way to give the powerful person psychological safety and control.
cn: 这一步可能听起来过于恭敬，但这是一种聪明的方式，能给有权势的人带来心理安全感和控制感。

en: You can say something like, "I know we seem to be moving toward a first-quarter commitment here. I have reasons to think that won't work. I'd like to lay out my reasoning. Would that be OK?"
cn: 你可以这样说：“我知道我们似乎正朝着第一季度的承诺迈进。我有理由认为这行不通。我想阐述一下我的理由。可以吗？”

en: This gives the person a choice, allowing him to verbally opt in.
cn: 这给了对方一个选择，让他口头上选择同意参与。

en: And, assuming he says yes, it will make you feel more confident about voicing your disagreement.
cn: 而且，假设他答应了，这也会让你更有信心表达你的不同意见。

en: You might feel your heart racing or your face turning red, but do whatever you can to remain neutral in both your words and actions.
cn: 你可能会感到心跳加速或脸变红，但要尽你所能保持言辞和行动上的中立。

en: When your body language communicates reluctance or anxiety, it undercuts the message.
cn: 当你的肢体语言传递出犹豫或焦虑时，会削弱所要传达的信息。

en: It sends a mixed message, and your counterpart gets to choose what signals to read.
cn: 这会传达出混杂的信息，而对方将会选择解读什么信号。

en: Deep breaths can help, as can speaking more slowly and deliberately.
cn: 深呼吸会有所帮助，说得更慢、更从容也会有帮助。

en: When we feel panicky, we tend to talk louder and faster. Simply slowing the pace and talking in an even tone helps the other person cool down and does the same for you.
cn: 当我们感到恐慌时，往往说话会更快、更大声。仅仅是放慢节奏、用平稳的语气说话，就能帮助对方冷静下来，对你自己也有同样的效果。

en: It also makes you seem confident, even if you aren't.
cn: 这也让你显得自信，即使你并非如此。

en: Emphasize that you're offering your opinion, not gospel truth.
cn: 要强调你提供的是个人意见，而非绝对真理。

en: It may be a well-informed, well-researched opinion, but it's still an opinion, so talk tentatively and slightly understate your confidence.
cn: 这可能是一个了解充分、研究透彻的意见，但它仍是一个意见，所以要留有余地地谈，稍稍低调地陈述你的确信程度。

en: Instead of saying, "If we set an end-of-quarter deadline, we'll never make it," say, "This is just my opinion, but I don't see how we will make that deadline."
cn: 不要说，“如果我们定在季末的最后期限，我们绝对完不成，”而要说，“这只是我的个人意见，但我看不出来我们怎样才能在最后期限前完成。”

en: Having asserted your position (as a position, not as a fact), demonstrate equal curiosity about other views.
cn: 在表明你的立场之后（作为立场，而非事实），要对其他观点表现出同等的好奇心。
