en: Remind the person that this is your point of view, and then invite critique.
cn: 提醒对方这是你的观点，然后请对方给予评判。

en: Be open to hearing other opinions.
cn: 保持开放心态，乐于听取其他意见。

en: Getting back into exercise after a break can be a challenge in the best of times, but with gyms and in-person exercise classes off-limits to many people these days, it can be tricky to know where to start.
cn: 中断一段时间后重新开始锻炼，即使在最好的情况下也是一项挑战，而如今健身房和面对面健身课对许多人来说都无法实现，要知道从何入手就更棘手了。

en: And it is important to get the right dose of activity. "Too much too soon either results in injury or burnout," says Mary Yoke, PhD, a faculty member in the kinesiology department at Indiana University in Bloomington.
cn: 而且，进行适量活动很重要。“操之过急要么导致受伤，要么导致倦怠，”印第安纳大学布卢明顿分校运动机能学系教员玛丽·约克博士说。

en: The following simple strategies will help you return to exercise safely after a break.
cn: 以下简单策略将帮助你在中断后安全地恢复锻炼。

en: Don't try to go back to what you were doing before your break.
cn: 不要试图一上来就恢复到你中断前的运动量。

en: If you were walking 3 miles a day, playing 18 holes of golf three times a week, or lifting 10-pound dumbbells for three sets of 10 reps, reduce activity to half a mile every other day, or nine holes of golf once a week with short walks on other days, or use 5-pound dumbbells for one set of 10 reps.
cn: 如果你之前每天步行3英里，每周打三次18洞高尔夫，或者举10磅的哑铃，做3组，每组10次，那么现在就把活动量减到每隔一天走半英里，或者每周打一次9洞高尔夫，其他日子进行短距离步行，或者使用5磅的哑铃做一组10次。

en: Increase time, distance, and intensity gradually.
cn: 逐步增加时间、距离和强度。

en: "This isn't something you can do overnight," says medical advisory guidance encouraging Americans not to overlook the benefits of activity during the pandemic.
cn: “这不是你能在一夜之间做到的事情，”鼓励美国人不要忽视疫情期间活动益处的医学指导建议说。

en: But you'll reap benefits such as less anxiety and improved sleep right away.
cn: 但你马上就能收获诸如减少焦虑和改善睡眠等益处。

en: If you're breathing too hard to talk in complete sentences, back off. If you feel good, go a little longer or faster.
cn: 如果你喘得连一句完整的话都说不出，就该放慢节奏。如果你感觉良好，就可以延长一点时间或加快一点速度。

en: Feeling wiped out after a session? Go easier next time.
cn: 一次锻炼后感觉筋疲力尽？下次就轻松一点。

en: And stay alert to serious symptoms, such as chest pain or pressure, severe shortness of breath or dizziness, or faintness, and seek medical attention immediately.
cn: 并且要警惕严重症状，如胸痛或胸闷、严重气短、头晕或昏厥，一旦出现应立即就医。

en: Consistency is the key to getting stronger and building endurance and stamina.
cn: 持之以恒是变得更强壮、培养耐力和持久力的关键。

en: Ten minutes of activity per day is a good start, says Marcus Jackovitz, DPT, a physical therapist at the University of Miami Hospital.
cn: 迈阿密大学医院的理疗师马库斯·杰科维茨说，每天活动十分钟是一个好的开始。

en: All the experts we spoke with highly recommend walking because it's the easiest, most accessible form of exercise.
cn: 我们采访的所有专家都强烈推荐步行，因为它是最简单、最易进行的锻炼形式。

en: Although it can be a workout on its own, if your goal is to get back to Zumba classes, tennis, cycling, or any other activity, walking is also a great first step.
cn: 虽然步行本身就可以是一种锻炼，但如果你的目标是恢复尊巴课、网球、骑行或任何其他活动，步行也是一个很好的第一步。

en: Even if you can't yet do a favorite activity, you can practice the moves.
cn: 即使你还不能进行某项喜爱的活动，你也可以练习动作。

en: With or without a club or racket, swing like you're hitting the ball. Paddle like you're in a kayak or canoe. Mimic your favorite swimming strokes.
cn: 不管有没有球杆或球拍，像击球一样挥杆。像在皮划艇或独木舟里一样划桨。模仿你最喜欢的游泳姿势。

en: The action will remind you of the joy the activity brought you and prime your muscles for when you can get out there again.
cn: 这些动作会让你想起那项活动曾带给你的快乐，并让你的肌肉做好准备，以便你日后可以再次实地进行。

en: Exercising with others "can keep you accountable and make it more fun, so you're more likely to do it again," Jackovitz says.
cn: 和别人一起锻炼“能让你负起责任，并让锻炼更有趣，这样你就更有可能再次锻炼”，杰科维茨说。

en: You can do activities such as golf and tennis or take a walk with others and still be socially distant.
cn: 你可以进行高尔夫和网球等活动，或者和别人一起散步，同时仍然保持社交距离。

en: But when you can't connect in person, consider using technology.
cn: 但当你无法亲身相聚时，可以考虑使用科技手段。

en: Chat on the phone with a friend while you walk around your neighborhood. FaceTime with a relative as you strength train or stretch at home.
cn: 在你绕着街区散步时和朋友通电话。在家进行力量训练或拉伸时，和亲戚视频通话。

en: You can also join a livestream or on-demand exercise class.
cn: 你还可以参加直播或点播的健身课程。

en: New building regulations aimed at improving energy efficiency are set to increase the price of new homes, as well as those of extensions and loft conversions on existing ones.
cn: 旨在提高能源效率的新建筑法规将推高新房价格，以及现有房屋扩建和阁楼改造的价格。

en: The rules, which came into effect on Wednesday in England, are part of government plans to reduce the UK's carbon emissions to net zero by 2050.
cn: 该法规于周三在英格兰生效，是政府计划到2050年将英国碳排放降至净零的一部分。

en: They set new standards for ventilation, energy efficiency and heating, and state that new residential buildings must have charging points for electric vehicles.
cn: 它们为通风、能源效率和供暖设定了新标准，并规定新建住宅建筑必须配备电动汽车充电桩。

en: The moves are the most significant change to building regulations in years, and industry experts say they will inevitably lead to higher prices at a time when a shortage of materials and high labour costs are already driving up bills.
cn: 这些举措是多年来建筑法规最重大的变革，业内专家表示，在材料短缺和劳动力成本高昂已经推高账单的情况下，这将不可避免地导致价格上涨。

en: Brian Berry, chief executive of the Federation of Master Builders, says the measures will require new materials, testing methods, products and systems to be installed.
cn: 建筑商联合会首席执行官布莱恩·贝里表示，这些措施将需要安装新材料、采用新测试方法、新产品和新系统。

en: "All this comes at an increased cost during a time when prices are already sky high. Inevitably, consumers will have to pay more," he says.
cn: “所有这一切都会在价格已经高得离谱的时候增加成本。消费者将不可避免地要付更多的钱，”他说。

en: Gareth Belsham, of surveyors Naismiths, says people who are upgrading, or extending their home, will be directly affected.
cn: 测量公司Naismiths的加雷思·贝尔沙姆说，正在升级或扩建房屋的人将直接受到影响。

en: "The biggest changes relate to heating and insulation," he explains. "There are new rules concerning the amount of glazing used in extensions, and any new windows or doors must be highly insulated."
cn: “最大的变化与供暖和隔热有关，”他解释说，“关于扩建部分玻璃的使用量有新规定，而且任何新的门窗都必须具有高隔热性能。”

en: Windows and doors will have to adhere to higher standards, while there are new limits on the amount of glazing you can have to reduce unwanted heat from the sun.
cn: 门窗将必须遵守更高的标准，同时对可安装的玻璃数量有新的限制，以减少来自太阳的多余热量。

en: Thomas Goodman, of MyJobQuote, says this will bring in new restrictions for extensions. "Glazing on windows, doors and rooflights must cover no more than 25% of the area."
cn: MyJobQuote的托马斯·古德曼说，这将对扩建引入新的限制。“窗户、门和天窗上的玻璃覆盖面积不得超过区域的25%。”

en: As the rules came into effect last Wednesday, property developers were rushing to file plans just before the deadline.
cn: 随着法规于上周三生效，房地产开发商争相在截止日期前提交规划。

en: Any plans submitted before that date are considered to be under the previous rules, and can go ahead as long as work starts before 15 June next year.
cn: 在该日期之前提交的任何规划都被视为符合旧规定，只要在明年6月15日之前开工就可以继续进行。

en: Builders which have costed projects, but have not filed the paperwork, may need to go back and submit fresh estimates, says Marcus Jefford of Build Aviator.
cn: Build Aviator的马库斯·杰福德说，那些已经估算了项目成本但尚未提交文件的建筑商，可能需要回过头去提交新的估算。

en: Materials prices are already up 25% in the last two years. How much overall prices will increase as a result of the rule changes is not clear.
cn: 材料价格在过去两年已经上涨了25%。法规变化将导致总体价格上涨多少尚不清楚。

en: "Whilst admirable in their intentions, they will add to the cost of housebuilding at a time when many already feel that they are priced out of homeownership," says Jonathan Rolande of the National Association of Property Buyers.
cn: “尽管其用意值得赞赏，但它们将在许多人已经感觉自己买不起房的时候，增加房屋建造成本，”全国房产买家协会的乔纳森·罗兰德说。

en: "An average extension will probably see around £3,000 additional cost thanks to the new regs."
cn: “由于新规，平均一次扩建可能会增加约3000英镑的成本。”

en: John Kelly, a construction lawyer at Freeths law firm, believes prices will eventually come down. But not in the immediate future.
cn: Freeths律师事务所的建筑律师约翰·凯利认为，价格最终会降下来。但近期内不会。

en: "As the marketplace adapts to the new requirements, and the technologies that support them, the scaling up of these technologies will eventually bring costs down, but in the short term, we will all have to pay the price of the necessary transition," he says.
cn: “随着市场适应新要求以及支撑这些要求的技术，这些技术的规模化最终将降低成本，但在短期内，我们都必须为这一必要的转变付出代价，”他说。

en: However, the long-term effects of the changes will be more comfortable and energy-efficient homes, adds Andrew Mellor, of PRP architects.
cn: 然而，PRP建筑事务所的安德鲁·梅勒补充说，这些变化带来的长期效果将是更舒适、更节能的住宅。

en: "Homeowners will probably recoup that cost over time in energy bill savings. It will obviously be very volatile at the moment, but they will have that benefit over time."
cn: “房主很可能会通过节省能源账单来随着时间的推移收回那笔成本。目前情况显然会非常不稳定，但他们会随着时间的推移享受到那个好处。”

en: High school students eager to stand out in the college application process often participate in a litany of extracurricular activities hoping to bolster their chances of admission to a selective undergraduate institution.
cn: 渴望在大学申请过程中脱颖而出的高中生，常常参加一连串的课外活动，希望能增加被择优录取的本科院校录取的机会。

en: However, college admissions experts say that the quality of a college hopeful's extracurricular activities matters more than the number of activities he or she participates in.
cn: 然而，大学招生专家表示，对有志上大学的学生来说，课外活动的质量比他或她参加活动的数量更重要。

en: Sue Rexford, the director of college guidance at the Charles E. Smith Jewish Day School, says it is not necessary for a student, filling out the Common Application, to list 10 activities in the application.
cn: 查尔斯·E·史密斯犹太走读学校的大学指导主任苏·雷克斯福德说，学生在填写通用申请表时，没有必要在申请中列出10项活动。

en: "No college will expect that a student has a huge laundry list of extracurriculars that they have been passionately involved in each for an extended period of time," Rexford wrote in an email.
cn: “没有大学会期望学生有一大串他们长期热情投入的课外活动清单，”雷克斯福德在一封电子邮件中写道。

en: Experts say it is tougher to distinguish oneself in a school-affiliated extracurricular activity that is common among high school students than it is to stand out while doing an uncommon activity.
cn: 专家表示，在高中生常见的、学校附属的课外活动中脱颖而出，比在从事一项不常见的活动中脱颖而出更加困难。

en: "The competition to stand out and make an impact is going to be much stiffer, and so if they're going to do a popular activity, I'd say, be the best at it," says Sara Harberson, a college admissions consultant.
cn: “要想脱颖而出并产生影响，竞争会激烈得多，所以如果他们要参加一项热门活动，我的建议是，要做到最好，”大学招生顾问萨拉·哈伯森说。

en: High school students who have an impressive personal project they are working on independently often impress colleges, experts say.
cn: 专家表示，高中生如果有一个正在独立完成的、令人印象深刻的个人项目，往往会给大学留下深刻印象。

en: "For example, a student with an interest in entrepreneurship could demonstrate skills associated with business by creating a mobile app or designing a product," Lindsey Conger, an independent college counselor with Moon Prep admissions consulting firm, wrote in an email.
cn: “例如，对创业感兴趣的学生可以通过创建手机应用程序或设计产品来展示与商业相关的技能，”Moon Prep招生咨询公司的独立大学顾问林赛·康格在一封电子邮件中写道。

en: Joseph Adegboyega-Edun, a Maryland high school guidance counselor, says unconventional extracurricular activities can help students impress college admissions offices, assuming they demonstrated serious commitment.
cn: 马里兰州的一名高中指导顾问约瑟夫·阿德博耶加-埃登说，非传统的课外活动可以帮助学生给大学招生办公室留下深刻印象，前提是他们表现出认真的投入。

en: "Again, since one of the big questions high school seniors must consider is 'What makes you unique?' having an uncommon extracurricular activity vs. a conventional one is an advantage," he wrote in an email.
cn: “再说一次，既然高中毕业班学生必须考虑的重大问题之一是‘是什么让你独一无二？’，那么拥有一项非传统的课外活动相对于传统活动来说是一个优势，”他在一封电子邮件中写道。

en: Experts say demonstrating talent in at least one extracurricular activity can help in the college admissions process, especially at top-tier undergraduate institutions.
cn: 专家表示，在至少一项课外活动中展示才艺，在大学录取过程中会有所帮助，尤其是在顶尖本科院校。

en: "Distinguishing yourself in one focused type of extracurricular activity can be a positive in the admissions process, especially for highly selective institutions, where having top grades and test scores is not enough," Katie Kelley, admissions counselor at IvyWise admissions consultancy, wrote in an email.
cn: “在某一种专注类型的课外活动中表现出色，在招生过程中可能是一个加分项，尤其是对于那些择优录取程度高的院校，仅仅拥有顶尖的成绩和考试分数是不够的，”IvyWise招生咨询公司的招生顾问凯蒂·凯利在一封电子邮件中写道。

en: "Students need to have that quality or hook that will appeal to admissions officers and allow them to visualize how the student might come and enrich their campus community."
cn: “学生需要有那种能吸引招生官的品质或钩子，让他们能够设想这名学生可能会如何来丰富他们的校园社区。”

en: Extracurricular activities related to the college major declared on a college application are beneficial, experts suggest.
cn: 专家建议，与大学申请中申报的大学专业相关的课外活动是有益的。

en: "If you already know your major, having an extracurricular that fits into that major can be a big plus," says Mayghin Levine, the manager of educational opportunities with The Cabbage Patch Settlement House, a Louisville, Kentucky, nonprofit community center.
cn: “如果你已经知道自己的专业，有一个契合该专业的课外活动可能是一个大大的加分项，”肯塔基州路易斯维尔非营利社区中心The Cabbage Patch Settlement House的教育机会经理梅金·莱文说。

en: High school students who have had a strong positive influence on their community through an extracurricular activity may impress a college and win a scholarship, says Erica Gwyn, a former math and science magnet program assistant at a public high school who is now executive director of the Kaleidoscope Careers Academy in Atlanta, a nonprofit organization.
cn: 曾在一所公立高中担任数学和科学磁石项目助理、现为亚特兰大非营利组织万花筒职业学院的执行董事埃里卡·格温说，通过课外活动对社区产生强烈积极影响的高中生可能会给大学留下深刻印象并赢得奖学金。

en: Everyone wants to be that person—the one who looks at the same information as everyone else, but who sees a fresh, innovative solution.
cn: 每个人都想成为那样的人——那个看着与其他人相同的信息，却能发现全新、创新解决方案的人。

en: However, it takes more than simply having a good idea. How you share it is as important as the suggestion itself.
cn: 然而，光有一个好主意是不够的。你如何分享这个主意，与建议本身一样重要。

en: Why? Because writing a new script—literally or figuratively—means that other team members will have to adapt to something new.
cn: 为什么？因为编写一个新剧本——无论是字面上还是比喻意义上——意味着其他团队成员将不得不适应新事物。

en: Not to mention, if the process you're scrapping is one someone else suggested, there's the possibility of hurt feelings.
cn: 更不用说，如果你要废弃的流程是别人提出的，还有可能伤害到感情。

en: To gain buy-in on an innovative, new idea, follow these steps:
cn: 要让大家接受一个创新的、新颖的想法，请遵循以下步骤：

en: Great ideas don't stand alone. In other words, you can't mention your suggestion once and expect it to be adopted.
cn: 好的想法不会独立存在。换句话说，你不能只是提一次你的建议，就期望它被采纳。

en: To see a change, you'll need to champion your plan and sell its merits.
cn: 要看到改变，你需要拥护你的计划并推销其优点。

en: In addition, you need to be willing to stand up to scrutiny and criticism and be prepared to explain your innovation in different ways for various audiences.
cn: 此外，你需要愿意经得起审视和批评，并准备好针对不同的受众以不同的方式解释你的创新。

en: Sometimes it makes sense to go to your boss first.
cn: 有时候，先去找你的老板是合理的。

en: But other times, it's useful to build a coalition among your co-workers or other stakeholders.
cn: 但其他时候，在你的同事或其他利益相关者中建立联盟是很有用的。

en: When it works, it works great—because you're ready for your stubborn supervisor's pushback with answers like, "Actually, I connected with a few people in our tech department to discuss how much time these kinds of website updates would take, and they suggested they have the bandwidth."
cn: 这种方法一旦奏效，效果就很好——因为你对固执的主管可能提出的反对意见做好了准备，可以用这样的话来回应：“实际上，我联系了技术部门的几个人，讨论了这类网站更新需要多少时间，他们表示有足够的资源来处理。”

en: One of the biggest barriers to gaining buy-in occurs when the owner of an idea is viewed as argumentative, defensive, or close-minded.
cn: 获得认同的最大障碍之一是，创意的提出者被视为好争辩、戒心重或思想封闭的人。

en: Because, let's be honest: No one likes a know-it-all.
cn: 因为，说实话：没有人喜欢万事通。

en: So, if people disagree with you, don't be indignant.
cn: 所以，如果人们不同意你的看法，不要愤愤不平。

en: Instead, listen to their concerns fully, try to understand their perspective, and include their concerns (and possible remedies) in future discussions.
cn: 相反，要充分倾听他们的顾虑，试着理解他们的观点，并将他们的顾虑（以及可能的补救措施）纳入未来的讨论中。

en: So, instead of saying, "Martha, our current slogan is confusing and should be updated," you could try, "Martha raises a great point that our current slogan has a long history for our stakeholders, but I wonder if we might be able to brainstorm a tagline that could build on that—and be clearer for new customers."
cn: 所以，与其说：“玛莎，我们目前的口号令人困惑，应该更新，”不如试着说：“玛莎提出了一个非常好的观点，我们目前的口号对我们的利益相关者来说历史悠久，但我想我们也许可以集思广益，想出一个既能基于此、又对新客户更清晰的宣传语。”

en: New ideas are the grandchildren of old ones.
cn: 新想法是旧想法的孙辈。

en: In other words, don't throw old solutions under the bus to make your improvement stand out.
cn: 换句话说，不要为了突显你的改进而把旧的解决方案说得一文不值。

en: Remember that in light of whatever problem the old system solved—or, maybe, has failed to solve in recent memory—it was a great idea at the time.
cn: 记住，考虑到旧系统所解决的——或者，也许是近来未能解决的——无论什么问题，它当时都是一个好主意。

en: Appreciating the older contributions as you suggest future innovations helps bolster the credibility of your idea.
cn: 在提出未来的创新时，肯定过去的贡献，有助于增强你想法的可信度。

en: When pitching a new idea, it's important to use the language of abundance instead of the language of deficit.
cn: 当提出一个新想法时，使用富足的语言而不是匮乏的语言是很重要的。

en: Instead of saying what is wrong, broken, or suboptimal, talk about what is right, fixable, or ideal.
cn: 不要说什么是错的、坏的或不够好的，而要谈什么是对的、可修复的或理想的。

en: For example, try, "I can see lots of applications for this new approach" rather than, "This innovation is the only way."
cn: 例如，试着说，“我可以看到这种新方法有很多应用”，而不是“这一创新是唯一的出路。”

en: Be optimistic but realistic, and you will stand out.
cn: 保持乐观但切合实际，你就会脱颖而出。

en: In an effort to support restoration programmes, specialists are developing supplies of seeds and seedlings, maintaining gene banks and sequencing the genomes of indigenous trees and other crops.
cn: 为了支持恢复项目，专家们正在开发种子和幼苗的供应，维护基因库，并对本地树木和其他作物的基因组进行测序。

en: Their work deals with one of the problems that could block major restoration efforts in different parts of the world.
cn: 他们的工作涉及一个可能阻碍世界不同地区重大恢复工作的问题。

en: "Where's the planting material going to come from? That's one big bottleneck," says genetic-resources specialist Ramni Jamnadass.
cn: “种植材料从哪里来？这是一大瓶颈，”遗传资源专家拉姆尼·贾姆纳达斯说。

en: Asia is arguably the region most neglected by global efforts to increase diversity in restoration and to study native species.
cn: 亚洲可以说是全球最被忽视的地区，在增加恢复多样性和研究本地物种方面所做的努力都不足。

en: Christopher Kettle, Bioversity International's director for forest genetic resources and restoration in Rome, says that the need for infrastructure—things such as mechanisms for collecting and storing seeds, and nurseries to raise seedlings—might be most desperate here because many trees are 'masting' species, which don't produce seeds every year.
cn: 国际生物多样性中心驻罗马的森林遗传资源与恢复主任克里斯托弗·凯特尔说，这里对基础设施的需求——例如收集和储存种子的机制，以及培育幼苗的苗圃——可能最为迫切，因为许多树木是“大年结实”物种，它们并非每年都产种子。

en: People need to be ready. "Otherwise, you miss the boat, you lose all the seed and you've got to wait another seven years," says Kettle.
cn: 人们需要做好准备。“否则，你就会错失良机，失去所有的种子，不得不又等七年，”凯特尔说。

en: "This is a really, really critical issue for restoration in Southeast Asia, because many of the most important timber species and tree species—the ones that will lock up the most carbon—they're all masting species."
cn: “这对于东南亚的恢复来说是一个极其关键的问题，因为许多最重要的用材树种和树种——那些能锁定最多碳的树种——全都是大年结实物种。”

en: Climate change is a driving factor in the push to restore forests, but it also raises questions, such as where trees can thrive in the future.
cn: 气候变化是推动森林恢复的一个驱动因素，但它也带来了一些问题，例如树木未来在哪里能够茁壮成长。

en: Climate change is also expected to alter relationships between trees, insects, diseases and other forest species.
cn: 气候变化预计还将改变树木、昆虫、疾病和其他森林物种之间的关系。

en: "Insects that today are a minor problem may become a major problem if they can produce three or four generations in a year," says forest ecologist John Stanturf.
cn: “今天还只是小问题的昆虫，如果一年内能繁殖三四代，就可能变成大问题，”森林生态学家约翰·斯坦特夫说。

en: This remains a significant knowledge gap.
cn: 这仍然是一个重大的知识空白。

en: "We know enough to know that this is a concern, but we don't know enough about how to respond to it yet. That's a great area to be doing research."
cn: “我们足够了解情况，知道这是一个令人担忧的问题，但我们对如何应对它还不够了解。这是一个做研究的好领域。”

en: So is soil, says Cindy Prescott, a forest ecologist at the University of British Columbia in Vancouver.
cn: 温哥华不列颠哥伦比亚大学的森林生态学家辛迪·普雷斯科特说，土壤也是如此。

en: "If you don't look at the soil at the start, you can spend a lot of money and time putting in species that aren't going to survive there."
cn: “如果你一开始不关注土壤，你可能会花费大量的金钱和时间，种下些在那里根本活不了的物种。”

en: With so much research left to do, leaders in the field have been doing some soul-searching, and acknowledging that restoration can be motivated by—and designed to meet—different needs.
cn: 还有如此多的研究要做，该领域的领导者们一直在进行一些反思，并承认恢复工作可能出于不同的需求，也应被设计来满足不同的需求。

en: "When you talk about conservation or restoration, the first question has to be restoration by whom, for whom?" says Janzen.
cn: “当你谈论保护或恢复时，第一个问题必须是：由谁恢复，为谁恢复？”詹曾说。

en: The question can have more than one answer.
cn: 这个问题可以有不止一个答案。

en: Much of the global funding for restoration is dedicated to developing it as a tool to mitigate climate change, notes Brancalion.
cn: 布兰卡利昂指出，全球大部分用于恢复的资金都致力于将其发展为缓解气候变化的工具。

en: "But if you ask a farmer in Brazil if he or she is concerned about climate change, they would say, 'No, I am concerned about water,'" he says.
cn: “但如果你问巴西的一位农民他或她是否担心气候变化，他们会说，‘不，我担心的是水，’”他说。

en: Their interests as stewards of the land need to be better integrated with those who have the money to support restoration.
cn: 他们作为土地管理者的利益，需要与那些有钱支持恢复的人的利益更好地结合起来。

en: That has been the strongest lesson of all for Chazdon.
cn: 这是查兹登得到的最深刻的教训。

en: Restoration is about more than what gets planted in the ground, she says.
cn: 恢复工作不仅仅是关于在地里种下什么，她说。

en: "Yes, it's about forests, but it's really about people. They are the agents of restoration."
cn: “是的，这关乎森林，但它真正关乎的是人。他们才是恢复的推动者。”
